forecast.ingress: There are about 21,500 people working as plumbing installers in Sweden, of whom about 1 percent are women and 99 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: VVS installers (plumbers and related trades) perform mainly hands-on work in real-world environments: installing, repairing, and servicing pipes, heating, water, and sanitation systems. The core tasks require physical presence, manual skill, inspection on site, troubleshooting in varied buildings, and adapting to existing infrastructure. AI can assist somewhat with reading plans, diagnostics, quoting, scheduling, and documentation, but it cannot carry out the central physical installation and repair work. Overall, this is mostly a physical, context-dependent occupation with limited but meaningful digital support potential.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
For VVS installers/pipefitters, there are some observable AI signals, but they remain limited and not yet mainstream in day-to-day field work. Current adoption is mostly indirect: larger contractors and suppliers may use AI-enabled tools for scheduling, customer support, quotation drafting, inventory/logistics, document search, and predictive maintenance on building systems. There are also emerging uses of AI in BIM/CAD workflows, fault diagnosis, and service ticket triage, especially in larger industrial or facility-management settings.

However, there is little evidence of widespread AI use directly in the core hands-on tasks of installation, repair, and maintenance. The occupation is still dominated by physical work, site-specific judgment, safety compliance, and manual troubleshooting, which limits near-term AI penetration. Compared with office-based technical occupations, the number of clear employer signals, job-ad mention patterns, and standard AI tools tailored to this trade is modest.

Overall this fits early adoption: some employers are experimenting and using AI around the workflow, but it is not yet a mainstream or standard toolset for most VVS-montörer.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is primarily hands-on and physical: building wooden structures, formwork, and construction tasks on sites or in mines. AI may assist indirectly with planning, estimating, drawings, scheduling, or safety documentation, but the core work requires real-world manipulation of materials and tools in changing environments. Because the main tasks are embodied and outdoor/on-site rather than screen-based, theoretical AI exposure is low.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
Current AI adoption signals for carpenters/construction woodworkers (SSYK 7111) are weak and mostly indirect. As of early 2026, there is some use of AI-adjacent tools in the broader construction sector, such as generative design, BIM-assisted planning, quantity takeoff support, schedule optimization, and mobile assistants for documentation and safety reporting. However, these are typically used by engineers, project managers, estimators, and larger contractors rather than by frontline carpenters on site.

For the occupation itself, the core work remains manual, spatial, and highly context-dependent, with limited standardization that would make direct AI deployment common. There are occasional pilots or niche uses for computer vision, AR guidance, and automated measurement, but these are not yet widespread enough to count as mainstream adoption. Swedish job ads and employer references more often mention digital tools, BIM, and construction software than explicit AI use for this trade.

Overall, this looks like marginal-to-early adoption: some experimentation and enabling tools exist, but there is little observable evidence of broad, routine AI use among carpenters in Sweden today.

nameSwedish: Odlare av jordbruksväxter, frukt och bär
nameEnglish: Arable crop, fruit and berry growers
description: Bedriver växtodling. Odlar och skördar jordbruksväxter som t.ex. spannmål, olje- och vallväxter, sockerbetor samt potatis och andra köksväxter på friland. Odlar frukt och bär. Underhåller ekonomibyggnader, maskiner och utrustning.
descriptionEnglish: Carry out crop production. Grow and harvest agricultural crops such as cereals, oil and forage crops, sugar beet, potatoes and other field vegetables. Grow fruit and berries. Maintain farm buildings, machinery and equipment.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs in this occupation over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have small to medium opportunities for work, but the labour market may vary depending on the specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work in this occupation will be small to medium. The chances of getting work in the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, qualifications, or vocational credentials.
forecast.ingress: The assessment for growers of horticultural and agricultural crops and mixed livestock farmers is a combined assessment for crop and livestock farmers, mixed farming, growers of agricultural crops, fruit and berries, and horticultural growers. There are about 17,700 people working in this occupation in Sweden, of whom about 19 percent are women and 81 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is primarily hands-on and outdoors: growing, harvesting, and maintaining crops, fruit, berries, buildings, machinery, and equipment. AI can support planning, weather and yield forecasting, pest monitoring, inventory, and machinery diagnostics, but these are auxiliary rather than core tasks. The main work still requires physical presence, real-world judgment, and manual labor in the field, so theoretical AI exposure is low.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption in Swedish plant cultivation, fruit and berry farming appears marginal and mostly experimental rather than mainstream. There are some observable signals around precision agriculture tools, computer vision for crop monitoring, drone-based field analysis, and automated decision support in larger farms or agritech pilots, but these are not yet widely deployed across the occupation. Most day-to-day work in crop growing, harvesting, greenhouse or field operations, and maintenance still relies on conventional machinery, manual labor, and established farm management software rather than generative AI or advanced AI assistants. In Sweden, larger agricultural enterprises and a few technology-forward growers may be testing AI for yield prediction, pest/disease detection, irrigation optimization, and labor planning, but adoption is uneven and limited. Overall, this occupation shows some real but early-stage AI usage signals, so a score in the low-to-mid 2s is appropriate.

Because the occupation depends primarily on immediate physical action and situational judgment in hazardous environments, theoretical AI exposure is low.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For Swedish firefighters, there are some observable AI-related signals, but they remain marginal in day-to-day operational work. Overall, current adoption looks limited to isolated pilots or supporting administrative/analytical functions rather than mainstream operational use.

descriptionEnglish: Are responsible for the care and supervision of inmates in correctional institutions and remand prisons. Monitor order and security. Engage inmates in leisure activities and carry out motivational work.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The occupation combines supervision, safety monitoring, and motivational work in a highly context-dependent custodial setting. AI could support incident documentation, scheduling, risk flagging, and perhaps help with communication or activity planning, but the core work depends on face-to-face presence, situational judgment, de-escalation, and maintaining order and security in real time. In Sweden, statutory responsibility and the need for human accountability in correctional settings further limit replacement potential. Overall, this is mostly interpersonal and hands-on work with some limited cognitive/digital support potential.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For Swedish prison officers (kriminalvårdare), there are some observable AI-related signals, but they are still limited and not mainstream. The role is heavily operational, security-sensitive, and public-sector regulated, which tends to slow adoption. Current use is more likely to involve adjacent administrative tools, incident reporting support, scheduling, translation, or general-purpose internal AI pilots rather than AI embedded directly in custody, supervision, or motivational work. I do not see strong evidence of broad, routine AI use in the day-to-day core tasks of this occupation as of early 2026, so the best fit is marginal-to-early adoption rather than significant deployment.

descriptionEnglish: Patrol and guard premises and areas. Control access to various establishments and monitor order at public events, etc. Respond to alarms and disturbances, contacting the police or fire brigade if needed. Carry out security checks at airports and in ports. Transport cash and other valuables. Protect individuals when required.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that competition for jobs as security guards and security officers will be low over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have good opportunities for work, but the labour market may vary depending on the specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work as security guards and security officers will be good. The chances of getting work in the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, qualifications, or vocational credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 18,200 people working as security guards and security officers in Sweden, of whom about 25 percent are women and 75 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The occupation is primarily physical and real-world operational: patrolling, guarding premises, controlling access, responding to alarms and disturbances, managing public order at events, and transporting valuables. These core tasks depend heavily on presence, situational awareness, and direct human intervention, which AI cannot perform on its own. However, there is some limited cognitive/digital exposure in areas such as monitoring systems, incident documentation, access control, and decision support for alarms or security checks. Overall, AI can assist parts of the workflow but cannot replace the main embodied and interpersonal duties, so exposure is modest.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption signals for security guards and order guards in Sweden appear marginal to early-stage rather than mainstream. There are some observable uses of AI-enabled tools in adjacent workflows, such as camera analytics, access control systems, alarm monitoring, body-worn video review, and incident-reporting software with automated transcription or classification. However, these are usually deployed as part of broader security technology stacks rather than as AI tools directly used by most guards in their daily work. The core job still relies heavily on human presence, judgment, de-escalation, physical patrol, and regulatory responsibility, which limits direct AI substitution or augmentation. In Swedish public-sector and event-security contexts, adoption is likely even slower due to procurement, privacy, and accountability constraints. Overall, the occupation shows some real current AI-enabled tooling, but it is not yet widespread enough to rate above low-to-mid early adoption.

description: Assisterar tandläkare vid undersökning, behandling och operation av patient. Putsar och polerar tänder samt tar röntgenbilder. Planerar behandlingstider, för journaler, tar betalt och gör inköp. Rengör instrument och annan utrustning.
descriptionEnglish: Assist dentists during examinations, treatment and operations. Clean and polish teeth and take X-rays. Schedule treatment appointments, keep records, take payments and make purchases. Clean instruments and other equipment.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be little competition for jobs for dental nurses over the coming year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have good opportunities for work, but the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for dental nurses will be good. Opportunities within the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or occupational credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 10,800 employed dental nurses in Sweden, of whom about 99 percent are women and 1 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Dental nurses perform a mixed role. A meaningful part of the job is cognitive and digital: scheduling treatment times, keeping patient records, billing, ordering supplies, and some documentation around examinations and procedures. These tasks are reasonably exposed to AI support through automation, decision support, and administrative tools. However, a large share of the occupation is hands-on and interpersonal: assisting the dentist during examinations, treatment and surgery, polishing teeth, taking X-rays, cleaning instruments, and working directly with patients in a clinical setting. Because the core work combines clinical support with physical, regulated tasks, the occupation has moderate theoretical exposure rather than high exposure.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For dental assistants in Sweden, there are some observable AI-adjacent signals, but current adoption appears limited and not yet mainstream. In practice, most of the role still centers on chairside assistance, instrument handling, x-ray workflows, patient scheduling, records, billing, and cleaning, which are heavily procedural and regulated. There are signs of digital support tools in dentistry, such as software for appointment management, image handling, documentation, and occasional AI-enabled triage or imaging support in broader dental systems, but these are not clearly widespread as day-to-day AI tools for tandsköterskor specifically. Swedish public and semi-public healthcare settings also tend to adopt new AI more slowly due to procurement, privacy, and clinical governance constraints. Overall, the strongest current signal is early experimentation and selective use of software with AI features, not broad operational adoption.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be very little competition for jobs for dental hygienists in the next year. scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Dental hygienists combine substantial knowledge-based work with direct hands-on clinical care. scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption signals for dental hygienists are still marginal. However, for the core tasks of tandhygienister—oral examination, plaque/tartar removal, periodontal treatment, preventive counseling, sampling, and radiography handling—there is little evidence of widespread, routine AI use in Swedish workplaces as of early 2026. Overall, adoption appears to be early experimentation rather than mainstream deployment.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be little competition for jobs for personal assistants over the coming year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have good opportunities for work, but the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for personal assistants will be good. Opportunities within the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or occupational credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 65,100 employed personal assistants in Sweden, of whom about 71 percent are women and 29 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Personal assistants in this occupation mainly provide hands-on, real-world support to an individual with a disability: helping with school or work attendance, daily living tasks, leisure activities, and personal accompaniment. These duties are highly interpersonal, context-dependent, and often require physical presence and situational judgment that AI cannot replace. AI may assist indirectly with scheduling, documentation, or communication, but it does not change the core task structure much. In the Swedish welfare/care context, statutory and practical limits also reduce substitution. Overall exposure is low.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
For personal assistants (SSYK 5343), there are only weak current AI adoption signals as of early 2026. The work is highly hands-on, relationship-based, and centered on physical support, supervision, and accompaniment, which limits direct AI use in the core tasks. Unlike office, customer service, or clinical occupations, there is little observable evidence of widespread AI tools being deployed in daily work routines for this occupation.

There may be some limited experimentation with generic AI tools for scheduling, shift communication, documentation, or translating messages, especially through employer-side administration, but these are peripheral and not occupationally transformative. In Sweden, public-sector and care-related workplaces also tend to adopt AI more slowly, and I do not see strong signs of broad employer uptake specific to personal assistants.

Overall, this looks like marginal-to-early experimentation rather than meaningful current adoption.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Ambulance attendants perform a mix of hands-on emergency care, patient monitoring, lifting/transport, and rapid interpersonal coordination in unpredictable real-world situations. AI can support parts of the role, such as decision support, documentation, route planning, and triage assistance, but it cannot replace the core physical and situational work of treating and transporting patients. In the Swedish care and emergency context, statutory responsibility, safety requirements, and the need for direct human judgment further limit delegability to AI. scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
For Ambulanssjukvårdare, there are some observable AI-related signals in Sweden, but they are still limited and not yet mainstream in day-to-day work. The most visible adoption is indirect: dispatch and triage systems in emergency care may use decision support, route optimization, speech-to-text, or documentation assistance, but these are typically system-level tools rather than AI used directly by ambulance staff in patient care. In the Swedish public-sector context, deployment tends to be cautious and slower due to safety, liability, procurement, and regulatory requirements.

There are no strong, widespread signals of ambulance crews routinely using AI copilots for clinical assessment or treatment in the field. Most current use appears to be pilots, adjacent administrative tooling, or embedded support in broader emergency-care IT systems. Overall, this fits marginal-to-early adoption rather than significant mainstream use.

description: Marknadsför, säljer och informerar om tjänster och produkter på exempelvis mässor, köpcentra och butiker. Visar kläder för återförsäljare, press och allmänhet på modevisningar. Poserar för exempelvis reklambilder eller tidningsreportage. Poserar för målare, skulptörer och konstskolor.
descriptionEnglish: Market, sell and provide information about services and products at, for example, trade fairs, shopping centres and shops. Model clothing for retailers, the press and the public at fashion shows. Pose for, for example, advertising images or magazine features. Pose for painters, sculptors and art schools.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is mixed in nature. A substantial part of the work involves interpersonal sales, presenting products, promoting brands, answering questions, and adapting to customer reactions in real time, which AI can support but not replace well. At the same time, the role also includes physical presence at fairs, shops, and fashion events, plus posing and live demonstration work that depends on embodiment, appearance, and situational context. AI could assist with marketing copy, scheduling, product information, and lead qualification, but the core task still requires on-site human interaction and presentation. Overall exposure is moderate.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For this occupation, there are only weak current adoption signals. Event sales staff, product demonstrators, models, and similar roles are still primarily physical, interpersonal, and presentation-based, so AI is not yet a mainstream day-to-day tool in Swedish workplaces. There are some observable uses around the edges: employers may use generative AI for drafting promotional copy, event messaging, basic customer follow-up, image selection, scheduling, and social media content. In retail demonstration and event marketing, AI-enabled lead capture, audience targeting, and translation tools are also possible. However, these appear to be limited experiments or support functions rather than widely adopted core workflows. There is no strong evidence of broad, routine AI deployment specific to this occupation in Sweden as of early 2026, so the current adoption level is best characterized as marginal to early experimentation.

descriptionEnglish: Sell and promote goods and services by telephone to businesses or private individuals. Approach and recruit members or donors for organisations.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for telemarketers over the coming year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have small to medium opportunities for work, but the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for telemarketers will be small to medium. Opportunities within the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or occupational credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 5,900 employed telemarketers in Sweden, of whom about 40 percent are women and 60 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is highly exposed because the core work is predominantly screen- and communication-based: identifying prospects, making sales calls, presenting offers, handling objections, and recording outcomes in digital systems. AI can strongly assist or automate lead generation, call scripting, personalized outreach, conversation support, CRM updates, and follow-up messaging. The job still requires human persuasion, empathy, and adaptation to the caller's responses, and in Sweden union agreements and sales regulations may slow full automation. However, the task structure is clearly cognitive and digital, with relatively little physical work, so exposure is high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Telemarketing is one of the occupations with strong current AI adoption signals because it is highly compatible with speech analytics, call transcription, automated lead scoring, predictive dialing, CRM summarization, and AI-assisted scripting/coaching. As of early 2026, these tools are visibly present in commercial contact-center and outbound sales stacks, and many employers can adopt them without changing the core workflow. In Sweden, this is especially plausible in private-sector sales organizations and membership/fundraising call operations, where AI is used to improve conversion rates, agent productivity, and quality monitoring. The occupation is not yet fully AI-native everywhere, but the observable deployment level is clearly beyond pilot-stage and fits significant adoption rather than marginal experimentation.

description: Tar betalt för kunders inköp i affärer, stormarknader och andra försäljningsställen. Säljer biljetter till teatrar, bio, konserter, museer, utställningar m.m. Säljer och växlar valuta.
descriptionEnglish: Take payment for customers' purchases in shops, supermarkets and other points of sale. Sell tickets for theatres, cinemas, concerts, museums, exhibitions, etc. Sell and exchange foreign currency.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is a mixed task bundle with meaningful AI exposure. Core duties such as processing payments, issuing tickets, checking prices, handling simple customer questions, and currency exchange are increasingly digital and can be assisted or partially automated by AI-enabled self-checkout systems, chat-based support, fraud detection, and ticketing platforms. However, the role still involves face-to-face customer interaction, exception handling, manual verification, service recovery, and physical presence at a register or point of sale. In Sweden, adoption may be slowed by labor agreements and workplace practices, so AI is more likely to augment than fully replace the role in the near term.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for cashier/point-of-sale work is moderate and clearly observable in 2026, though not as deep as in professional knowledge work. The main signals are in retail and hospitality systems that increasingly use AI-enabled self-checkout support, fraud/anomaly detection, intelligent cashier assistance, demand forecasting, personalized promotions, and multilingual customer-facing chat or voice assistants that reduce cashier workload. Large Swedish retail chains and ticketing/payment environments have also been adopting AI-powered back-office tools that affect front-line cashier operations indirectly, such as queue optimization, staffing, and transaction monitoring. However, the core cashier task itself is still mostly carried out by conventional POS systems and human interaction, with AI augmenting rather than replacing it. Adoption is therefore meaningful and visible across larger employers, but not yet universal or deeply embedded enough to merit a higher score.

Regional shop managers (1730)

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation combines managerial and sales duties with substantial in-store physical and interpersonal work. AI could meaningfully assist with scheduling, inventory planning, sales analysis, staffing, reporting, and administrative decisions, which raises exposure on the cognitive side. However, the role also requires direct supervision of staff, handling customers, resolving day-to-day store issues, and participating in daily sales operations and café work, which are difficult to automate. In Sweden, the practical adoption of AI would likely be moderated by collective agreements and the need for human accountability on staffing and operations, so the overall exposure is mixed rather than high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for retail store managers and department managers in Sweden appears to be in the early adoption stage. Some Swedish retail employers are also likely using AI-enabled POS/ERP/HR systems that can support daily store management tasks indirectly. However, for this specific occupation, the core work remains highly hands-on and operational: leading staff, managing the store floor, customer interaction, merchandising, and daily sales execution. There is no strong evidence that AI tools are yet broadly embedded in the day-to-day workflow of most store and department managers, beyond being available in larger chains or as part of back-office systems. So the signal is real but not mainstream.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The occupation is mainly hands-on and customer-facing: stocking shelves, unpacking goods, pricing items, and helping shoppers in a store. scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For grocery store sales assistants in Sweden, there are some observable AI-related signals, but they are still limited and not clearly mainstream. Retailers are increasingly using AI in adjacent areas such as demand forecasting, inventory optimization, dynamic pricing, shelf monitoring, and customer-facing chatbots, but these tools usually sit behind the scenes and are more common in larger chains than in day-to-day store-floor work. For the occupation itself, tasks like unloading goods, pricing, stocking shelves, and helping customers are still largely manual, with only modest AI support through store systems and scheduling tools. There is little evidence that AI is broadly used as a core day-to-day work tool for this role across Swedish employers. Overall, this looks like early adoption rather than significant penetration.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Apotekstekniker work is mixed: a meaningful part of the job involves customer guidance, answering questions about self-care products and over-the-counter medicines, and helping with product selection, which AI can support through information retrieval, recommendation, and basic advisory tools. scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption signals for pharmacy technicians in Sweden appear to be early and limited rather than mainstream. However, as of early 2026 there is little strong evidence that AI is widely embedded in the day-to-day work of Swedish apotekstekniker in the way it is for software development or some customer service roles. Most deployment seems to be at the employer/system level rather than as a clearly visible, standard tool used by the occupation itself. Overall this fits early adoption: some tools exist and some employers may be experimenting, but usage is not yet mainstream.

descriptionEnglish: Monitor, operate and maintain the internal and external environment of properties. Check ventilation and heating systems. Carry out minor repairs and provide service to tenants and visitors.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be a small amount of competition for jobs for property caretakers over the coming year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have good opportunities for work, but the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for property caretakers will be good. Opportunities within the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or occupational credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 41,100 employed property caretakers in Sweden, of whom about 15 percent are women and 85 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The occupation is mainly hands-on and site-based, involving inspection, maintenance, minor repairs, and direct service to tenants and visitors. AI can assist with scheduling, fault reporting, documentation, energy monitoring, and prioritizing maintenance tasks, but it cannot replace the core physical work or real-world troubleshooting in buildings. Because the job combines some digital oversight with substantial embodied and interpersonal tasks, exposure is moderate to low rather than high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For Swedish fastighetsskötare there are some observable AI-adoption signals, but they are still limited and not yet mainstream. In practice, a few property management firms and municipal housing companies have begun using AI-enabled tools for work-order triage, tenant chatbots, predictive maintenance, and energy optimization in building systems. Most employers still rely on conventional CAFM/FM systems, sensor dashboards, and rule-based automation rather than strongly AI-driven workflows. Compared with white-collar roles, there is much less evidence of broad, routine AI use in job ads or day-to-day operations. So the current adoption level is best described as early adoption with a few pilots and selective deployments.

description: Klipper, behandlar och vårdar hår med hjälp av t. ex. sax, klippmaskin och kniv. Utför kemiska behandlingar som permanent, färgning och blekning. Gör håruppsättningar, läggningar och hårförlängningar. Tillverkar och provar ut peruker och tupéer.
descriptionEnglish: Cut, treat and care for hair using, for example, scissors, clippers and razors. Carry out chemical treatments such as perms, colouring and bleaching. Create hair arrangements, setting and hair extensions. Make and fit wigs and toupees.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for hairdressers over the coming year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have small to medium opportunities for work, but the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for hairdressers will be good. Opportunities within the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or occupational credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 13,500 employed hairdressers in Sweden, of whom about 85 percent are women and 15 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Hairdressers perform mainly hands-on, real-world service work: cutting, treating, styling, coloring, and fitting hairpieces. These tasks depend heavily on physical dexterity, direct observation, customer interaction, and adapting in real time to each client’s hair, preferences, and condition. AI can assist with booking, marketing, style inspiration, and possibly consultation support, but it cannot perform the core embodied service work. Therefore the occupation has low theoretical AI exposure.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption signals for hairdressers are weak. In Sweden, there are some consumer-facing beauty apps and a few salon software tools that may include AI features such as style previews, booking automation, or marketing assistance, but these are not yet standard tools in day-to-day salon work. Core tasks like cutting, coloring, styling, and hair treatment remain highly manual and skill-based, with little evidence of widespread AI deployment in salons. As of early 2026, adoption appears mostly marginal or experimental rather than mainstream.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Tour guides and travel leaders have a mixed task profile. A substantial part of the job is knowledge-based and communication-heavy: preparing tour content, giving factual explanations about sights, answering routine questions, managing schedules, and representing a travel company. These elements are quite AI-exposable, especially through itinerary planning, translated scripts, automated customer information, and real-time assistance. However, the role also depends heavily on face-to-face interaction, group management, situational judgment, adapting to weather or on-site disruptions, and maintaining a personal presence that AI cannot fully replace. In Sweden, collective agreements and labor norms may also slow practical adoption. Overall, this is a moderate exposure occupation.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are observable but still limited AI adoption signals for guides and tour leaders in Sweden as of early 2026. Some travel companies, destination organizations, and booking platforms use AI for multilingual content generation, itinerary drafting, FAQ/chat support, and personalized trip recommendations, which can support this occupation indirectly. Individual guides may also use general-purpose AI tools for route planning, translation, background research, and creating scripts or tour descriptions. However, the core on-site parts of the job—live guiding, group handling, local interaction, and situational adaptation—remain strongly human and are not yet widely automated or AI-supported in a standardized way. Overall this looks like early adoption rather than mainstream deployment.

Biträder vid informationssökning. Sköter bild- och ljudanläggningar. Biträder vid registrering, skötsel och utställning av museiföremål.
descriptionEnglish: List, sort and retrieve archive documents. Assist with cataloguing and registering books and other published material. Arrange and lend books, periodicals, etc. Assist with information searches. Operate image and sound equipment. Assist with the registration, care and exhibition of museum objects.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for library and archives assistants over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have small to medium opportunities for work, but the labour market may vary depending on the specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the employment prospects for library and archives assistants will be small to medium. Job opportunities in the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are approximately 3,300 employed library and archives assistants in Sweden, of whom about 73 percent are women and 27 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely knowledge- and information-based: listing, sorting, retrieving, cataloguing, registering, and assisting with information searches are all tasks where AI can meaningfully support or automate parts of the workflow. The role also involves managing library materials and museum records, which are digital/administrative in nature and therefore fairly exposed to document-processing, search, recommendation, and metadata tools. However, there are still some physical and practical components, such as handling books, archive materials, equipment, and museum objects, plus some service-oriented interaction with users. Overall, the job is mostly cognitive and screen-based, but not fully digital, so the exposure is high rather than extreme.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for archive and library assistants in Sweden appears to be in the early-adoption range rather than mainstream. There are observable signals of AI use in adjacent tasks such as OCR and document digitization, metadata extraction, searchable archives, chatbots for library/customer inquiries, and basic recommendation/search tools in cultural institutions. Some Swedish libraries, museums, and municipal archives likely pilot or selectively use AI-assisted cataloging, transcription, and information retrieval. However, the occupation is still heavily centered on physical handling, controlled cataloging, records management, and user service in settings where public-sector procurement, privacy, and quality requirements slow deployment. Compared with more digitized office occupations, there is no strong evidence that AI tools are broadly embedded in daily workflows across most employers. So the signal is real but limited, supporting a mid-4s score.

description:
Samordnar och övervakar person- och godstrafik vid t.ex. lokaltrafikföretag, åkerier, godsterminal, grosshandel och industriföretag. Utarbetar och justerar transportscheman. Ansvarar för tågtrafik inom ett geografiskt område samt dirigerar tågtrafik enligt säkerhetsbestämmelser och tidtabell. Lägger växlar och ställer signaler med hjälp av ställverk. Övervakar trafik på flygplatser samt registrerar starter och landningar. Beräknar ankomsttider och svarar för behandling av gods vid flygterminal. Anvisar kajplats åt fartyg och tillhandahåller utrustning.
 
Hit hör inte
Logistiker (2141)
Speditör (3331)
Trafikinformatör (4222)
descriptionEnglish:
Co-ordinate and oversee passenger and freight traffic, for example in local transport companies, haulage firms, freight terminals, wholesale companies and industrial firms. Draw up and adjust transport schedules. Responsible for train traffic within a geographical area and direct train movements in accordance with safety regulations and timetables. Set points and signals using a signal box. Monitor traffic at airports and record take-offs and landings. Calculate arrival times and handle freight at an air terminal. Allocate berths to ships and provide equipment.

Includes not
Logistician (2141)
Freight forwarder (3331)
Traffic information officer (4222)

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be little competition for jobs for transport managers and transport coordinators over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have good opportunities for work, but the labour market may vary depending on the specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the employment prospects for transport managers and transport coordinators will be good. Job opportunities in the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are approximately 8,900 employed transport managers and transport coordinators in Sweden, of whom about 30 percent are women and 70 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely coordination- and information-driven: planning transport schedules, monitoring traffic, calculating arrivals, directing train movements, and recording departures/arrivals are all tasks that AI systems can strongly support through optimization, prediction, and decision support. Much of the work is screen-based and relies on data, rules, and real-time status updates rather than manual labor. However, the role also includes operational oversight in real-world environments (rail, airport, port), safety-critical judgment, and coordination with people and physical infrastructure, which limits full automation. In Sweden, safety regulations, union agreements, and accountability requirements would also slow replacement. Overall, the occupation has high but not maximal AI exposure.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption signals for transport coordinators/dispatchers are visible but still mostly early-stage. In Sweden, employers in transport, logistics, airport operations, and rail traffic increasingly use scheduling, route optimization, demand forecasting, and dispatch support systems that may include AI or machine-learning components. However, for this SSYK group, most core work is still operational, safety-bound, and rule-based: coordinating traffic, adjusting timetables, monitoring movements, handling signals, and managing arrivals/departures. Those tasks are commonly supported by established transport management software and automation rather than broadly deployed generative AI. Observable AI use is more likely in adjacent planning functions, exception handling, chat/assistant tools, and predictive ETA or disruption management than in the main dispatcher role itself. Overall this suggests early adoption with some employer experimentation, but not yet mainstream AI dependence.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation combines substantial cognitive, screen-based work with significant physical warehouse tasks. Core duties such as registering and controlling material flows, planning deliveries, checking stock levels, and documenting goods are information-intensive and could be meaningfully supported by AI systems for forecasting, inventory optimization, and administrative control. However, a large part of the job also involves receiving goods, picking, packing, loading, and operating equipment like forklifts, which require physical presence and real-world handling. Overall, the role is moderately exposed to AI because the planning, tracking, and control components are digitalizable, but the hands-on logistics work limits full automation.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption signals for warehouse and terminal workers in Sweden appear to be in the early-adoption range rather than mainstream. In 2026, there are observable tools in logistics and warehouse environments such as AI-supported warehouse management systems, demand forecasting, route/load optimization, computer-vision based inventory checks, and automated picking/packaging assistance, but these are typically deployed by employers as part of broader automation projects rather than as direct AI tools used by most workers. The occupation still relies heavily on physical handling, scanning, checking, loading, and operational coordination, so day-to-day AI usage is limited and uneven across employers. Larger logistics firms and advanced warehouses are more likely to use AI-enabled planning and inventory systems, while smaller terminals and traditional warehouse operations often use standard digital systems with little explicit AI. Overall, this points to some real deployment signals, but not yet broad or routine AI use across the occupation.

descriptionEnglish: Responsible for, co-ordinate and lead day-to-day operations in a warehouse or terminal.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation combines substantial cognitive and coordination work with hands-on operational leadership. The supervisor plans daily operations, coordinates staff and flows, handles scheduling, monitoring, reporting, and problem-solving, all of which are tasks AI systems can support through forecasting, optimization, documentation, and decision support. However, the role also requires on-site presence, direct oversight of warehouse/terminal operations, interpersonal coordination with workers and other managers, and rapid response to real-world disruptions that are difficult to automate. scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for warehouse and terminal supervisors in Sweden appears to be in early adoption rather than mainstream use. description: Intervjuar personer och hanterar svaren för marknads-, samhälls- och opinionsundersökningar.
descriptionEnglish: Interview people and process responses for market, social and opinion surveys.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely cognitive and screen-based: it involves conducting interviews, recording responses, and managing survey data for market, social, and opinion research. AI can already assist substantially with questionnaire design, automated interviewing tools, transcription, coding of open-ended responses, quality checks, and preliminary analysis. However, the job still includes live interpersonal interaction, adapting to respondents, building rapport, and handling sensitive or nuanced situations, which limits full automation. In Sweden, adoption may be moderated by collective agreements and workplace routines, but the core task structure is still highly exposed to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
Current AI adoption for market researchers and interviewers in Sweden appears to be in early adoption rather than mainstream deployment. There are observable signals of AI use in adjacent tasks such as survey design assistance, questionnaire drafting, transcription, coding of open-ended responses, sentiment analysis, and summarization of interview notes. Many market research and analytics teams can access these tools through common platforms, and some employers are likely experimenting with LLMs and automated text analysis.

However, the core occupation still relies heavily on human-led interviewing, respondent handling, survey fieldwork, and quality control, where AI is not yet a standard frontline tool. Compared with occupations where AI is deeply embedded in daily workflows, current adoption here looks uneven and limited to selected tasks or pilot use rather than broad, routine deployment across employers. Public-sector and research-oriented survey work in Sweden also tends to adopt new tools more slowly.

Overall, this fits a score of 4: some employers are experimenting and practical tools exist, but AI is not yet mainstream for the occupation as a whole.

descriptionEnglish: Receive visitors. Provide information and direct people to a place, room or person. Manage entry and exit.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for office receptionists over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have small to medium opportunities for work, but the labour market may vary depending on the specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the employment prospects for office receptionists will be small to medium. Job opportunities in the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are approximately 12,100 employed office receptionists in Sweden, of whom about 86 percent are women and 14 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Kontorsreceptionister perform a mixed set of tasks. A substantial part of the role is cognitive and digital: receiving visitors, giving information, directing people to the right place or person, and handling access control. These tasks can be partially supported by AI through chatbots, visitor management systems, digital check-in, and automated routing. However, the job also relies heavily on real-time interpersonal interaction, judgment, and handling physical presence at the reception desk or entrance. Because the core work is not purely screen-based and includes direct human contact and on-site coordination, the occupation has moderate AI exposure rather than high exposure.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption signals for office receptionists in Sweden are still early rather than mainstream. There are observable tools such as AI phone/chat assistants, visitor-management systems with automated check-in, and workplace front-desk software that can route inquiries or answer basic questions. Some employers, especially larger private organizations, are experimenting with these features to reduce routine reception workload. However, the occupation still relies heavily on human presence for greeting visitors, identity checks, access control, and nuanced directing of people and deliveries. In the Swedish public sector and many smaller workplaces, deployment appears limited and uneven. Overall this fits early adoption with some experimentation, but not broad, routine AI use yet.

description: Tar emot, checkar in och checkar ut gäster på hotell, konferensanläggning, vandrarhem eller campingplats m.m. Informerar om bl.a. anläggningens service samt sevärdheter och nöjesevenemang. Gör i ordning konferenslokaler och ansvarar för att den tekniska utrustningen fungerar och att förtäring serveras enligt beställningen.
descriptionEnglish: Receive, check in and check out guests at hotels, conference centres, hostels or campsites, etc. Provide information about, for example, the venue’s services, sights and entertainment events. Prepare conference rooms and ensure that technical equipment works and that refreshments are served as ordered.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for hotel receptionists over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have small to medium opportunities for work, but the labour market may vary depending on the specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the employment prospects for hotel receptionists will be small to medium. Job opportunities in the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are approximately 8,700 employed hotel receptionists in Sweden, of whom about 73 percent are women and 27 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Hotel receptionists perform a mix of digital and interpersonal tasks. A substantial part of the job is screen-based and information-driven: checking guests in and out, handling bookings, answering questions, providing local information, and coordinating services and conference arrangements. These tasks are fairly exposed to AI through chatbots, reservation systems, automated check-in, and decision support. However, the role also requires face-to-face customer service, handling special requests, resolving issues in real time, and overseeing practical setup of conference rooms and equipment, which limits full automation. Overall, this is a mixed occupation with moderate AI exposure.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are clear but still limited current AI adoption signals for hotel reception and related front-desk work in Sweden. Many hotels and conference venues now use AI-enabled tools for chatbots/virtual concierge services, automated guest messaging, booking support, translation, and review-response drafting, and these are visible in commercial products and some employer deployments. However, for the core occupation itself—check-in/check-out, guest service, local information, conference room setup, and coordination of equipment/food—AI is still mostly supplementary rather than deeply embedded in daily workflows. The role remains heavily human-facing and operational, so adoption is best characterized as early-stage experimentation and selective use rather than mainstream, hence a 4.

description: Tar emot och kopplar telefonsamtal. Ger upplysningar, tar emot meddelanden och lämnar besked. Sköter växel samt hänvisnings- och personsökningssystem.
descriptionEnglish: Answer and connect telephone calls. Give information, take messages and pass on messages. Operate switchboards and paging/person-finder systems.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely cognitive and screen-based: answering incoming calls, routing them, providing information, taking messages, and operating switchboard/paging systems. These tasks are highly structured, information-driven, and well suited to AI-supported voice bots, chat/voice triage, automated call routing, and knowledge-base lookup. The role still involves real-time interpersonal communication and occasional judgment in handling exceptions, escalation, or sensitive callers, which reduces it from the very highest exposure range. In Sweden, adoption may be slowed by collective agreements and service-quality requirements, but the core task structure is still highly exposed to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are strong current adoption signals for telephone operator-type work, even if the occupation name is somewhat legacy. In practice, the core tasks of routing calls, answering routine questions, taking messages, and managing switchboard/call-handling workflows are now heavily supported by AI-driven contact-center tools: speech-to-text, intent classification, intelligent call routing, IVR/voice bots, agent-assist, and automated message summaries. These tools are widely available and actively deployed in Swedish customer service, reception, and shared-service environments, especially in larger private employers and telecom/service providers. Adoption is not uniform across all workplaces because many organizations still rely on humans for complex or sensitive calls, and public-sector adoption is slower, but the observable signal is clearly beyond early experimentation. For that reason, currentAdoption is best rated as 7.

description: Tar emot, samordnar och förmedlar larm om inträffade eller befarade olyckor i luften, på land och till sjöss. Leder arbetet med räddningsinsatser. Tar emot inbrottslarm, överfallslarm, driftlarm och trygghetslarm på larmcentral eller ledningscentral för bevakningsföretag.
descriptionEnglish: Receive, co-ordinate and relay alerts about actual or suspected accidents in the air, on land and at sea. Direct rescue operations. Receive burglary alarms, assault alarms, fault alarms and personal safety alarms at an alarm centre or control centre for security companies.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is heavily centered on receiving, interpreting, coordinating, and forwarding information through alarm and communications systems. The core work is screen- and knowledge-based: assessing incident reports, prioritizing alarms, documenting events, and directing rescue or security responses. These are tasks where AI could strongly assist with triage, pattern recognition, decision support, automated classification, and drafting of logs or instructions. However, the role also includes high-stakes human judgment, real-time coordination, and responsibility during emergencies, which limit full automation. There is little physical labor, but some interpersonal communication and statutory accountability remain important, keeping the score below the very highest range.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are clear but still uneven current adoption signals for emergency- and alarm-center work in Sweden. AI is being used or piloted for call triage, speech-to-text/transcription, call summarization, incident classification, dispatch support, and alarm filtering in control-room environments, especially in larger private security firms and some public-safety adjacent operations. However, for SOS operators specifically, the core work remains highly human-in-the-loop because correctness, accountability, multilingual communication, situational judgment, and coordination with rescue services are safety-critical. In the Swedish public sector, adoption is slower and more cautious, so the technology is present but not yet broadly transformed into routine, mainstream practice across employers. This fits early adoption rather than widespread mature deployment.

description: Utför annat bevaknings- och säkerhetsarbete än inom 5411-5414. Exempel på arbetsuppgifter: Bevakar stränder och simbassänger för att förhindra olyckor och rädda individer från att drunkna. Bistår personer i nöd till fjälls. Kontrollerar att parkeringsbestämmelser följs.
descriptionEnglish: Carry out other guarding and security work than in 5411–5414. Examples of duties: monitor beaches and swimming pools to prevent accidents and rescue people from drowning. Assist people in distress in mountainous areas. Check that parking regulations are observed.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is mainly hands-on and situational, involving physical presence in outdoor or public environments such as beaches, pools, parking areas, and mountain terrain. The core work is monitoring, preventing accidents, responding to emergencies, and interacting directly with the public or people in distress. AI can provide some support through scheduling, incident reporting, camera analytics, or risk alerts, but it cannot replace the essential embodied, real-time, and safety-critical aspects of the job. Overall, the theoretical AI exposure is low.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption signals for this occupation appear marginal. The work is highly physical, situational, and safety-critical (e.g., lifeguarding, mountain rescue assistance, parking enforcement patrols), which limits direct use of generative AI in core tasks. As of early 2026, there are some observable AI-enabled tools in adjacent areas such as camera/video analytics, incident detection, scheduling, route planning, and multilingual support for emergency communication, but these are not yet widely embedded across employers in this SSYK group. In Sweden, adoption is likely stronger in larger municipal or private security operations, yet the occupation overall shows more experimentation than mainstream deployment. Public-sector and field-based roles also tend to lag in visible AI use. Overall, the current adoption level is low to early-stage rather than established.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for customer service staff over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have small to medium opportunities for work, but the labour market may vary depending on the specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the employment prospects for customer service staff will be small to medium. forecast.ingress: There are approximately 30,500 employed customer service staff in Sweden, of whom about 59 percent are women and 41 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Customer service personnel primarily handle information, advice, policy explanations, and responses to customer questions. These are largely screen- and knowledge-based tasks that AI systems can already support well through chatbots, call summarization, FAQ retrieval, case classification, and draft responses. The role still includes interpersonal judgment, escalation handling, and nuanced service recovery, which reduce full automation, but the core task structure is highly exposed to AI assistance.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Customer service staff are one of the clearest current beneficiaries of AI in Swedish workplaces. Many employers in retail, telecom, banking, insurance, transport, and utilities have either deployed or are actively piloting these tools, especially for email, chat, and ticket triage. The function described here — answering questions and providing advice about products, services, or policies — maps directly to these systems. Adoption is still uneven, and public-sector customer service lags, but this is no longer just experimentation; it is significant and broadly visible in the market.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is strongly information- and transaction-oriented: it involves advising customers about travel options, destinations, accommodation, timetables, fares, and routes; arranging itineraries; booking tickets and hotel rooms; and handling passenger service at terminals. These are mostly screen-based, knowledge-heavy tasks that AI systems can already assist with through search, recommendation, customer chat, scheduling, and booking automation. However, the role also includes real-time customer interaction, handling exceptions, verifying travel documents, registering baggage, and service work in airports, ferry terminals, and bus stations, which require human presence and context-sensitive judgment. Overall, the occupation has relatively high theoretical AI exposure, but not near-total automation because of its interpersonal and operational components.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: This occupation shows clear early-to-mid stage AI adoption signals, but not yet broad mainstream deployment. On the travel sales / passenger service side, many employers now use AI-enabled chatbots, virtual assistants, and agent-assist tools for answering travel questions, rebooking, itinerary suggestions, and multilingual customer support. Airline, ferry, and transport customer service operations increasingly rely on automated self-service, speech/text classification, and knowledge-search copilots to handle repetitive inquiries. That said, core tasks in this SSYK group still involve reservation handling, ticketing, baggage registration, passenger-facing service, and exception management, which are often integrated into legacy systems rather than fully AI-driven. In Sweden, adoption is likely more visible in larger travel firms, airports, and transport operators than in smaller agencies or public-facing terminal services. Overall, current adoption is real and observable, but not yet pervasive enough for a higher score.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Ship officers spend a meaningful share of their work on cognitive and digital tasks such as navigation, route planning, monitoring instruments, communications, documentation, and compliance with safety procedures. These are areas where AI can provide decision support and automation assistance. However, the occupation also requires direct control of a vessel, real-time judgment in changing weather and traffic conditions, leadership on deck and bridge, and legal responsibility that cannot be delegated to AI. Because the job is a mixed combination of knowledge work and hands-on operational command, its theoretical AI exposure is moderate rather than high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are only limited observable signals of meaningful AI use for ship officers in current Swedish practice. Navigation and bridge operations already rely heavily on established digital systems such as ECDIS, AIS, radar, weather routing, and voyage planning software, but these are generally rule-based decision-support tools rather than widely deployed AI. As of early 2026, there are some emerging vendor offerings for predictive maintenance, route optimization, fuel-efficiency optimization, and anomaly detection in maritime operations, and larger shipping companies may pilot these on selected vessels. However, for the core duties of navigating and commanding a vessel, AI is not yet broadly embedded in everyday workflows, and regulatory, safety, and liability constraints slow adoption. Overall this looks like early experimentation with some adjacent operational AI, not mainstream AI-assisted occupational practice.

description: Utför assisterande juridiskt arbete. Förbereder, granskar och bearbetar juridiska handlingar. Kallar parter till förhandlingar. Skickar förelägganden. Protokollför rättegångar och förhandlingar. Upprättar förteckningar över dödsbons tillgångar och skulder samt bistår vid arvskifte.
descriptionEnglish: Carry out legal support work. Prepare, review and process legal documents. Summon parties to hearings. Issue notices. Keep records of trials and hearings. Draw up inventories of deceased estates’ assets and liabilities and assist with division of estates.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is predominantly administrative and knowledge-based: preparing, reviewing, and processing legal documents, issuing notices, keeping court records, and compiling estate inventories are all text-heavy tasks that AI systems can materially assist with. The work relies on structured information, standardized forms, and document handling, which are areas where AI has relatively high theoretical exposure. However, there are still important human and legal judgment components, including ensuring procedural correctness, interacting with parties, and handling case-specific context within Swedish legal rules. Overall, the role is highly exposed to AI, though not fully automatable due to statutory responsibilities and the need for accountability.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current adoption appears to be in the early-stage range. For Swedish court and legal administrative secretaries, there are observable AI signals such as generic drafting tools, document summarization, transcription support, and workflow automation in legal-administrative environments. However, the occupation is heavily tied to formal procedure, confidentiality, accuracy, and public-sector constraints, which slows broad deployment in courts and other legal institutions. In practice, AI is more likely used for limited drafting, review support, meeting notes, and search than for core procedural tasks. That means the occupation shows real but not yet mainstream adoption as of early 2026.

description: Överlämnar delgivningshandlingar. Inkasserar utestående fordringar samt undersöker tillgångar. Gör upp avbetalningsplaner och medverkar i förlikningar. Upprättar ansökningshandlingar till Kronofogden samt ansöker om verkställighet såsom utmätningar och avhysningar. Lånar ut pengar mot pant. Värderar panter eller annan säkerhet. Informerar om lånevillkor och beräknar ränta.
descriptionEnglish: Serve legal notices. Collect outstanding debts and investigate assets. Agree repayment plans and take part in settlements. Prepare applications to the Swedish Enforcement Authority and apply for enforcement measures such as attachment of earnings and eviction. Lend money against pledged items. Value pledges or other security. Provide information on loan terms and calculate interest.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is mostly cognitive and document-based: collecting debts, checking assets, arranging repayment plans, preparing applications to the Enforcement Authority, requesting seizures/evictions, and calculating interest or assessing collateral. These are tasks where AI can strongly assist with document drafting, case triage, data retrieval, and decision support. However, there are still meaningful interpersonal and procedural elements such as serving documents, negotiating settlements, assessing real-world collateral, and handling legally sensitive enforcement actions, which limit full automation. Overall, the job is highly exposed to AI, but not completely screen-bound because it combines administrative/legal work with human judgment and some face-to-face interaction.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are observable AI adoption signals for debt collection, credit assessment, and consumer finance workflows, but they are still mostly early-stage rather than deeply embedded for this occupation. In Sweden, many employers in finance and collections are using or testing AI for call transcription, customer communication drafts, prioritization of cases, document handling, fraud/risk detection, and basic decision support. However, the core tasks in this SSYK group—service of documents, enforcement applications to the Swedish Enforcement Authority, asset investigation, settlement negotiation, and pawn valuation—remain heavily regulated, case-specific, and human-led. Public-sector and quasi-legal enforcement processes also tend to adopt AI more slowly. For pawn lending, AI is more likely to appear in back-office workflow support and risk scoring than in the actual valuation or loan decision. Overall, the current signal is real but not mainstream, so this fits early adoption.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation combines real-time interpersonal service at the gaming table with knowledge-based and numerical tasks. The core responsibilities include running casino games, announcing numbers during bingo, selling chips, and calculating probabilities/statistics for odds setting. description: Utför annat kontorsarbete än inom 4111-4117 Exempel på arbetsuppgifter: Sköter löpande ärenden inom administration. Kontrollerar, kompletterar, registrerar, besvarar förfrågningar m.m. Skriver protokoll, brev, tabeller och andra dokument. Gör uträkningar och avstämningar.
descriptionEnglish: Carry out other office work than in 4111–4117. Examples of duties: handle ongoing administrative matters. Check, complete and register information, answer enquiries, etc. Write minutes, letters, tables and other documents. Carry out calculations and reconciliations.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for office assistants and secretaries over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have small to medium opportunities for work, but the labour market may vary depending on the specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the employment prospects for office assistants and secretaries will be small to medium. Job opportunities in the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are approximately 67,300 employed office assistants and secretaries in Sweden, of whom about 79 percent are women and 21 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely office-based and centered on administrative, document, and information-processing tasks: handling ongoing administrative matters, checking and completing records, registering information, answering inquiries, writing minutes and letters, and performing calculations and reconciliations. These are all highly compatible with AI support for drafting, summarizing, data entry assistance, routine correspondence, and basic validation. The role is not fully automated to a 9–10 because it still involves some human judgment, coordination, and interaction with internal/external contacts, and Swedish workplace rules and collective agreements may slow implementation. Still, the core task structure is predominantly digital and knowledge-based, so exposure is high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
This occupation shows clear early-to-mid adoption signals, but not yet broad mainstream AI deployment. The work is highly text- and process-heavy: drafting letters, minutes, registrations, routine correspondence, summaries, and reconciliations. Those tasks are well suited to generative AI, document automation, and workflow copilots, so many Swedish employers likely have access to relevant tools in office suites and case-management systems. There are also observable market signals in Sweden such as frequent AI mentions in job ads and increasing use of AI-assisted administrative features in common workplace software.

However, for this specific occupation, adoption is still uneven. Much of the role sits in municipal, regional, and other public-sector environments where procurement, data governance, and compliance slow deployment. In practice, many workers may use AI indirectly through features embedded in Microsoft 365, translation, transcription, or document drafting rather than through dedicated, task-specific AI systems. That means the occupation has tangible current usage signals, but not the high, mature adoption seen in software development or customer service. A score of 5 best fits: early adoption with some employers experimenting and a growing installed base, but not yet universal or deeply transformed.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be very little competition for jobs for medical secretaries and healthcare administrators over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have very good opportunities for work, but the labour market may vary depending on the specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the employment prospects for medical secretaries and healthcare administrators will be very good. Job opportunities in the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are approximately 18,800 employed medical secretaries and healthcare administrators in Sweden, of whom about 98 percent are women and 2 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely administrative and text-based: writing and managing patient records, handling referrals, and processing information from clinicians. Those core tasks are highly susceptible to AI support through speech-to-text, document drafting, information extraction, coding/structuring of records, and workflow automation. There are still important human and legal constraints in Swedish healthcare, including responsibility for correct documentation, confidentiality, and patient interaction at reception, which reduce full automation. Overall, the job is mostly cognitive and digital with some interpersonal components, so exposure is high but not near-total.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are clear current signals of AI use in this occupation, but they still look like early-stage adoption rather than mainstream deployment. In Swedish healthcare administration, tools for speech-to-text, automatic transcription, document drafting, triage support, and inbox/routing assistance are increasingly available and discussed, especially for medical documentation and patient communication. However, the public-sector nature of much of the work, strict privacy and patient-data requirements, and the need for accuracy and accountability slow broad rollout. Observable adoption appears to be uneven across employers: some clinics and regions are piloting or selectively using AI-assisted documentation and administrative support, while many medical secretaries still work largely with conventional systems and human review. That places the occupation in the early adoption range rather than the significant-adoption range.
