forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a 2-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that opportunities for work as buyers and procurement officers will be strong. forecast.ingress: There are about 19,100 employed buyers and procurement officers in Sweden, of whom around 44 percent are women and 56 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely knowledge-based and screen-based. The core tasks include planning procurement needs, collecting and comparing bids, building calculations, negotiating prices and delivery terms, managing supplier contacts, and drafting contracts and agreements. These are all activities where AI can strongly assist with search, summarization, comparison, drafting, and decision support. However, the job still involves human negotiation, relationship management, accountability, and judgment in procurement decisions, which limits full automation. In a Swedish context, collective agreements and organizational procurement rules may also slow adoption, but the task structure remains highly exposed to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for buyers/procurement officers in Sweden appears to be in early-stage experimentation rather than broad, routine use. Observable signals include the availability of AI features in procurement suites for spend analysis, supplier risk screening, contract review, and drafting RFQs/RFPs, and some employers are beginning to mention AI-supported sourcing, analysis, and contract work in job ads. However, there is not yet strong evidence that AI is standard practice across most procurement functions, especially for end-to-end negotiation, vendor management, and formal contracting. In the Swedish public sector, procurement work is often more conservative and process-bound, which slows deployment. Overall this points to some adoption, but not yet mainstream.

descriptionEnglish: Sells products and services to retailers, wholesalers and companies through field sales or from an office. Is responsible for a product or district. Provides information on products and services, develops customer-specific solutions and takes orders. Maintains customer records and produces sales statistics.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs as business-to-business salespeople over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have limited to moderate opportunities for work, but the labour market may vary depending on specialisation within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a 2-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that opportunities for work as business-to-business salespeople will be strong. Your chances of getting a job in this occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, authorisations, or vocational credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 84,900 employed business-to-business salespeople in Sweden, of whom around 26 percent are women and 74 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely knowledge- and screen-based: identifying customer needs, preparing tailored solutions, informing clients about products and services, handling orders, maintaining customer records, and producing sales statistics. These are tasks that AI can strongly assist with through lead scoring, CRM automation, drafting offers, product recommendations, and analytics. There is still an important human component in relationship building, negotiation, trust creation, and field-based customer contact, which keeps it from the very top exposure range. In Sweden, adoption may be moderated by union agreements and existing sales workflows, but the core task structure is still highly exposed to AI support.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: B2B sales is a current, observable area of AI adoption in Sweden, mainly through AI-assisted CRM use, email/proposal drafting, call summarization, lead scoring, and sales analytics. For corporate salespeople, these tools are already embedded in many mainstream SaaS platforms (CRM, marketing automation, contact-center and meeting tools), and Swedish employers increasingly mention AI-supported sales and customer interaction in job ads. That said, adoption is still uneven: many firms use AI as a productivity layer rather than a fully transformed sales process, and frontline enterprise sales workflows still depend heavily on human relationship management, negotiation, and account knowledge. Overall this fits significant current adoption, but not near-universal saturation.

descriptionEnglish: Markets and sells insurance and analyses and advises on insurance needs. Investigates risks in order to assess premiums and policy terms. Buys and sells parts of insurance portfolios to other insurance companies.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs as insurance salespeople and insurance advisers over the next year. scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely cognitive and screen-based. Core tasks include marketing and selling insurance, analyzing customer insurance needs, assessing risk, setting premiums and policy terms, and broking insurance shares between companies. These activities rely heavily on information processing, document review, calculations, comparison of policies, and decision support, all of which are highly amenable to AI assistance. There is some interpersonal work in advising clients and negotiating with insurers, which reduces exposure slightly, but the job remains predominantly knowledge-intensive and digital rather than physical or hands-on.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for insurance sales and advisory roles in Sweden appears to be in the early-adoption stage. However, for the core advisory and sales function, adoption is not yet broadly mainstream across employers, and many tasks still rely on human judgment, regulatory compliance, and relationship-based selling. In Sweden specifically, large insurance companies and banks are more likely to experiment with or deploy AI-enabled CRM, knowledge search, and drafting tools, while smaller firms and more regulated advice-heavy settings lag behind. Overall this suggests meaningful but not yet pervasive deployment.

scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
Current adoption appears moderate to significant, but not yet universal. For claims adjusters and valuers, there are clear observable AI signals in 2025–early 2026: insurers and loss-adjusting firms are using AI for document intake, claims triage, invoice and photo analysis, fraud/anomaly detection, and draft correspondence. Computer vision tools help assess vehicle and property damage from images, while LLM-based assistants are increasingly used to summarize policies, claims files, and customer communications.

In Sweden, this occupation sits in a sector where digitalization is already advanced and many employers have practical reasons to deploy AI to reduce handling times and standardize decisions. However, human judgment remains central for liability assessment, negotiation, site inspections, and final compensation decisions, so deployment is typically decision-support rather than full automation. Public-sector or highly regulated claims contexts also slow rollout.

Overall, the occupation shows strong current adoption signals in employer tools and workflows, but not the kind of near-complete AI saturation seen in software or some customer service roles.

description: Sammanställer, rapporterar, utvärderar och redovisar företags eller myndigheters ekonomiska resultat. Sammanställer budgetunderlag. Bistår revisor med att granska företags och myndigheters redovisning och förvaltning.
descriptionEnglish: Compiles, reports on, evaluates and accounts for the financial results of companies or public authorities. Prepares budget documentation. Assists auditors in examining the accounts and administration of companies and public authorities.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for accounting economists in the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have limited to moderate opportunities for work, although the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the profession.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: In a two-year perspective, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for accounting economists will be limited to moderate. The chances of finding work in the profession increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 28,300 working accounting economists in Sweden, of whom about 78 percent are women and 22 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is highly screen- and knowledge-based. Core tasks include compiling financial statements, preparing budgets, reporting results, and supporting audits—work that is largely data-driven, rule-based, and document intensive. AI can strongly assist with bookkeeping analysis, anomaly detection, drafting reports, reconciliation, and compliance checks. There are some human judgment and regulatory/accounting-standard elements, but the main task structure is very exposed to AI automation and augmentation.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current adoption signals for accounting economists in Sweden are clearly present but not yet universal. In practice, many employers now use AI-enabled features in finance systems, ERP platforms, invoice processing, expense handling, document extraction, and basic reporting automation. LLM-based assistance is also increasingly used for drafting internal explanations, reconciling data, and summarizing financial materials, especially in larger private-sector firms and accounting/billing service providers. However, for this occupation the core work is still governed by accounting rules, controls, and auditability, which limits fully autonomous AI use. Adoption is therefore stronger than mere experimentation, but it is not yet as mature or standardized as in some other knowledge occupations. Public-sector accounting roles also lag behind. Overall, the observable signal level fits significant but incomplete adoption.

nameSwedish: Arbetsledare inom bygg, anläggning och gruva
nameEnglish: Supervisors in construction, civil engineering and mining
description: Leder och fördelar arbete på arbetsplats. Bevakar att maskiner och material finns på plats och samordnar arbete med underentreprenörer. Har ansvar för att arbetet utförs enligt gällande ritningar och anvisningar och för att säkerhetsföreskrifter följs. Har visst personalansvar.
descriptionEnglish: Leads and allocates work on site. Ensures that machinery and materials are in place and coordinates work with subcontractors. Is responsible for work being carried out in accordance with drawings and instructions and for safety regulations being observed. Has some staff responsibility.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation combines coordination, planning, and compliance monitoring with substantial on-site supervision and interpersonal leadership. AI could assist with scheduling, progress tracking, document handling, risk checks, and coordination with subcontractors, but core tasks still require physical presence, judgment in changing site conditions, and direct responsibility for safety and work execution. The role is therefore moderately exposed, but not highly, because a large part of the work depends on real-world context and hands-on supervision.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
Current AI adoption for construction/site foreman roles in Sweden appears to be in the early adoption stage. There are observable signals of AI use around the occupation, but not widespread, role-specific deployment yet. The strongest current uses are indirect: AI-enabled planning and scheduling tools, computer vision/safety monitoring on larger sites, drone/image analysis for progress tracking, and AI features inside project-management or BIM/CAD software. These can support coordination, documentation, and risk monitoring, which are relevant to supervisors in construction, civil works, and mining.

However, for this specific occupation, most core tasks still rely on on-site human judgment, hands-on coordination, compliance oversight, and communication with crews and subcontractors. In Sweden, many construction and mining employers are still experimenting rather than scaling AI broadly in frontline supervisory roles. Publicly visible adoption is more common in larger contractors and industrial mining settings than in smaller firms. Overall, the evidence suggests some practical tooling exists and is being tested or selectively used, but it is not yet mainstream enough to score as moderate or high adoption.

Utför konstruktions-, ritnings- och beräkningsarbete. Organiserar, leder och samordnar det praktiska arbetet vid tillverkning av fordon, maskiner m.m. Provar och kontrollerar maskiner och andra mekaniska produkter.

Hit hör inte
Civilingenjörsyrken inom maskinteknik (2144)
descriptionEnglish: Carries out technical work related to the design, manufacture, assembly, maintenance and repair of vehicles, machines and other mechanical equipment, and heating, water, sanitation, ventilation and refrigeration systems. Performs design, drafting and calculation work. Organises, leads and coordinates practical work in the manufacture of vehicles, machines and similar products. Tests and checks machines and other mechanical products.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be little competition for jobs for engineers and technicians in mechanical engineering in the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have good opportunities for work, although the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the profession.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: In a two-year perspective, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for engineers and technicians in mechanical engineering will be great. The chances of finding work in the profession increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 20,200 working engineers and technicians in mechanical engineering in Sweden, of whom about 15 percent are women and 85 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation combines substantial technical knowledge work with hands-on engineering and site-based responsibilities. Tasks such as construction drafting, calculations, planning, coordination, documentation, and test analysis are highly screen- and knowledge-based and could be significantly supported by AI. However, the role also includes organizing practical work, overseeing production, and inspecting or testing machinery and mechanical systems in real-world settings, which require physical presence, judgment, and interaction with equipment and workers. In Sweden, adoption may also be moderated by established work practices and collective agreements. Overall, it is a mixed role with meaningful but not dominant AI exposure.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are clear current AI adoption signals for mechanical engineers and technicians, but they are still mostly early and uneven rather than fully mainstream. In Sweden, employers in manufacturing, automotive, industrial equipment, HVAC, and maintenance increasingly use AI-enabled tools for CAD/CAM support, predictive maintenance, quality inspection, scheduling, and documentation, and job ads increasingly mention AI literacy or digital engineering tools. However, for SSYK 3114 the core work remains strongly hands-on and field-based, so AI is typically supporting design, analysis, troubleshooting, and reporting rather than replacing or fundamentally transforming the occupation. Compared with software, customer service, or analytics roles, observable adoption is more limited and fragmented across firms, with larger industrial employers leading and smaller workshops adopting less. This places the occupation in early adoption with some employers experimenting and a growing set of tools, but not yet mainstream deployment.

description: Leder, planerar och samordnar verksamhet vid bankkontor eller bankavdelning. Gör kredit- och riskbedömningar vid exempelvis långivning. Svarar för ekonomisk rådgivning till företag och privatpersoner. Upprättar deklarationer, testamenten och andra ekonomiska handlingar.
descriptionEnglish: Leads, plans and coordinates activities at a bank branch or department. Carries out credit and risk assessments in areas such as lending. Provides financial advice to companies and private individuals. Prepares tax returns, wills and other financial documents.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for bank clerks in the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have limited to moderate opportunities for work, although the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the profession.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: In a two-year perspective, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for bank clerks will be limited to moderate. The chances of finding work in the profession increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 23,500 working bank clerks in Sweden, of whom about 60 percent are women and 40 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely knowledge- and screen-based: it involves credit and risk assessment, financial advisory work, document preparation, and coordination at a bank office. These tasks are highly amenable to AI support for data analysis, drafting, customer guidance, and decision support. However, the role still includes interpersonal advising, responsibility-bearing judgments, and some need for compliance and relationship management, which keeps it below the very highest exposure range.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
For Swedish bank officers, there are clear current AI adoption signals, but they are not yet universal or fully transformed into end-to-end automation. Banks in Sweden and the Nordics are actively using AI/ML for customer service chatbots, fraud detection, anti-money-laundering monitoring, credit scoring support, document classification, and internal advisory support. In practice, employees in bank branches and advisory functions increasingly encounter AI-assisted tools for summarizing customer data, drafting communications, and supporting compliance/risk workflows.

However, the occupation still relies heavily on regulated human judgment for lending decisions, financial advice, legal/estate-related documents, and customer-facing accountability. That means the adoption is significant but mostly augmentative rather than replacing the core role. Observable signals suggest many employers have deployed some AI-enabled tools, but usage is uneven across institutions and work tasks. This fits a solid mid-high adoption level rather than top-tier saturation.

scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption signals for veterinary nurses / animal care nurses in Sweden appear to be early-stage rather than mainstream. There are observable tools in the broader veterinary field, such as AI-assisted imaging support, scheduling/chatbots, and decision-support features in some practice management or diagnostic platforms, but there is little evidence that these are widely embedded in day-to-day workflows for Djursjukskötare m.fl. in Swedish clinics or animal hospitals. The role is still heavily hands-on and procedure-based, and most tasks—animal handling, assisting in examinations and surgery, sampling, and client guidance—are not yet commonly AI-automated. So the best fit is early adoption with some experimenting, not significant widespread use.

Utför experiment, analys- och kontrollarbete vid kemiskt och fysikaliskt industri- eller forskningslaboratorium. Kontrollerar, testar och analyserar råvaror och produkter. Assisterar vid utformning och utförande av experiment. 

Hit hör inte

Civilingenjörsyrken inom kemi (2145)
Ingenjörsyrken inom kemi (3115)

Laboratoriebiträde (5349)
descriptionEnglish: Carries out experiments, analysis and testing work in chemical and physical industrial or research laboratories. Checks, tests and analyses raw materials and products. Assists in the design and conduct of experiments.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be little competition for jobs for laboratory engineers in the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have good opportunities for work, although the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the profession.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: In a two-year perspective, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for laboratory engineers will be great. The chances of finding work in the profession increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 9,700 working laboratory engineers in Sweden, of whom about 50 percent are women and 50 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Laboratory engineers perform a largely knowledge-based job centered on experiments, analysis, quality control, and interpretation of results. Much of the work involves reviewing data, planning tests, documenting findings, and supporting experiment design, all of which are highly compatible with AI tools for data analysis, pattern detection, reporting, and decision support. However, the occupation also includes hands-on laboratory work, sample handling, equipment operation, and direct interaction with physical materials and instruments, which AI cannot fully replace. Overall, the role is predominantly cognitive/digital but still contains important physical and technical components, so exposure is high but not near-total.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
For laboratory engineers, there are clear current AI adoption signals, but they are still mostly at the early-adoption stage rather than mainstream. In Swedish industrial and research laboratories, AI is being used for data analysis, anomaly detection, instrument calibration support, predictive maintenance, image/signal interpretation, and to speed up documentation and reporting. These uses are observable in vendor tools, lab software, and employer interest, especially in pharmaceutical, biotech, food, chemical, and materials settings.

However, the core day-to-day work in this occupation is still heavily governed by validated methods, quality assurance, traceability, and regulatory requirements, which slows broad AI deployment. Many labs may experiment with AI-enabled platforms, but full operational integration is not yet common across employers. Compared with occupations like software development or customer support, adoption is much less mature.

So the current adoption level is best rated as early but real: some employers are experimenting and some tools are in use, but it is not yet mainstream.

nameEnglish: Technicians in diagnostic imaging and medical equipment
description: Installerar, kontrollerar, övervakar och utför service på medicinteknisk utrustning som används vid diagnosticering eller behandling av olika sjukdomar. Sköter och övervakar t.ex. hjärtlungmaskiner.
descriptionEnglish: Installs, checks, monitors and services medical equipment used in the diagnosis or treatment of various diseases. Operates and monitors, for example, heart-lung machines.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation combines substantial cognitive/technical work with hands-on physical service tasks. Technicians in medical imaging and medical equipment install, inspect, monitor, troubleshoot, and service specialized devices such as heart-lung machines, which requires being on site, interacting with equipment, and responding to real-world conditions that AI cannot fully perform remotely. At the same time, AI could meaningfully assist with diagnostics, fault detection, maintenance scheduling, documentation, and monitoring data analysis, so there is a moderate level of theoretical exposure. Because the core work is still equipment-based and context-dependent rather than mostly screen-based, the exposure is mixed rather than high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For Swedish biomedical equipment and diagnostic technicians, there are some observable AI-related signals, but current adoption appears marginal rather than mainstream. AI is increasingly present in the surrounding hospital and medtech environment: imaging diagnostics, decision support, predictive maintenance, and device monitoring are areas where vendors and healthcare providers are piloting or deploying AI-enabled systems. However, the occupation itself is primarily hands-on, regulated, and safety-critical, centered on installation, inspection, servicing, calibration, and supervision of medical devices. Those tasks are not yet widely automated by AI tools in day-to-day practice. In Sweden, public healthcare procurement and regulatory constraints also slow broad rollout. So the current picture is a few pilots and vendor-added features, but limited direct workflow adoption for this occupation.

nameEnglish: Biomedical laboratory scientists and related workers
description: Utför kliniska analyser vid sjukhus, läkemedelsindustrin eller laboratorium. Gör kemiska och biokemiska analyser samt tar prover. Hanterar teknisk apparatur och annan utrustning för provtagning och analys. Tar fram underlag som läkare behöver för att fastställa rätt diagnos. Deltar i forskning och utvecklingsarbete. Genomför analyser och dokumenterar bevis vid brottsutredningar.
descriptionEnglish: Carries out clinical analyses in hospitals, the pharmaceutical industry or laboratories. Performs chemical and biochemical analyses and takes samples. Handles technical apparatus and other equipment for sampling and analysis. Produces material doctors need to establish the correct diagnosis. Takes part in research and development work. Carries out analyses and documents evidence in criminal investigations.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be very little competition for jobs for biomedical analysts in the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have very good opportunities for work, although the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the profession.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: In a two-year perspective, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for biomedical analysts will be very great. The chances of finding work in the profession increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 9,500 working biomedical analysts in Sweden, of whom about 85 percent are women and 15 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation combines substantial knowledge-based work with hands-on laboratory and specimen handling. AI could assist strongly with interpreting test results, quality control, documentation, pattern recognition, reporting, and supporting research or forensic analysis. However, core tasks still require physical sample collection, operation of laboratory equipment, verification of results, compliance with strict procedures, and direct responsibility for diagnostic support, which limits full automation. In Sweden, regulatory requirements and established professional routines would also slow replacement, so the role is moderately exposed rather than highly exposed.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current adoption signals for biomedical analysts (SSYK 3212) are present but still limited and uneven. In Swedish healthcare and laboratory settings, AI is beginning to appear in adjacent workflows such as image/slide analysis, decision support, laboratory automation, and quality control, but there is not yet strong evidence that AI is routinely used across the occupation’s core tasks. Most routine work still depends on validated instruments, established laboratory information systems, and strict regulatory procedures, which slow deployment. Some employers may be piloting AI for pattern recognition, workflow optimization, or research support, especially in larger hospitals, private labs, and life-science companies, but this looks like early adoption rather than mainstream use. Overall, the occupation shows clear experimentation and some practical use, but not broad, mature AI integration yet.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be little competition for jobs for pharmacists in the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have good opportunities for work, although the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the profession.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: In a two-year perspective, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for pharmacists will be great. The chances of finding work in the profession increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: The assessment for pharmacists is an overall assessment for prescriptionists and pharmacists. There are about 6,600 working pharmacists in Sweden, of whom about 87 percent are women and 13 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Receptarier perform a largely knowledge-intensive job: reviewing prescriptions, preparing and dispensing medicines, checking quality, advising patients, and communicating with other healthcare actors. Many of these tasks are digital and rule-based, so AI can meaningfully support decision support, interaction checks, documentation, forecasting, and customer guidance. However, the role also includes hands-on preparation/dispensing, direct patient interaction, professional accountability, and compliance with Swedish healthcare/pharmacy regulations, which limit full automation. Overall, the occupation is strongly exposed to AI assistance, but not fully replaceable because of the mix of cognitive, procedural, and interpersonal duties.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are visible but still limited AI adoption signals for Swedish pharmacists/receptarier as of early 2026. In retail pharmacy and hospital pharmacy, AI is being used in adjacent workflows such as decision support, interaction checking, inventory forecasting, demand planning, and customer service/chat functions, but these are not yet broadly transforming the core dispensing and counseling role. In pharmaceutical industry settings, AI use is more established in quality control, documentation, pharmacovigilance, and analytical workflows, which does lift the occupation’s overall adoption signal somewhat. However, for everyday receptarie work in Swedish pharmacies, current deployment appears mostly experimental or embedded in specialized systems rather than a standard, widespread toolset across employers. The public-facing advisory and dispensing tasks remain heavily regulated and human-led, which slows large-scale adoption.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Aircraft technicians perform a mixed job: they rely on technical knowledge, documentation, troubleshooting logic, and inspection planning, which AI can support through diagnostics, maintenance records analysis, predictive maintenance, and procedural guidance. However, the core work is still highly hands-on and safety-critical, involving physical inspection, repair, testing, and compliance checks on aircraft and helicopters in real-world environments. Because the occupation combines significant cognitive tasks with substantial embodied maintenance work, its theoretical AI exposure is moderate rather than high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For aircraft technicians, there are some observable AI-related signals, but current adoption appears marginal rather than mainstream. In aviation maintenance, most of the visible digitization is still centered on established MRO software, predictive maintenance analytics, sensor-based monitoring, and rule-based diagnostics rather than broadly deployed generative AI workflows. A few larger airlines, MRO providers, and OEM-linked maintenance organizations may be experimenting with AI for fault prediction, inspection support, documentation search, and parts/logistics planning, but these are not yet widespread, routine tools across the occupation. The safety-critical nature of aircraft maintenance, certification requirements, and the need for traceable human accountability slow rapid AI deployment. In Swedish and European contexts, there are signs of interest in advanced analytics and maintenance optimization, but limited evidence that frontline flygtekniker roles are routinely using AI tools day to day. Overall this fits early experimentation with some pilots, not significant current adoption.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Air traffic controllers (Flygledare) perform highly cognitive, screen-based monitoring and decision-making: tracking aircraft positions, sequencing traffic, issuing clearances, and managing route, altitude, and runway instructions. These core tasks are information-dense and are areas where AI can strongly assist with surveillance, conflict detection, prediction, and decision support. However, the role remains safety-critical, time-sensitive, and heavily regulated, with human accountability, real-time judgment, and communication under uncertainty that cannot be fully delegated to AI. Because the occupation is predominantly digital/cognitive but includes significant human judgment and operational responsibility, it fits high theoretical exposure rather than full automation.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For Swedish air traffic controllers, there are clear signs of AI-enabled support tools in the broader aviation domain, but little observable evidence of direct, routine AI use in the core operational role as of early 2026. Air traffic control is a safety-critical, highly regulated public infrastructure function, so deployment tends to favor deterministic decision support, automation, and certified systems rather than general-purpose AI. Observable adoption signals are therefore mostly limited to pilots, vendor demonstrations, research, and adjacent applications such as surveillance analytics, flow management, and predictive maintenance, not mainstream controller-facing AI in daily operations. I do not see strong evidence of widespread employer adoption or job-advertisement signaling for AI in the front-line flygledare role, so this fits marginal adoption rather than early mainstream use.

description: Samordnar, leder och följer upp arbete vid tillverkningsprocesser.
descriptionEnglish: Coordinates, leads and follows up work in manufacturing processes.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation combines substantial cognitive and coordination work with ongoing on-site supervision in manufacturing. The core tasks include planning work, monitoring production flow, coordinating staff, solving operational problems, and reporting follow-up data, all of which are moderately AI-exposable through scheduling, analytics, and decision support. However, the role also depends on direct presence on the shop floor, quick human judgment, communication with workers, and responsiveness to real-world production issues, which limits full automation. In Sweden, collective agreements and established workplace routines may further slow adoption, but the task mix still suggests medium AI exposure.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For Swedish manufacturing supervisors, there are clear but still limited current AI adoption signals. In 2026, many factories and production environments are using digital planning, predictive maintenance, computer vision quality inspection, and AI-supported reporting in adjacent operational systems. Some employers also mention AI-assisted scheduling, production follow-up, and decision support in job ads and internal transformation projects. However, for the occupation itself, AI is not yet a mainstream day-to-day tool in the way it is for software development or customer support. Most supervisors still rely on traditional MES/ERP systems, dashboards, and manual coordination, with AI appearing mainly as embedded features in industrial software or in pilot initiatives. Overall this looks like early adoption with some real deployment, but not broad penetration across Swedish employers.

description: Utför annat tekniskt arbete än inom 3111-3117. Exempel på arbetsuppgifter: Samordnar tillverkning av metall-, glas-, keramik-, pappers-, läder-, textil- och träprodukter. Utför konstruktions-, ritnings- och beräkningsarbete. Gör kvalitets- och produktkontroller.
descriptionEnglish: Carries out other technical work not covered by 3111–3117. Examples of duties: coordinates the manufacture of metal, glass, ceramic, paper, leather, textile and wood products; performs design, drafting and calculation work; carries out quality and product inspections.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is mainly technical and knowledge-based: coordinating production, doing design/drawing/calculation work, and performing quality and product inspections. These tasks are highly compatible with AI support through drafting, simulation, documentation, specification checking, scheduling, and quality-analysis tools. However, the role is not fully screen-based: it likely includes coordination with production staff, hands-on verification, site or workshop presence, and context-specific judgment tied to materials and manufacturing processes. In Sweden, strong labor agreements may slow implementation, but the core task mix is still predominantly cognitive and digitally augmentable, so exposure is fairly high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
For SSYK 3119, there are clear early-adoption signals but not yet strong evidence of broad, routine AI use across the occupation. The role includes technical coordination, drafting, calculations, quality control, and product inspection, which are areas where AI-enabled CAD tools, generative drafting assistants, image-based defect detection, and engineering analysis software are increasingly available in Sweden. In manufacturing and product-development settings, some employers are likely experimenting with these tools, especially for documentation, design iteration, and quality inspection support.

However, the occupation is quite heterogeneous and typically embedded in production environments where adoption depends heavily on employer size, sector, and existing digital maturity. Compared with software, customer service, or analytics roles, there are fewer strong observable signals that AI is already mainstream for most workers in this occupation. The evidence points to experimentation and selective deployment rather than widespread standard use. Therefore, the current adoption level is best rated as early adoption, around the midpoint of the scale.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is highly screen- and knowledge-based. The core tasks include investigations, calculations, compiling and analyzing measurement data, and producing maps, all of which are well suited to AI assistance in data processing, pattern detection, drafting, visualization, and map generation. There may still be some field coordination, quality control, and domain-specific judgment involved, but the majority of the work is digital and cognitive rather than physical. In Sweden, adoption may be moderated by procurement, standards, and collective agreements, but the task structure itself is strongly exposed to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are clear current AI adoption signals for GIS and cartographic work, but they are still mostly in the early-to-mid adoption phase rather than fully mainstream. In practice, GIS professionals increasingly use AI-enabled features in mapping, spatial analysis, image classification, geocoding, object detection, and automated data cleaning within platforms such as ArcGIS, QGIS plugins, and cloud geospatial tools. Generative AI is also being used for scripting support, documentation, and workflow automation around spatial data processing. However, much of the core work still depends on domain-specific spatial reasoning, quality control, and authoritative data handling, so AI is typically assistive rather than replacing or fully transforming the occupation. In Sweden, adoption is likely stronger in consulting, utilities, infrastructure, and private-sector geospatial teams than in municipal or other public-sector settings, where rollout is slower. Overall, this suggests early adoption with some real deployment, but not yet broad, standardized use across the occupation.

descriptionEnglish: Carries out technical work related to chemical engineering production. Carries out quality and production inspections of chemical and chemical engineering products during the production process.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation combines substantial cognitive/technical work with meaningful hands-on production support. The role involves organizing and coordinating chemical manufacturing, monitoring quality and production processes, and performing technical checks, which are tasks that AI can help with through data analysis, process optimization, anomaly detection, documentation, and decision support. However, the occupation also includes on-site operational coordination and practical oversight in a physical production environment, where human judgment, safety responsibility, and direct interaction with equipment and workers remain important. Overall, it is a mixed exposure job with significant AI-assistable elements but not a fully screen-based occupation.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for chemical and chemical-engineering technicians appears to be in early-stage experimentation rather than mainstream deployment. There are observable signals of AI use in adjacent industrial settings: process industries, quality control, predictive maintenance, and lab/production monitoring increasingly mention machine learning, computer vision, and anomaly detection in vendor solutions and some Swedish employer postings. However, for this SSYK group, the core work is hands-on technical support in production, supervision, sampling, and quality/production control, where adoption is still mostly limited to decision-support tools rather than direct AI workflow integration. Compared with office-based technical roles, there are fewer clear signs of widespread AI tool use in daily tasks, and much of the value comes through broader plant systems rather than individual worker AI tools. In Sweden as of early 2026, the signal is therefore real but limited: some employers are experimenting, but AI is not yet a standard, broadly adopted tool for this occupation.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be little competition for jobs for engineers and technicians in electrical engineering in the next year. scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is mixed: it includes substantial cognitive and digital work such as technical design, drawings, calculations, testing documentation, and coordination, which are all fairly AI-exposed. At the same time, the role also involves practical work on electrical equipment and systems, on-site verification, installation support, maintenance, and troubleshooting in real-world settings, which AI cannot fully replace. Overall, AI can meaningfully assist with planning, analysis, documentation, and diagnostics, but the occupation remains anchored in hands-on technical work.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
Current AI adoption for electrical engineering technicians in Sweden appears to be in the early adoption stage rather than mainstream. Observable signals exist in the form of AI-assisted design tools, predictive maintenance, condition monitoring, automated inspection, and simulation software used in electrical engineering environments. Some employers likely use generative AI for documentation, requirements drafting, troubleshooting support, and code/PLC assistance, but these uses are not yet consistently embedded across the occupation.

The role is strongly tied to hands-on technical work, equipment commissioning, testing, maintenance, and coordination of practical operations, which limits how far generic AI can replace core tasks. Adoption is therefore more visible in adjacent digital tooling than in end-to-end workflow automation. In Sweden, larger industrial firms, utilities, and equipment manufacturers are more likely to be experimenting with AI, while smaller firms and field-heavy maintenance contexts probably show lower penetration. Overall, the current signal is real but not yet widespread enough to score as significant mainstream adoption.

descriptionEnglish: Carry out operational production, information and material planning based on estimated volumes, actual consumption and stock levels. Review designs in consultation with the designer on the basis of functional and manufacturing requirements. Set operation times and calculate labour, inventory, transport, service and production costs.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be little competition for jobs for engineers and technicians in manufacturing, logistics, and production planning in the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have good opportunities for work, although the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the profession.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: In a two-year perspective, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for engineers and technicians in manufacturing, logistics, and production planning will be great. The chances of finding work in the profession increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 7,800 working engineers and technicians in manufacturing, logistics, and production planning in Sweden, of whom about 24 percent are women and 76 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is mainly office- and knowledge-based, involving operational production planning, information and material planning, reviewing designs against technical requirements, scheduling operations, and calculating costs. These are highly digital, data-driven tasks where AI can assist substantially with forecasting, optimization, cost estimation, and document review. However, the role still requires coordination with engineers, practical understanding of production constraints, and human judgment tied to local processes and workflow realities. Because the work is not fully automatable and includes some context-dependent decision-making and collaboration, the exposure is high but not maximal.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are clear current AI adoption signals for this occupation, but they are still mostly early-stage and uneven rather than mainstream. In industrial, logistics, and production planning roles, employers are increasingly exposing planners to AI-enabled ERP/MES forecasting, demand planning, inventory optimization, scheduling, and exception-management tools. Large Swedish manufacturers and logistics operators are also experimenting with copilots and analytics assistants for production follow-up, reporting, and scenario analysis. However, observable adoption is not yet as standardized as in software development or customer support: many workplaces still rely on conventional planning systems, Excel, and rule-based optimization, with AI often appearing as add-on functionality or pilot projects rather than core workflow. The occupation includes operational planning and cost calculation, where AI can help, but it also requires domain judgment, coordination, and plant-specific constraints that limit full automation. Overall this points to early but real adoption, around the middle of the scale.

description: Utför annat socialt arbete än inom 2661-2663. Exempel på arbetsuppgifter: Handlägger mottagande och introduktion av nyanlända i kommun. Gör personutredningar och deltar i utslussningen vid intagnas återanpassning till samhället.
descriptionEnglish: Carry out social work other than that covered by 2661-2663. Examples of duties: managing reception and induction for newly arrived people in a municipality; carrying out personal assessments and participating in the step-down process as detainees are reintegrated into society.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be little competition for jobs for social workers and needs assessors in the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have good opportunities for work, although the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the profession.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: In a two-year perspective, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for social workers and needs assessors will be great. The chances of finding work in the profession increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: The assessment for social workers and needs assessors is an overall assessment for social workers, needs assessors, etc., and other occupations in social work. There are about 36,400 working social workers and needs assessors in Sweden, of whom about 83 percent are women and 17 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely cognitive and documentation-based: assessing cases, handling reception and introduction of newcomers, preparing investigations, coordinating discharge/reintegration, and producing decisions or recommendations. These are tasks where AI can strongly assist with information retrieval, drafting, summarizing case files, risk screening, and administrative processing. However, the role also involves substantial human interaction, judgment in complex welfare situations, statutory responsibilities, and coordination with clients and other public authorities. In Sweden, legal accountability and public-sector practice limit full delegation to AI, so the exposure is high but not near-fully automatable.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
Current adoption appears to be in the early-adoption range rather than mainstream. For this SSYK group, there are observable AI signals in Swedish public-sector and social-work-adjacent settings, especially for drafting, summarising case notes, document handling, translation, intake support, and decision-support triage. Municipal integration units, social services, and correctional/rehabilitation contexts have also begun testing generative AI and automation tools for administrative workload reduction.

However, the occupation is still highly human-centred, case-specific, and legally sensitive. Work involving reception of newcomers, person assessments, and reintegration planning depends on professional judgement, confidentiality, and accountability, which slows deployment. In Sweden, public-sector AI adoption is rising but remains uneven and generally more cautious than in private-sector service roles. That means there are real tools and some employer experimentation, but not yet broad, routine, occupation-wide AI use. A mid-4s score best matches the current observable signal.

description: Utreder individers behov av hjälp och stöd från kommunen t.ex. hemtjänst, personlig assistans eller särskilt boende m.m.
descriptionEnglish: Assess individuals' need for help and support from the municipality, such as home help, personal assistance or supported accommodation.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be little competition for jobs for social workers and needs assessors in the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have good opportunities for work, although the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the profession.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: In a two-year perspective, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for social workers and needs assessors will be great. The chances of finding work in the profession increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: The assessment for social workers and needs assessors is an overall assessment for social workers, needs assessors, etc., and other occupations in social work. There are about 36,400 working social workers and needs assessors in Sweden, of whom about 83 percent are women and 17 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely cognitive and document-based: assessing eligibility, gathering and interpreting information, applying legal and municipal rules, and producing written decisions. AI could meaningfully assist with information intake, summarization, risk flagging, and drafting case notes or decision templates. However, the role also requires substantial human judgment, interviews, empathy, negotiation with applicants and relatives, and accountability under Swedish welfare law, which limits full automation. In a Swedish public-sector context, statutory duties and collective agreement constraints would further slow adoption. Overall, the task mix is strongly exposed but not fully automatable.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
For this occupation, current AI adoption signals in Sweden appear to be in the early-adoption range rather than mainstream. There are observable tools and pilots that can support municipal case work, such as AI-assisted document summarization, call transcription, meeting notes, search across policy/regulation documents, and drafting of standard letters or decisions. In Swedish public-sector settings, these capabilities are increasingly available through enterprise productivity suites and some municipality/vendor implementations.

However, the core work of assessing individual needs for home care, personal assistance, or special housing is still highly regulated, context-dependent, and requires professional judgment, legal accountability, and face-to-face or phone-based assessment. That limits full automation and reduces broad operational deployment. Current signals are therefore more consistent with some employers experimenting or using adjacent productivity tools, while AI is not yet a mainstream, occupation-specific workflow standard across Swedish municipalities.

So the appropriate rating is moderate early adoption, around 4.5/10.

description: Ger information om samhällets olika möjligheter att ge hjälp och stöd till människor i behov av råd, stöd och hjälp. Undersöker olika lösningar för att förbättra hjälpsökandes livssituation.
descriptionEnglish: Provide information about the various ways society can offer help and support to people in need of advice, support or assistance. Explore different solutions to improve the situation of people seeking help.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be little competition for jobs for counselors in the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have good opportunities for work, although the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the profession.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: In a two-year perspective, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for counselors will be great. The chances of finding work in the profession increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 10,400 working counselors in Sweden, of whom about 87 percent are women and 13 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely knowledge- and communication-based: assessing a person’s situation, explaining available support options, documenting cases, and helping identify appropriate solutions. Those core tasks are highly exposed to AI through information retrieval, case summarization, decision support, drafting of guidance, and structured triage. However, the role also depends heavily on trust, empathy, nuanced human judgment, and context-sensitive conversations in a Swedish welfare setting. Statutory responsibilities and professional discretion also limit full delegation to AI. Overall, it is a predominantly cognitive role with meaningful interpersonal components, so exposure is high but not near-maximum.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
For social counsellors (kuratorer), there are observable but still limited current AI adoption signals. In Sweden, some municipalities, schools, healthcare-adjacent services, and welfare organizations have begun using AI-enabled tools for administrative support, case documentation, scheduling, document search, and drafting standard communications. General-purpose LLMs are also likely being used informally by staff for summarizing notes or preparing messages.

However, there is no strong evidence of widespread, occupation-specific AI workflows comparable to those in software development, customer support, or legal drafting. The work is highly human-centered, sensitive, and often handled in the public sector, where procurement, privacy, confidentiality, and risk concerns slow deployment. As of early 2026, the dominant pattern appears to be experimentation and selective administrative assistance rather than mainstream AI use in core counselling practice.

Overall, this fits early adoption rather than broad deployment.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be little competition for jobs for social workers and needs assessors in the next year. scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Socialsekreterare work is largely cognitive and documentation-heavy: assessing cases, compiling investigations, mapping needs, writing reports, advising, and making recommendations. scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for Swedish social workers/social secretaries appears to be in the early-adoption stage rather than mainstream. The occupation is also in a regulated, high-stakes public-sector setting involving sensitive personal data, child welfare, and legal/administrative decisions, which slows adoption significantly. As of early 2026, the clearest signal is limited pilots and cautious tool use rather than widespread operational integration.

descriptionEnglish: Lead and plan parish work in the Church of Sweden or another faith community, excluding free churches. Lead worship or mass and preach, and conduct liturgical duties at the altar. Perform baptisms, weddings and funerals. Interpret holy scriptures, are responsible for the church doctrine and teach matters of faith. Hear confessions and provide pastoral care.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Priests perform a mixed set of tasks. A substantial part of the role is knowledge-based and text-heavy: interpreting sacred texts, preparing sermons, teaching doctrine, planning services, and handling administrative and liturgical documentation. These activities are relatively exposed to AI support through drafting, research, summarization, and scheduling. However, the occupation also includes highly interpersonal and human-centered duties such as baptism, weddings, funerals, confession, and pastoral care, which depend on trust, presence, and religious authority and are not easily automated. Because the role combines significant cognitive work with essential face-to-face and spiritual functions, overall exposure is moderate rather than high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current observable AI adoption signals for priests in Sweden are very limited. There is some use of general-purpose AI tools in adjacent administrative work within church organizations, such as drafting emails, newsletters, event text, summaries, or translation, but these are not clearly widespread or occupation-specific. The core duties of priests in Sweden—liturgy, preaching, sacraments, pastoral care, confession, funerals, and doctrinal teaching—remain highly human-centered and are not meaningfully AI-automated in practice. I do not see strong evidence of systematic AI tooling being actively used across this occupation, beyond isolated experimentation or individual productivity use. Given the public-sector-like constraints, ethical sensitivity, and trust-based nature of the role, current adoption is best rated as marginal.

description: Framför klassisk musik, populär- eller kyrkomusik som sångare eller instrumentmusiker. Komponerar musikaliska verk, leder och instruerar orkestrar, körer m.m.
descriptionEnglish: Perform classical, popular or church music as singers or instrumental musicians. Compose musical works, and lead and instruct orchestras, choirs, etc.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for musicians, singers, and composers in the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have limited to moderate opportunities for work, although the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the profession.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: In a two-year perspective, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for musicians, singers, and composers will be limited to moderate. The chances of finding work in the profession increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 6,400 working musicians, singers, and composers in Sweden, of whom about 43 percent are women and 57 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The occupation combines substantial cognitive/digital work with important embodied and interpersonal performance duties. Composing music, arranging, preparing setlists, rehearsal planning, notation, and some orchestral or choir leadership are highly AI-exposable tasks, especially for drafting, generating ideas, and analysis. However, live performance as a singer or instrumentalist depends on human expression, timing, interpretation, stage presence, and interaction with audiences and ensembles, which AI cannot replace. In Sweden, adoption may also be moderated by strong labor agreements and the artistic nature of the work. Overall this is a mixed occupation with meaningful but partial AI exposure.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption signals for musicians, singers, and composers are visible but still mostly early-stage. There is clear use of generative music and audio tools for ideation, demo production, arrangement, lyric drafting, voice cleanup, and marketing/content creation, and some Swedish music workplaces, studios, and individual creators are experimenting with these tools. However, there is not yet broad, standardized, employer-driven deployment across the occupation in the way seen in software or customer support. Most professional use remains selective and project-based rather than embedded in everyday workflows, and many roles still depend heavily on human performance, interpretation, and composition. In Sweden specifically, adoption is present in creative industries but remains uneven and concentrated among tech-forward studios, labels, media companies, and independent creators. Overall this fits early adoption rather than mainstream use.

description: Skapar konst i olika former t.ex. måleri, grafik, teckning och skulptur, video och film, relationell konst, objekt, installation, miljögestaltning, performance, ljuskonst m.m. Vårdar, konserverar, restaurerar och preparerar föremål och material. Gör bokomslag, bokillustrationer, skisser till kläder m.m.
descriptionEnglish: Create art in various forms, such as painting, printmaking, drawing and sculpture, video and film, relational art, objects, installations, environmental design, performance, light art, etc. Conserve, preserve, restore and prepare objects and materials. Produce book covers, book illustrations, costume sketches, and similar work.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is mixed in nature. A significant part of the work is creative and knowledge-based, such as conceptualizing artworks, producing sketches, making design decisions, writing descriptions, and preparing digital or visual materials, all of which AI can assist with to some degree. However, the role also involves substantial hands-on artistic production, material handling, conservation/restoration work, and highly personal creative judgment that are difficult to automate. Because the core tasks combine digital/cognitive elements with physical, tactile, and highly individualized artistic work, the exposure is moderate rather than high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are clear current AI usage signals in this occupation, but they are still mostly early and uneven rather than mainstream. Visual generative AI tools are actively used by some illustrators, graphic artists, concept artists, book cover designers, and exhibition/installation practitioners for ideation, moodboards, drafts, variations, and rapid mockups. In Sweden and internationally, employers and clients in creative industries increasingly mention AI-assisted image generation, text-to-image workflows, and post-production tools in briefs and job ads. However, for fine art, conservation/restoration, and higher-end commissioned artistic work, human originality, authorship, and manual craft remain central, and many workflows still rely on traditional methods. Overall this looks like early adoption with real but partial deployment, not a fully AI-transformed occupation.

description: Översätter text eller tal från ett språk till ett annat. Språkgranskar texter. Utarbetar lexikon. Utvecklar teorier och modeller om språks uppbyggnad och egenskaper. Undersöker och beskriver ett språks ursprung och utvecklingen av dess ordförråd, grammatik och syntax.
descriptionEnglish: Translate written text or speech from one language into another. Proofread and edit texts for language. Compile dictionaries. Develop theories and models of language structure and properties. Investigate and describe a language's origins and the development of its vocabulary, grammar and syntax.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be very little competition for jobs for interpreters and translators in the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have very good opportunities for work, although the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the profession.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: In a two-year perspective, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for interpreters and translators will be very great. The chances of finding work in the profession increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 3,600 working interpreters and translators in Sweden, of whom about 65 percent are women and 35 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is overwhelmingly knowledge- and language-based, with core tasks such as translating text and speech, language reviewing, lexicography, and linguistic analysis. These activities are highly screen-based and closely aligned with current AI capabilities in machine translation, text generation, terminology management, and linguistic pattern analysis. While professional judgment, nuance, domain expertise, and quality control remain important, the task structure itself is strongly exposed to AI assistance and partial automation. Physical or interpersonal components are minimal.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are strong current adoption signals for translation, interpreting, and linguistics work, though not yet uniform across all employers. Machine translation and LLM-based drafting/editing tools are widely available and commonly used for first-pass translation, terminology work, text polishing, and language checking, especially in agencies, publishing, localization, and corporate communications. Many Swedish employers now advertise familiarity with AI-assisted writing, translation memory tools with machine translation integration, and language quality control workflows. That said, human oversight remains essential for high-stakes legal, medical, government, and nuanced literary/interpreting tasks, and public-sector adoption is slower. Overall, this looks like significant, current adoption with meaningful day-to-day use, but not full automation or universal deployment.

description: Samlar lokala, nationella och internationella nyheter med hjälp av intervjuer, undersökningar och observationer för tidningar, TV, radio och andra media. Redigerar artiklar och inslag. Granskar och bevakar uppgifter av allmänintresse. Undersöker och rapporterar om utvecklingen inom speciella områden, såsom medicin, vetenskap och teknik. Skriver ledare och kommentarer samt gör inslag om aktuella ämnen och uttrycker en tidnings, radio- eller TV-kanals åsikt.
descriptionEnglish: Gather local, national and international news through interviews, research and observation for newspapers, television, radio and other media. Edit articles and features. Review and monitor matters of public interest. Investigate and report on developments in specialist areas such as medicine, science and technology. Write editorials and commentaries, and produce features on current affairs representing a newspaper's, radio station's or television channel's viewpoint.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for journalists in the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have limited to moderate opportunities for work, although the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the profession.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: In a two-year perspective, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for journalists will be limited to moderate. The chances of finding work in the profession increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 10,600 working journalists in Sweden, of whom about 52 percent are women and 48 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Journalists primarily perform knowledge work: collecting information, interviewing, researching, verifying facts, editing, writing, and producing commentary for digital and broadcast media. These tasks are highly text- and information-centric, which aligns closely with current AI strengths such as drafting, summarization, translation, topic research, fact-check support, and content editing. There are still important human elements—source relationships, editorial judgment, on-the-ground reporting, accountability, and ethical decisions—but the core task structure is strongly exposed to AI assistance. In Sweden, adoption may be moderated by newsroom practices, unions, and editorial standards, but the occupation remains highly theoretically exposed.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
There are clear current adoption signals for journalists, but not yet universal or deeply standardized across the occupation. Newsrooms are actively using AI for transcription, translation, headline and social copy drafting, article summarization, SEO, content classification, and fast background research. Major media organizations in Sweden and internationally have also published AI guidelines and experimented with AI-assisted newsroom workflows, which is a strong observable signal of real deployment rather than pure talk.

At the same time, adoption is uneven and constrained by editorial trust, source verification, copyright, and reputational risk. Many journalists still use AI as a supporting tool rather than for core reporting or final publication, and public-service or regulated Swedish outlets tend to be more cautious. So this is above early experimentation, but short of near-universal workflow dependence.

description: Planerar och gör research för och skriver exempelvis böcker, manus och pjäser för publicering eller presentation. Korrekturläser och redigerar texter.
descriptionEnglish: Plan and conduct research for, and write, books, scripts and plays for publication or presentation. Proofread and edit texts.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is overwhelmingly knowledge- and text-based: planning, researching, writing books/scripts/plays, proofreading, and editing. These are core tasks that AI models can strongly assist with through drafting, editing, summarizing, idea generation, and language refinement. There are some human-only elements such as originality, judgment, and creative direction, but the work structure is highly screen-based and closely matches current AI capabilities. Swedish union or contractual protections may slow adoption, but they do not materially reduce the underlying task exposure.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are clear current adoption signals for writers, authors, and editors, but not yet universal deployment. AI-assisted proofreading and text refinement tools are also widely available and increasingly embedded in standard writing workflows. That said, most core professional writing remains human-led, and adoption is uneven across employers: larger private-sector organizations are faster to adopt than smaller publishers or public-sector cultural institutions. The occupation therefore fits significant but not dominant real-world adoption, rather than fully mature, ubiquitous use.

nameSwedish: Arkeologer och specialister inom humaniora m.m.
nameEnglish: Archaeologists and specialists in the humanities, etc.
description: Studerar människors kultur och livsbetingelser med hjälp av fynd och föremål från forntida boplatser, gravar m.m. Leder och organiserar arkeologiska utgrävningar. Studerar och tolkar urkunder, sociala samt rumsliga fenomen. Utvecklar teorier, modeller och metoder, bl.a. för att tolka och beskriva händelser, kulturella och ekonomiska förhållanden. Genomför experiment, analyserar och värderar data. Undersöker sociala strukturer och beteenden hos olika grupper i samhället, föreslår och värderar utfallet av socialpolitiska åtgärder och utvecklar teorier och modeller för politiska system och institutioner.
descriptionEnglish: Study human culture and living conditions using finds and artefacts from prehistoric settlements, graves, etc. Lead and organise archaeological excavations. Study and interpret records, and social and spatial phenomena. Develop theories, models and methods, including for interpreting and describing events and cultural and economic conditions. Conduct experiments, analyse and evaluate data. Examine social structures and behaviour in different groups in society, propose and evaluate the outcomes of social policy measures, and develop theories and models for political systems and institutions.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is primarily knowledge-intensive and screen-based. Core tasks include researching, interpreting texts and artifacts, developing theories and methods, analyzing data, writing reports, and evaluating findings—activities that AI can strongly assist with through information retrieval, summarization, pattern detection, drafting, and analytical support. However, archaeologists also lead field excavations and work with physical sites and materials, which adds some on-site, hands-on work that AI cannot fully replace. Overall, the role is mostly cognitive/digital with some physical components, so exposure is high but not maximal.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for archaeologists and humanities specialists in Sweden appears to be in the early-adoption stage rather than mainstream. Observable signals suggest some use of AI-enabled tools for text analysis, transcription, metadata extraction, image recognition, GIS/spatial analysis, and literature review, especially in research institutions, museums, universities, and cultural heritage projects. However, there is limited evidence that AI is broadly embedded in day-to-day workflows across employers in this occupation. The work remains highly expert-led, project-based, and often constrained by small teams, bespoke datasets, and public-sector procurement and governance, which slows large-scale deployment. Overall, there are real but still modest current adoption signals, consistent with experimentation and selective use rather than widespread operational dependence.
