description: Planerar och organiserar biblioteksverksamhet. Bedömer, köper in och klassificerar böcker, tidskrifter och andra typer av publicerat material. Erbjuder fördjupad litteratur- och informationsförmedling samt vägleder bibliotekets användare i informationssökning. Söker, bearbetar och presenterar information. Samlar in, undersöker, bedömer och ordnar handlingar. Värderar och bedömer vilken information som ska sparas för framtiden.
descriptionEnglish: Plan and organise library operations. Assess, acquire and classify books, journals and other published material. Provide advanced literature and information services and guide library users in information searching. Search for, process and present information. Collect, examine, assess and organise records. Evaluate what information should be preserved for the future.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for librarians and archivists in the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have limited to moderate opportunities for work, although the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the profession.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: In a two-year perspective, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for librarians and archivists will be great. The chances of finding work in the profession increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 7,900 working librarians and archivists in Sweden, of whom about 76 percent are women and 24 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is highly knowledge-intensive and centered on digital/text-based work: selecting, classifying, cataloguing, searching, evaluating, organizing, and presenting information. These are exactly the kinds of tasks AI systems can strongly assist with through automated metadata creation, semantic search, document classification, summarization, recommendation, and information retrieval. The role also includes advisory and curatorial judgment, which still requires human oversight, but the core task structure is predominantly screen-based and cognitively driven. Some interpersonal guidance to library users and institutional responsibility for preserving records reduce full automation potential, but overall AI exposure is high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for librarians and archivists in Sweden appears to be in early-stage experimentation rather than broad, routine deployment. There are observable signals such as library information search support, metadata assistance, document classification, digitization workflows, OCR, and archive description where AI tools can be used or piloted, and some Swedish public institutions and university libraries have started testing generative AI for reference support, summarization, and internal knowledge work. However, these jobs are still heavily shaped by public-sector governance, privacy, archival integrity, and quality-control requirements, which slow adoption. Unlike occupations with mature, widely deployed AI tooling, there is not yet strong evidence that AI is standard day-to-day infrastructure across Swedish libraries and archives. Overall this fits the lower end of early adoption: real tools and pilot use exist, but mainstream penetration is limited.

description: Planerar och organiserar verksamhet på museer och konstgallerier samt ordnar utställningar och visar samlingar. Samlar in, undersöker, bedömer, klassificerar och dokumenterar miljöer eller föremål av kulturhistoriskt värde. Vårdar, förmedlar kunskap och ger råd om föremål.
descriptionEnglish: Plan and organise work in museums and art galleries, and arrange exhibitions and displays of collections. Collect, examine, assess, classify and document environments or objects of cultural and historical value. Preserve, communicate knowledge about and advise on objects.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The occupation is largely knowledge-based and screen-based: planning museum and gallery activities, organizing exhibitions, researching and classifying objects, documenting collections, and providing expert advice. These are tasks where AI can significantly assist with information retrieval, cataloguing, drafting exhibit texts, metadata creation, translation, and preliminary analysis. However, the role also includes substantial human judgment, curatorial responsibility, stakeholder interaction, and some hands-on work with collections and exhibitions that AI cannot fully replace. In Sweden, adoption may be moderated by public-sector norms and collective agreements, but the core task structure is still moderately to highly exposed.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
Current AI adoption for museum curators and similar museum professionals in Sweden appears to be early but real. Observable signals include the use of generative AI for exhibition copy drafting, public-facing text, translation, image tagging, and metadata enrichment, as well as some experimentation with chatbots for visitor information and digital guides. AI-enabled collection management and search tools are also becoming available in cultural heritage software, which gives some practical entry points for this occupation.

However, there is little sign that AI is yet deeply embedded in core professional workflows such as provenance research, conservation decisions, curatorial judgment, or exhibition planning. Swedish museums are typically public-sector or publicly funded organizations, which tend to adopt new tools more cautiously than private-sector knowledge work. As a result, adoption is visible but not widespread enough to score as mainstream.

descriptionEnglish: Carry out other specialist legal work than that covered by 2611-2615. Examples of duties: responsibility for recovery of taxes, fines and other public funds, and enforcement of judgments and decisions in individual cases; handling legal matters within the armed forces or the church; drafting regulations and advising on the interpretation of environmental laws and regulations; conducting negotiations and providing legal advice.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for lawyers in the next year. scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is predominantly legal and administrative knowledge work: reviewing legal matters, interpreting regulations, drafting rules, giving legal advice, and handling enforcement or negotiations. These are tasks that AI can significantly assist with through document analysis, legal research, drafting, summarization, and decision support. The role is still constrained by professional responsibility, statutory duties, and the need for human judgment in public authority contexts, which reduces full automation. Some tasks involve negotiation and case handling where interpersonal and context-specific judgment matter, but the core work is still largely screen-based and cognitively intensive, so exposure is high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are clear current adoption signals for legal work, but they are uneven and not yet universal. In Sweden and broadly across Europe, lawyers and legal departments are actively using AI for document review, contract drafting, legal research, summarization, and first-pass analysis, especially in larger firms and in-house teams. Public-sector legal functions and quasi-public/legal-administrative roles adopt more slowly due to procurement, confidentiality, and governance constraints, which is relevant for this occupation’s mix of duties. For tasks like drafting regulations, handling legal cases, and advising on legal interpretation, AI tools are already being used as productivity support rather than as autonomous decision-makers. That places the occupation in the 'significant tooling available and many employers adopting' band, but not at the very high end because use is still selective and heavily supervised.

descriptionEnglish: Interpret laws and agreements as representatives of, for example, public authorities, local authorities and organisations. Monitor new legislation. Ensure compliance with legislation in the relevant field. Draft regulations and general guidance based on existing legal texts. Respond to consultations on proposed legislation.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for lawyers over the coming year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have limited to moderate opportunities for work, but the labour market may vary depending on specialisation within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for lawyers will be very good. The opportunities for work in the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, authorisations or professional licences.
forecast.ingress: The assessment for lawyers is an overall assessment for other lawyers, prosecutors, administrative and organisational lawyers, judges, business and corporate lawyers, and attorneys. There are about 23,100 employed lawyers in Sweden, of whom about 58 percent are women and 42 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is overwhelmingly knowledge-based and screen-based. The core tasks involve interpreting legislation and agreements, monitoring new laws, drafting regulations and general guidance, and responding to referrals/consultations. These are exactly the kinds of text-heavy analytical activities that AI can substantially assist with through document review, legal search, summarization, drafting, and consistency checks. There are some institutional and legal constraints in Sweden that limit full delegation, but the task structure itself is highly exposed to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are clear current adoption signals for AI in this occupation, but they are still mostly in early-stage and assistive use rather than fully embedded practice. For Swedish administrative and public-law lawyers, observable use cases include AI-assisted first drafts of legal texts, summarization of long regulatory documents, document review, research support, and help tracking legislative changes. These tools are increasingly available in mainstream legal software and generic LLM products, and some Swedish public agencies and municipalities have begun pilots or limited deployment for internal drafting and analysis. However, the occupation is highly sensitive to accuracy, confidentiality, traceability, and legal responsibility, which slows broad rollout. In the public sector especially, adoption remains uneven and cautious, with many employers still in experimentation or policy-development phases rather than routine production use. Overall this fits early adoption with real but not yet mainstream deployment.

Represent clients in legal matters and negotiations. Prepare cases and represent clients in court. Draft insurance terms and conditions.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for lawyers over the coming year. scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is highly knowledge- and document-intensive. Core tasks include interpreting laws, drafting and reviewing contracts and legal documents, assessing legal enforceability, preparing cases, and supporting negotiations and court proceedings. These are largely screen-based cognitive activities that AI can substantially assist with through legal research, document drafting, clause review, issue spotting, and case summarization. However, the role also includes client representation, negotiations, and court-facing work that require professional judgment, accountability, persuasion, and human interaction, which limits full automation. In Sweden, legal responsibility and professional standards further constrain delegation to AI, but the underlying task structure is still strongly exposed.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current adoption signals for business and corporate lawyers in Sweden are significant and clearly observable, though not yet universal. Law firms and in-house legal teams are actively using AI-enabled tools for contract review, clause extraction, legal research support, document drafting, and due diligence triage. Major legal software vendors now market AI features specifically to Nordic legal workflows, and Swedish employers increasingly mention AI, automation, and legal tech in legal job ads and competence requirements. However, usage is still uneven: many organizations restrict AI for confidentiality, privilege, and GDPR/data-residency reasons, and Swedish public-sector legal work remains more cautious. Overall, this is well past experimentation and into practical adoption, but not yet fully mainstream across the occupation.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The role of a prosecutor is highly knowledge-intensive and largely screen-based: reviewing case files, assessing evidence, making legal judgments, drafting charges, preparing for hearings, and formulating arguments in court. scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption in the prosecutor role appears to be in early-stage experimentation rather than mainstream deployment. In Sweden, the public sector generally adopts AI more cautiously than private-sector knowledge work, and there is little evidence that AI is yet a standard tool embedded in everyday prosecutorial workflows across employers. So this looks like a few pilots and localized use, not broad adoption.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for lawyers over the coming year. scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is overwhelmingly knowledge- and judgment-based. Judges interpret laws and contracts, prepare and manage cases, decide procedural order, adjudicate disputes, and write judgments and decisions—tasks that are highly text-centric, analytical, and document-driven. AI can strongly support legal research, summarization, drafting, and case management, and some decision-support functions are plausible. However, the core adjudicative authority remains human and legally constrained, so AI exposure is high but not equivalent to full automation.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For Swedish judges, there are some observable AI-related signals, but they are still limited and not yet mainstream in day-to-day adjudication. Courts and public legal bodies may use generic generative AI tools for administrative drafting, summarization, or internal knowledge search, but there is little evidence that judges are routinely using specialized AI decision-support systems in actual case handling. The work is highly regulated, high-stakes, and tied to legal accountability, which slows deployment in the Swedish public sector. As of early 2026, the strongest signal is experimentation and cautious piloting rather than broad operational adoption, so this fits a marginal-to-early adoption level.

descriptionEnglish: Interpret laws and agreements. Prepare cases and represent a party in disputes and negotiations. Present a party's case in court. Provide legal advice and prepare legal documents.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for lawyers over the coming year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have limited to moderate opportunities for work, but the labour market may vary depending on specialisation within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for lawyers will be very good. The opportunities for work in the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, authorisations or professional licences.
forecast.ingress: The assessment for lawyers is an overall assessment for other lawyers, prosecutors, administrative and organisational lawyers, judges, business and corporate lawyers, and attorneys. scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is highly knowledge-intensive and largely screen-based. Core tasks include interpreting laws and contracts, preparing cases, representing clients in disputes and negotiations, arguing in court, giving legal advice, and drafting legal documents. These are text-heavy, analytical, and decision-support oriented activities that AI can strongly assist with through legal research, document drafting, case summarization, contract analysis, and procedural support. There are still important human elements such as client trust, courtroom advocacy, negotiation strategy, and formal legal responsibility, which prevent a perfect 10, but the overall exposure is very high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are strong current adoption signals for lawyers in Sweden, but not yet universal deployment. AI-assisted legal drafting, contract review, e-discovery, research, and document summarization are now mainstream enough that many Swedish law firms and in-house legal teams actively use commercial LLM-based tools or integrated legal-tech products. This is especially visible in private practice, where efficiency pressure is high and use cases are low-risk/augmentative. Swedish and Nordic vendors, as well as global legal AI platforms, are widely available and increasingly mentioned in procurement, training, and job ads. However, adoption is still uneven by firm size and practice area, and public-sector legal work remains more cautious due to confidentiality, professional responsibility, and data protection concerns. Overall this fits significant but not saturated adoption, so 7/10.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is highly screen- and knowledge-based. Core tasks include risk and vulnerability analysis, designing security solutions, and configuring IT security systems, all of which rely on digital information processing, documentation, pattern detection, and decision support—areas where AI can strongly assist. There are some human and organizational components such as advising stakeholders, prioritizing risks, and aligning solutions with business needs, but the main work is still cognitive and technical. In Sweden, adoption may be moderated by procurement, governance, and labor arrangements, yet the task structure itself is highly exposed to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for IT security specialists is clearly above early experimentation and appears to be in the significant-adoption range. In practice, security teams are already using AI-enabled capabilities in SIEM/SOAR platforms, EDR/XDR tools, phishing detection, alert triage, vulnerability prioritization, and security copilots embedded in mainstream vendor products (for example Microsoft, Palo Alto, CrowdStrike, Splunk, and others). These are observable, deployed workflows rather than theoretical use cases. In Sweden, large enterprises and many regulated organizations are actively piloting or rolling out such tools, especially for SOC operations and threat analysis. However, adoption is not yet universal or fully mature: many employers still restrict generative AI use for sensitive security work due to confidentiality, false positives, and governance concerns, and public-sector uptake is slower. Overall, the occupation shows strong current adoption signals, but not quite at the level of fully standardized, ubiquitous AI-assisted work across the market.

descriptionEnglish: Plan, develop, document and carry out tests at different levels, from individual software to entire systems. Verify that programs and systems work according to user requirements, established guidelines and regulations.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be low competition for jobs for system testers and test managers over the coming year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have very good opportunities for work, but the labour market may vary depending on specialisation within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for system testers and test managers will be very good. The opportunities for work in the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, authorisations or professional licences.
forecast.ingress: There are about 6,900 employed system testers and test managers in Sweden, of whom about 35 percent are women and 65 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is highly screen- and knowledge-based. Core tasks include planning, designing, documenting, and executing software and system tests, verifying requirements, and checking compliance with rules and regulations. These are largely digital, text-heavy, and pattern-based activities that AI can strongly support through test case generation, defect detection, requirements analysis, regression testing, and documentation assistance. While human oversight, coordination, and final responsibility remain important, the core task structure is very exposed to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are clear current adoption signals for AI in software testing and test leadership, but it is not yet fully mainstream across all Swedish employers. In practice, many teams now use LLM-based assistants to draft test cases, generate test data, summarize requirements, suggest edge cases, and help with regression-test prioritization. Test automation platforms and QA tools increasingly include AI features for self-healing tests, flaky-test analysis, and anomaly detection, and these are observable in the market rather than just theoretical. In Sweden, this is especially visible in larger private-sector IT organizations and product companies, while public-sector testing tends to adopt more slowly due to procurement, governance, and security constraints. Overall, the occupation shows significant tooling availability and a meaningful share of employers experimenting or adopting, but the use is not yet universal enough for an 8+.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is highly screen- and knowledge-based, centered on designing, coding, testing, and iterating digital games and interactive media. That said, adoption is not uniform: some studios are conservative due to IP, style consistency, licensing, and quality-control concerns, so usage is strongest in programming, prototyping, marketing content, and iteration rather than fully autonomous production.

nameEnglish: Information officers, communication specialists and PR specialists
description: Planerar, utvecklar, genomför och utvärderar informations- och kommunikationsstrategier om företags eller organisations mål, policy, verksamhet, resultat och aktuella händelser. Utformar informationsinnehåll.
descriptionEnglish: Plan, develop, implement and evaluate information and communication strategies concerning a company's or organisation's goals, policies, activities, results and current events. Design informational content.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for information officers, communication specialists and PR specialists over the coming year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have limited to moderate opportunities for work, but the labour market may vary depending on specialisation within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for information officers, communication specialists and PR specialists will be limited to moderate. The opportunities for work in the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, authorisations or professional licences.
forecast.ingress: There are about 19,500 employed information officers, communication specialists and PR specialists in Sweden, of whom about 68 percent are women and 32 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is highly knowledge- and communication-intensive, with core tasks centered on planning, developing, producing, and evaluating information and communication strategies and creating informational content. These are screen-based activities where AI can already assist strongly with drafting, editing, summarizing, audience analysis, content planning, translation, and media monitoring. The role also involves some human judgment, stakeholder alignment, and organizational context that AI cannot fully replace, which keeps it below the absolute maximum. In Sweden, adoption may be moderated by union agreements and established communication responsibilities, but the task structure itself is still highly exposed to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
Current AI adoption for communicators, information officers, and PR specialists in Sweden appears significant and clearly observable by early 2026, though not yet fully mature or universal. The strongest signals are widespread use of generative AI for drafting press releases, web copy, social media posts, internal communications, FAQs, campaign concepts, and media monitoring/summarization. These are exactly the kinds of text-heavy, repetitive, and time-sensitive tasks where many employers can already deploy AI tools with low integration cost.

In the Swedish market, agencies and larger organizations are increasingly listing AI-assisted content creation, prompt-based drafting, and AI-supported communication workflows in job ads and internal competency requirements. Many teams are also using general-purpose tools such as Microsoft Copilot, ChatGPT, and similar enterprise assistants for brainstorming, rewriting, translation, tone adaptation, and versioning in both English and Swedish. This is especially visible in private-sector communication departments and agencies.

However, adoption is not as advanced as in professions where AI is embedded in core production systems. In communications and PR, AI is often an assistant rather than a fully integrated workflow engine, and human review remains essential for brand voice, compliance, political sensitivity, and reputational risk. Public-sector and regulated organizations in Sweden also tend to adopt more cautiously due to governance, transparency, and procurement constraints.

Overall, the occupation shows strong current adoption signals with many employers already using AI tools operationally, but the use is still uneven and not yet fully standardized across the field. That supports a rating in the upper-middle range, around 7.

descriptionEnglish: Plan and organise recruitment, development, redeployment and termination of staff. Prepare staff budgets and training plans. Take part in negotiations on pay and employment conditions, and interpret and provide information on employment law and agreements.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for HR specialists over the coming year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have limited to moderate opportunities for work, but the labour market may vary depending on specialisation within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for HR specialists will be very good. The opportunities for work in the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, authorisations or professional licences.
forecast.ingress: There are about 21,000 employed HR specialists in Sweden, of whom about 78 percent are women and 22 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is primarily cognitive and screen-based: recruiting, workforce planning, budgeting, training plans, labor-law interpretation, and participation in wage and employment-condition negotiations are all information-intensive tasks where AI can provide substantial assistance. AI is well suited to drafting job ads, screening applications, analyzing compensation data, summarizing collective agreements, preparing HR documentation, and supporting compliance work. However, the role also involves interpersonal negotiation, judgment in sensitive personnel matters, and interaction with unions and managers, which reduces full automation. In Sweden, strong collective agreements and legal responsibilities also limit how far AI can replace core HR decision-making, but the exposure remains high because most tasks are digital and knowledge-based.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for HR specialists in Sweden appears to be in the early-adoption range rather than mainstream. There are clear observable signals of use: AI is increasingly used for drafting job ads, screening CVs, summarizing interviews, answering routine employee questions, and supporting policy/document creation in HR systems. Many common HR software platforms now include AI features, and Swedish employers are increasingly mentioning AI in recruitment and HR workflows. However, for this occupation, adoption is still uneven and often limited to assistive tasks rather than core decision-making. Sensitive areas such as labor law interpretation, union negotiations, compensation setting, and formal personnel decisions tend to remain human-led, especially in Sweden where compliance, transparency, and public-sector caution slow deployment. Overall, the occupation shows real but not yet deep or universal AI adoption.

description: Informerar och ger råd till studerande om studieval, yrken och yrkesval. Organiserar elevernas arbetsplatspraktik.
descriptionEnglish: Provide information and advice to students on study choices, occupations and career options. Organise pupils' work placements.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be low competition for jobs for study and career counsellors over the coming year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have very good opportunities for work, but the labour market may vary depending on specialisation within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for study and career counsellors will be very good. The opportunities for work in the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, authorisations or professional licences.
forecast.ingress: There are about 4,700 employed study and career counsellors in Sweden, of whom about 81 percent are women and 19 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The occupation is largely cognitive and information-based: advising students on study choices, occupations, and career paths, plus organizing work placements. These tasks involve gathering, interpreting, and communicating structured information, which AI can significantly assist with through recommendation support, matching, scheduling, drafting guidance, and handling routine questions. However, the role also requires interpersonal counseling, trust-building, nuanced judgment, and coordination with students, schools, and workplaces in real-world contexts. In Sweden, public-sector and school-based responsibilities also limit full automation because many advisory and statutory duties must remain human-led. Overall, the job is highly exposed to AI as a decision-support and administrative tool, but not easily fully automated.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for Swedish study and career guidance is best described as early adoption. There are observable signals that some municipalities, schools, and career guidance organizations are experimenting with AI for drafting communication, summarizing student information, creating informational materials, and supporting search over program and labor-market information. However, the occupation is still heavily shaped by human counseling, trust, confidentiality, and nuanced guidance, which limits direct automation. In the Swedish public-sector context, adoption is also slower and more cautious due to governance, privacy, and procurement constraints. Overall, AI tools exist and are being piloted or used in pockets, but they are not yet mainstream in this occupation.

nameEnglish: Special needs teachers and special educational needs teachers, etc.
description: Undervisar elever med behov av särskilt stöd i förskola och förskoleklass samt grund- och gymnasieskola. Anpassar undervisningen efter elevernas förutsättningar och använder speciella hjälpmedel och arbetsmetoder. Ger specialpedagogiskt stöd till lärare och utvecklar undervisningsmetodik. Utvärderar och inspekterar tillämpningen av skollag, läroplaner och förordningar.
descriptionEnglish: Teach pupils in need of special support in preschool, preschool class, primary school and upper secondary school. Adapt teaching to pupils' circumstances and use special aids and working methods. Provide special educational support to teachers and develop teaching methods. Evaluate and inspect the application of education acts, curricula and regulations.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that competition for jobs for special needs teachers and special education teachers will be very low over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have very good employment prospects, but the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that employment opportunities for special needs teachers and special education teachers will be very good. Job prospects in the occupation improve if you have relevant certificates, licenses, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are approximately 14,200 practicing special needs teachers and special education teachers in Sweden, of whom about 89 percent are women and 11 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely knowledge-based and involves planning, adapting instruction, documenting student progress, producing support materials, and evaluating policies and educational practices. These are tasks where AI could meaningfully assist with assessment, lesson adaptation, report drafting, and analysis of guidelines. However, the role also requires substantial human interaction, judgment in sensitive pedagogical situations, collaboration with teachers and parents, and in-person work with students who need special support. In Sweden, statutory responsibilities and the relational nature of school support also limit full automation, so the exposure is high but not near-complete.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for special teachers and special educators in Sweden appears to be in the early-adoption stage rather than mainstream. There are observable signals of AI use in adjacent activities such as lesson planning, adaptation of teaching materials, speech-to-text, text simplification, translation, and support for administrative documentation. Some Swedish schools and municipalities are experimenting with generative AI for pedagogical support and special-needs accommodations, and vendors of education software increasingly market AI-enabled accessibility and individualized learning features. However, the occupation itself is highly dependent on human judgment, relationship-based work, sensitive pupil data, and public-sector governance, which slows deployment. There is no strong evidence of broad, standardized, occupation-wide AI integration comparable to more digitized professions. Overall this points to some active experimentation and selective use, but not yet significant mainstream adoption.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for investigators and qualified case officers over the coming year. scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely knowledge-intensive and screen-based. Swedish legal and organizational constraints may slow adoption, yet the exposure to AI is still high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
There are clear current adoption signals for planners and investigators, but they are uneven and not yet fully mainstream across the whole occupation. In Sweden, many roles in this category already use LLMs and AI-assisted tools for drafting reports, summarizing documents, preparing stakeholder memos, scanning regulations, and supporting policy or case analysis. These are observable, practical uses rather than theoretical potential, and they are increasingly common in municipalities, agencies, consultancies, and larger organizations.

However, the occupation also includes public-sector investigative and regulatory work where adoption is slower due to legal accountability, confidentiality, procurement constraints, and the need for traceable reasoning. In practice, AI is often used as a drafting and analysis aid rather than an autonomous decision-maker, and many employers are still in pilot or limited-rollout stages. That places the role above early experimentation, but below the most deeply AI-integrated professions.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is primarily knowledge-based and screen-based: analyzing organizations, identifying inefficiencies, preparing proposals, and planning improvement measures. description: Studerar, analyserar, utvecklar och förklarar ekonomiska drivkrafter utifrån nationalekonomiska teorier och modeller. Jämför samhällsekonomiska teorier och modeller för att analysera och prognostisera beteenden och fenomen på individ-, företags- och samhällsnivå exempelvis arbetslöshet, inflation, investeringar och ekonomisk tillväxt. Analyserar stora världshändelser och länders förhållanden. Föreslår och värderar utfallet av politiska reformer och åtgärder. Utvecklar nya ekonomiska modeller.
descriptionEnglish: Study, analyse, develop and explain economic drivers using theories and models from economics. Compare economic theories and models to analyse and forecast behaviour and phenomena at individual, company and societal level, such as unemployment, inflation, investment and economic growth. Analyse major world events and national conditions. Propose and evaluate the outcomes of policy reforms and measures. Develop new economic models.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is almost entirely cognitive and knowledge-based. Core tasks include analyzing economic data, comparing theories and models, forecasting macroeconomic trends, evaluating policy reforms, and developing new models. These are exactly the kinds of screen-based analytical and text-intensive tasks where AI can strongly assist with research, pattern detection, drafting, scenario analysis, and model building. There is little to no physical, outdoor, or hands-on work, and limited need for direct interpersonal service, so theoretical exposure is very high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for economists and macro analysts in Sweden appears to be in early-stage use rather than mainstream deployment. Observable signals include growing use of LLMs and analytics tools for desk research, scenario drafting, literature summarization, data cleaning, and first-pass forecasting support in banks, consultancies, government agencies, and large firms. These tools can meaningfully assist with writing, information synthesis, and exploring macro scenarios, but they do not yet replace the core work of economic judgment, model design, policy evaluation, or interpretation of Swedish/European institutional context. In the public sector and many research-oriented settings, adoption is still cautious due to requirements around transparency, reproducibility, data confidentiality, and model governance. Overall, this occupation shows clear but not yet deep or universal AI adoption signals, fitting an early adoption score of 5.

descriptionEnglish: Manage, buy and sell shares, bonds and other securities. Set financial goals and develop strategies to achieve them. Manage funds. Monitor investment performance, and review and revise investment plans in response to needs and market changes.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for financial analysts and fund managers over the coming year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have limited to moderate opportunities for work, but the labour market may vary depending on specialisation within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for financial analysts and fund managers will be limited to moderate. The opportunities for work in the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, authorisations or professional licences.
forecast.ingress: The assessment for financial analysts and fund managers is an overall assessment for financial analysts and investment advisers, traders and fund managers. There are about 7,300 employed financial analysts and fund managers in Sweden, of whom about 31 percent are women and 69 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is highly screen- and knowledge-based. Core tasks include analyzing markets, selecting and trading securities, setting financial goals, developing investment strategies, monitoring portfolio performance, and revising plans based on market changes. These are activities where AI can strongly assist with information processing, forecasting, screening opportunities, risk analysis, and generating recommendations. There are limited physical or hands-on components, and the work is largely digital and decision-oriented. In Sweden, human oversight, fiduciary responsibility, and regulated investment processes will remain important, but the task structure is still very exposed to AI support and partial automation.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for traders and fund managers is clearly visible and more than experimental, but it is not yet universal or fully autonomous. In practice, many Swedish banks, asset managers, and trading desks use AI-enabled tools for market screening, news and sentiment analysis, portfolio monitoring, anomaly detection, risk management, and reporting. LLMs are also being used for drafting investment memos, summarizing research, and supporting compliance workflows. However, core decisions in trading and fund management remain strongly human-led because of fiduciary responsibility, market risk, regulation, and the need for judgment under uncertainty. So the occupation shows significant active use of AI tools today, but not at the highest saturation level.

descriptionEnglish: Analyse companies' finances, financial conditions and prospects, and business opportunities. Develop financial and investment strategies for clients. Advise individuals, companies and organisations on savings, investments and insurance, and on buying and selling securities. Analyse and evaluate investment options, shares or bonds, and work with portfolio management. Provide advice and handle tax matters, company formation, and the purchase and sale of, for example, property.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for financial analysts and fund managers over the coming year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have limited to moderate opportunities for work, but the labour market may vary depending on specialisation within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for financial analysts and fund managers will be limited to moderate. forecast.ingress: The assessment for financial analysts and fund managers is an overall assessment for financial analysts and investment advisers, traders and fund managers. There are about 7,300 employed financial analysts and fund managers in Sweden, of whom about 31 percent are women and 69 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is overwhelmingly cognitive, analytical, and screen-based. Core tasks include analyzing company finances and prospects, evaluating investment alternatives, developing financial and investment strategies, advising clients on savings, insurance, securities, and tax-related matters, and managing portfolios. These are all information-intensive activities where AI can strongly assist with data analysis, forecasting, scenario modeling, report drafting, and recommendation support. There are some interpersonal elements in advising clients and some regulated judgment, but the core work is still highly knowledge-based and digital, so exposure is very high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
There are strong current adoption signals for finance analysts and investment advisors, but not full saturation. In Sweden, employers in banking, asset management, insurance, and corporate finance are increasingly using AI for market/news monitoring, screening companies and securities, drafting client materials, summarizing research, portfolio analytics, and internal compliance/support tasks. These are well-supported by modern LLM and analytics tools, and many firms already expose such tooling to analysts or expect staff to use it.

However, the occupation still involves high-trust advisory work, regulatory judgment, suitability checks, and client-specific recommendations, which limits fully automated deployment. The observable pattern is substantial augmentation rather than replacement: AI helps with research, drafting, and pattern detection, while final investment decisions and client advice remain human-led. Overall, this fits significant and growing adoption, but not near-universal mainstream automation across all employers.

description: Analyserar, följer upp och bevakar företags, organisationers och myndigheters resultat, lönsamhet, verksamhetsplan och hållbarhetsstrategier. Formulerar mål, gör prognoser och anger riktlinjer för ekonomiarbetet. Utvecklar planerings- och uppföljningssystem.
descriptionEnglish: Analyse, follow up and monitor the results, profitability, business plans and sustainability strategies of companies, organisations and public authorities. Formulate objectives, make forecasts and set guidelines for financial work. Develop planning and monitoring systems.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for controllers over the coming year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have limited to moderate opportunities for work, but the labour market may vary depending on specialisation within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for controllers will be very good. The opportunities for work in the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, authorisations or professional licences.
forecast.ingress: There are about 19,100 employed controllers in Sweden, of whom about 59 percent are women and 41 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The controller role is predominantly cognitive and screen-based: analyzing financial and operational data, monitoring performance, preparing forecasts, setting guidelines, and developing planning and follow-up systems. These tasks are highly compatible with AI support for data analysis, reporting, scenario forecasting, anomaly detection, and drafting recommendations. The occupation still requires human judgment, organizational context, and stakeholder communication, which reduces it slightly from the very highest exposure band. In Sweden, implementation may be moderated by collective agreements and organizational routines, but the core task structure remains highly exposed to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are clear current adoption signals for Controllers, but not yet at the level of fully embedded, occupation-specific AI workflows. In Swedish workplaces, controllers increasingly use AI-enabled features in BI and planning tools for forecasting, variance analysis, reporting, and scenario generation, especially in larger private-sector firms. General-purpose LLMs are also being used for drafting management summaries, explaining deviations, preparing budget commentary, and speeding up ad hoc analysis. However, the role still depends heavily on structured financial data, internal governance, and company-specific models, so AI is mostly augmenting rather than replacing core work. Adoption is stronger in private enterprises and shared-service finance functions, while public-sector controllers move more slowly. Overall this fits significant current use with uneven penetration, not yet near-universal deployment.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that competition for jobs for preschool teachers will be very low over the next year. scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Preschool teachers spend a substantial share of their work on planning, documenting, assessing, and adapting pedagogical activities, which are knowledge- and text-based tasks that AI can assist with. However, the core of the job is highly interpersonal and context-dependent: supervising young children, building trust, responding to emotions and conflicts, and ensuring safety in real time. scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are some observable AI adoption signals for preschool teachers in Sweden, but they remain early and fragmented. However, there is little evidence of routine, occupation-specific AI deployment in everyday preschool teaching, and public-sector constraints make widespread adoption slow. The role is still primarily human-centered, with safety, child development, privacy, and accountability limiting automation. Overall, this looks like early adoption rather than mainstream use.

descriptionEnglish: Teach in primary school. Plan, deliver and evaluate teaching in primary school in accordance with the curriculum and subject syllabuses. Carry out assessment and grading.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that competition for jobs for primary school teachers will be very low over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have very good employment prospects, but the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that employment opportunities for primary school teachers will be good. Job prospects in the occupation improve if you have relevant certificates, licenses, or professional credentials.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Primary tasks are cognitive and screen-based: planning lessons, preparing materials, assessing student work, documenting progress, and grading. These are areas where AI can provide substantial support through content generation, differentiation, feedback drafting, and administrative automation. However, the role also depends heavily on real-time classroom management, pedagogical judgment, interpersonal interaction, and legal/statutory responsibilities that cannot be delegated to AI in Sweden. Because the work is mostly digital/knowledge-based but still includes important human and contextual elements, the exposure is high but not near-maximal.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are clear but still limited current adoption signals for Swedish primary school teachers. Teachers also increasingly encounter general-purpose LLM tools such as Microsoft Copilot, ChatGPT, and education-focused platforms through employer accounts or personal use. However, deployment is not yet mainstream in Swedish grundskolan, and use is uneven due to public-sector procurement constraints, data/privacy concerns, child safeguarding, and uncertainty around assessment integrity. The strongest observable signal is experimentation and partial uptake rather than broad standardized use, so this fits early adoption.

description: Utför annat undervisningsarbete inom högskola och universitet än inom 2311-2314. Exempel på arbetsuppgifter: Undervisar, handleder samt examinerar inom grundutbildningen. Deltar i utvecklingen av utbildningen inom området.
descriptionEnglish: Carry out other teaching work in higher education and universities than that covered by 2311-2314. Examples of duties: teach, supervise and examine at undergraduate level; take part in developing education in the field.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is highly knowledge-based and centered on teaching, supervising, examining, and developing university education. The core work involves creating course materials, delivering lectures, assessing student performance, giving feedback, and administrative/academic coordination, all of which are strongly exposed to AI support for drafting, content generation, grading assistance, and learning analytics. However, the role also includes significant interpersonal and judgment-based components such as mentoring students, adapting teaching to group dynamics, and making academic decisions, which AI cannot fully replace. In Sweden, formal responsibilities and academic quality assurance further limit direct substitution, so the exposure is high but not near-total.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current adoption signals for other university and higher education teachers in Sweden appear to be in the early-adoption range rather than mainstream. There are observable uses of generative AI for lecture preparation, drafting learning materials, brainstorming assignments, language polishing, and supporting administrative tasks such as email writing and rubric drafting. Some Swedish universities have begun issuing guidance, training, and limited-access tools for staff, which indicates real experimentation. However, the core teaching, supervision, assessment, and curriculum development work is still largely human-led, and there is no strong evidence of widespread, standardized AI deployment across Swedish higher education faculty. Public-sector caution, academic integrity concerns, and uneven institutional policies also slow broad adoption. Overall, the occupation shows meaningful but still limited current AI use.

Doctoral students conducting their research outside higher education institutions (included in the relevant occupational field).

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Doctoral candidates primarily perform research, literature review, data analysis, writing, and academic communication, all of which are highly screen- and knowledge-based tasks that AI can meaningfully assist with. They also often prepare presentations, draft papers, and support undergraduate teaching, which are similarly exposed to AI tools. The occupation is less exposed than fully autonomous knowledge work because research requires original judgment, supervision, experimentation, and some interpersonal/teaching elements that AI cannot fully replace, but the core task structure is still strongly cognitive and digital.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Doctoral researchers (PhD students) in Sweden show early but not yet mainstream AI adoption. Observable signals include widespread use of general-purpose LLMs for literature searches, writing support, coding, translation, summarization, and coding assistance in research groups, especially in STEM and data-heavy fields. Some universities and supervisors have begun issuing AI-use guidance and encouraging responsible use in research and teaching support. However, adoption is uneven across disciplines, often informal, and not tied to standardized institutional workflows. There is limited evidence of occupation-specific, employer-deployed AI tooling comparable to what is seen in software development or customer service. In public universities, governance, privacy, and academic integrity concerns also slow formal deployment. Overall this fits early adoption: tools are clearly present and used by many, but not yet mainstream or standardized across the occupation.

description: Undervisar samt deltar i utvecklingen av grund- och forskarutbildning. Handleder och examinerar studerande inom grundutbildning. Handleder doktorander. Deltar i internt ledningsarbete. Planerar och genomför forskningsverksamhet.
descriptionEnglish: Teach and take part in the development of undergraduate and postgraduate education. Supervise and examine undergraduate students. Supervise doctoral students. Take part in internal management work. Plan and carry out research activities.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: University lecturers perform a largely cognitive and screen-based job: preparing and delivering teaching content, supervising students and doctoral candidates, assessing assignments and exams, developing curricula, and conducting research and administrative planning. These activities are highly compatible with AI assistance in drafting materials, literature review, data analysis, feedback generation, grading support, and research workflow management. However, the role also includes important interpersonal, pedagogical, and institutional tasks such as live teaching, supervision, mentoring, examination judgment, and internal leadership, which AI cannot fully replace. In Sweden, statutory and academic quality requirements, along with strong labor agreements, would likely slow direct substitution even though the task structure is highly exposed.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
There are clear but still uneven current adoption signals for university and higher-education lecturers in Sweden. By early 2026, many lecturers and departments are using generative AI in limited, practical ways: drafting lecture materials, creating quizzes and rubrics, summarizing research, language editing, and support for administrative tasks. Universities also increasingly offer institutional guidance on AI in teaching, assessment, and research integrity, which indicates real workplace use rather than mere speculation.

However, adoption is not yet mainstream in a uniform, embedded sense across the occupation. Use is highly dependent on individual lecturers, faculties, and university policies, and concerns about academic integrity, data protection, and assessment validity slow broader deployment. In Sweden’s public-sector higher-education context, formal procurement and standardized rollout are still relatively cautious compared with private-sector roles. So the signal is stronger than early experimentation, but not strong enough to rate as widespread, mature adoption.

description: Har ämnesansvar och övergripande ansvar för utbildnings- och forskningsverksamhet inom ämnet. Leder, genomför och utvecklar utbildning på alla nivåer inom högskola och universitet. Handleder forskarstuderande, leder forskargrupper och bedriver forskning. Utför sakkunniguppdrag.
descriptionEnglish: Have subject responsibility and overall responsibility for education and research within the subject. Lead, carry out and develop education at all levels in higher education and universities. Supervise postgraduate research students, lead research groups and conduct research. Undertake expert advisory assignments.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Professors' work is overwhelmingly knowledge-based and screen-based: researching, writing, reviewing literature, preparing and delivering teaching materials, supervising doctoral students, analyzing data, and producing expert opinions. These are tasks where AI can strongly assist with search, drafting, summarization, coding, and research support. There are still important human components—mentoring students, leading research groups, academic judgment, and in-person teaching or collegial interaction—but they do not dominate the core task structure. In Sweden, strong institutional rules and academic norms may slow adoption, but the occupation remains highly exposed to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption among university professors in Sweden appears to be in the early-adoption range. There are clear observable signals of use: professors and academic staff increasingly use generative AI for drafting lecture materials, summarizing literature, brainstorming research ideas, language polishing, and administrative support. Swedish universities have also been running policy discussions, guidelines, and pilots around AI in teaching, assessment, and research workflows. However, this is not yet mainstream or uniformly embedded in the profession. In many departments, use remains individual and uneven, with cautious governance due to concerns about academic integrity, research quality, privacy, and handling of sensitive data. Compared with more operational occupations, the role is less standardized for AI tooling, so adoption is real but still fragmented rather than fully normalized.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation combines clinical assessment, diagnosis, treatment planning, rehabilitation, and preventive care with direct patient interaction and hands-on work. scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For SSYK 2289, there are some observable AI adoption signals, but they appear limited and uneven rather than mainstream. Current tools that may be present include AI-supported scheduling, documentation assistance, clinical decision support, and some digitally enabled rehabilitation or therapy applications, but these are not strong, occupation-wide signals. Public healthcare adoption is also typically slower in Sweden due to procurement, regulation, and integration constraints. Overall, this occupation shows marginal-to-early adoption rather than broad active use.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The occupation combines substantial knowledge-based work with hands-on and customer-facing duties. Overall, this is a mixed role with moderate theoretical AI exposure.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption signals for opticians in Sweden appear to be in the early-adoption range rather than mainstream. Some employers and suppliers likely experiment with AI for sales, triage, inventory, and image-based assistance. However, the core clinical work of eye examination, fitting, and prescribing remains heavily dependent on professional judgment, in-person testing, and regulated workflows, which limits broad deployment. As of early 2026, there are clear tools and pilots, but not strong evidence of widespread routine AI use across the occupation in Sweden.

nameEnglish: Audiologists and speech and language therapists
description: Utreder och bedömer hörselbesvär och anpassar hörhjälpmedel. Informerar och utbildar om hörselsvårigheter. Undersöker, ställer diagnos och behandlar röst-, tal- och språkstörningar. Leder träning och ordinerar kommunikationshjälpmedel.
descriptionEnglish: Assess and evaluate hearing problems and fit hearing aids. Provide information and training on hearing difficulties. Examine, diagnose and treat voice, speech and language disorders. Lead therapy and prescribe communication aids.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Audiologists and speech-language pathologists perform a substantial amount of cognitive, assessment-based work that is highly amenable to AI support: case history review, hearing and speech-language testing interpretation, documentation, diagnosis support, treatment planning, and patient education materials. AI could assist with screening, draft reports, pattern recognition in test data, and personalized rehabilitation suggestions. However, the occupation also requires direct patient interaction, hands-on fitting and adjustment of hearing aids and communication aids, clinical judgment in complex cases, and therapy/training that depends on human observation and rapport. In the Swedish context, statutory responsibilities and strong professional norms also limit full delegation. Overall, the role is mostly knowledge-based with important interpersonal and clinical components, placing it in the 7–8 range.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current adoption signals for audiologists and speech-language pathologists in Sweden appear to be in the early stage rather than mainstream. Observable use cases exist, especially for AI-supported transcription, documentation, appointment/admin support, hearing-aid fitting software, speech analysis, and some digital therapy/assessment tools, but these are not yet broadly described as standard practice across employers. In Swedish healthcare, especially public sector settings, procurement, privacy, and clinical validation requirements slow deployment. Overall, the occupation shows some real experimentation and limited operational use, but not enough evidence of widespread, routine AI integration in core clinical work.

description: Forskar inom, tillverkar, kontrollerar, marknadsför och säljer läkemedel inom exempelvis apotek, läkemedelsindustri och sjukhus. Informerar allmänheten och vårdpersonal om läkemedlets effekter och användning.
descriptionEnglish: Research, manufacture, check, market and dispense medicines in settings such as pharmacies, the pharmaceutical industry and hospitals. Inform the public and healthcare staff about the effects and use of medicines.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that competition for jobs for pharmacists will be low over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have good employment prospects, but the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that employment opportunities for pharmacists will be good. Job prospects in the occupation improve if you have relevant certificates, licenses, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: The assessment for pharmacists is a combined assessment for prescriptionists and pharmacists. There are approximately 6,600 practicing pharmacists in Sweden, of whom about 87 percent are women and 13 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The occupation is predominantly knowledge-based and screen-based: researching medicines, compiling and checking documentation, evaluating effects and interactions, writing reports, advising patients and healthcare professionals, and supporting marketing and sales with pharmaceutical knowledge. These are tasks where AI can strongly assist with literature review, decision support, drafting, quality control, and information synthesis. However, the role also includes some laboratory/manufacturing oversight, regulatory responsibility, and interpersonal communication that reduce full automation potential. In Sweden, professional accountability and regulatory constraints also limit delegation of core decisions, so exposure is high but not near-total.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
There are clear but still limited current AI adoption signals for pharmacists (Apotekare) in Sweden. In pharmacy and pharmaceutical settings, AI is being used for literature screening, pharmacovigilance signal detection, demand forecasting, medication safety checks, and drafting patient-facing or professional information. Larger Swedish employers in pharma and hospital settings are increasingly exposed to these tools, and some workflows around regulatory writing, quality control, and market intelligence are being augmented by LLMs or analytics systems.

However, this is not yet a broadly standardized, occupation-wide deployment level. Many core pharmacist tasks remain highly regulated, require human accountability, and involve sensitive clinical judgment, which slows adoption compared with software, marketing, or customer support roles. In public-sector and hospital pharmacy contexts, implementation is especially uneven. So the signal is real and growing, but still best characterized as early-to-mid adoption rather than mainstream.

description: Förebygger, förbättrar och kompenserar nedsatt aktivitetsförmåga. Tränar fysiska och psykiska förmågor samt förändrar arbetsmetoder. Föreskriver och anpassar hjälpmedel, t.ex. IT-baserade hjälpprogram, specialdesignade redskap m.m. Medverkar i frågor om tillgänglighet i den fysiska miljön, t.ex. anpassning av bostad, arbetsplats och skola.
descriptionEnglish: Prevent, improve and compensate for reduced ability to carry out activities. Train physical and mental abilities and adapt working methods. Prescribe and adapt aids, such as IT-based assistive software and specially designed devices. Take part in accessibility matters in the physical environment, such as adapting housing, workplaces and schools.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that competition for jobs for occupational therapists will be very low over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have very good employment prospects, but the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that employment opportunities for occupational therapists will be very good. Job prospects in the occupation improve if you have relevant certificates, licenses, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are approximately 8,700 practicing occupational therapists in Sweden, of whom about 93 percent are women and 7 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale:
Occupational therapists have a mixed task profile. A substantial part of the job is cognitive and documentation-heavy: assessing functional ability, planning interventions, writing reports, recommending assistive devices, and using digital tools to adapt workplaces, homes, or schools. These elements are relatively exposed to AI support, especially for screening, documentation, drafting plans, and generating recommendations.

At the same time, core work depends on in-person evaluation, hands-on training, observation of physical and psychological functioning, and close interpersonal interaction with patients, families, schools, and employers. Many decisions are highly context-dependent and tied to statutory responsibilities in the Swedish welfare and care system, which limits direct automation. Overall this places the occupation in the middle range with meaningful AI assistive potential but strong non-automatable components.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption among occupational therapists in Sweden appears to be in the early adoption phase rather than mainstream. Observable signals include the use of digital and AI-enabled assistive technologies, such as IT-based aids, communication supports, cognitive training tools, and assessment/documentation software that may incorporate automation or decision support. In Swedish healthcare and rehabilitation settings, some employers are also experimenting with AI for scheduling, documentation, symptom screening, and accessibility-related planning. However, there is limited evidence that AI is yet widely embedded in core occupational therapy workflows across employers. The profession remains highly hands-on, individualized, and tied to patient interaction and environmental adaptation, which slows large-scale AI deployment. Public sector constraints and cautious procurement further limit diffusion. Overall, there are real but still modest current adoption signals, consistent with early experimentation and selective use rather than broad routine use.

descriptionEnglish: Assess, diagnose and treat patients who have or are at risk of developing functional impairments due to diseases or injuries in various organ systems, such as the respiratory, circulatory, nervous and musculoskeletal systems, as well as mental ill health.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that competition for jobs for physiotherapists will be very low over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have very good employment prospects, but the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that employment opportunities for physiotherapists will be very good. Job prospects in the occupation improve if you have relevant certificates, licenses, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are approximately 11,900 practicing physiotherapists in Sweden, of whom about 76 percent are women and 24 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Physiotherapists and physical therapists have a mixed task profile. A meaningful share of the job is cognitive and screen-based: taking histories, documenting assessments, planning treatment, monitoring progress, and producing patient education materials are all areas where AI can assist. AI can also support exercise prescription, triage, and clinical decision support. However, the core work also depends heavily on hands-on examination, manual treatment, patient interaction, and real-world functional assessment, which are difficult to automate and in many cases require professional judgment and in-person care. In the Swedish welfare and healthcare context, statutory responsibilities and strong professional norms further limit delegation of core duties to AI. Overall, the occupation has moderate theoretical AI exposure rather than high exposure.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for physiotherapists and physical therapists in Sweden appears to be in the early-adoption stage. There are observable signals of AI use in adjacent healthcare areas, such as digital triage, rehabilitation apps, motion-analysis tools, and documentation support, but these are not yet mainstream day-to-day tools for most physiotherapists. In Swedish public healthcare, deployment tends to be slower due to procurement, compliance, and integration constraints, which limits broad adoption. Some private clinics and rehab providers may already use AI-enabled scheduling, symptom screening, exercise recommendation, or patient communication tools, but the occupation itself is not strongly associated with mature, widespread AI workflows in current job-market signals. Overall this suggests experimentation and selective uptake rather than established routine use.

scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption signals for chiropractors and naprapaths in Sweden appear marginal. descriptionEnglish: Examine, diagnose and treat sick and injured animals. Advise on animal health care and breeding. Inspect livestock and slaughter animals. Advise on and inspect food hygiene, animal welfare, disease control and the import of animals and foodstuffs.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Veterinarians perform a mix of cognitive, diagnostic, and advisory tasks that are highly amenable to AI support: symptom triage, differential diagnosis, review of lab results, image analysis, record keeping, treatment suggestions, herd health monitoring, and food hygiene or disease surveillance. Much of the work is screen- and knowledge-based, especially in consultation, compliance, and inspection contexts. However, the occupation also requires hands-on physical examination and treatment of animals, direct client interaction, and real-world judgment in often unpredictable settings, which limits full automation. In Sweden, statutory responsibilities, professional accountability, and cautious adoption in regulated public-interest tasks further reduce near-term replaceability. Overall, the role is moderately to highly exposed to AI, but not close to fully automatable.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption in veterinary work appears to be in early-stage use rather than mainstream deployment. There are observable signals of AI in adjacent veterinary areas such as image analysis for radiology, decision support for triage, herd-health monitoring, and automated analysis of lab or sensor data, but these are not yet broadly embedded across Swedish veterinary practice. Most veterinarians still rely primarily on clinical examination, laboratory tests, and established practice software, with AI often limited to a few larger clinics, animal hospitals, research settings, or vendor demonstrations. In Sweden, public and regulatory parts of the occupation are also likely to adopt more slowly due to trust, liability, and biosecurity requirements. Overall, this looks like some real experimentation and selective use, but not widespread daily AI dependence.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Psychotherapists perform a mix of cognitively intensive and highly relational work. A substantial part of the job involves interviewing clients, identifying patterns, planning treatment, documenting sessions, and preparing assessments — all areas where AI could assist with note-taking, triage, symptom screening, and decision support. Overall, the occupation shows limited but real pilot-level adoption rather than mainstream use.
