Ger stöd vid organisationsförändringar, ledarskapsutveckling och personalförsörjning m.m. Utvecklar teorier, modeller och metoder inom psykologi och psykoanalys.
descriptionEnglish: Examine, assess, diagnose and treat people's mental functions, problems and illnesses. Provide advice and guidance in conflict resolution. Provide support in organisational change, leadership development and workforce planning, etc. Develop theories, models and methods within psychology and psychoanalysis.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that competition for jobs for psychologists will be very low over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have very good employment prospects, but the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that employment opportunities for psychologists will be very good. Job prospects in the occupation improve if you have relevant certificates, licenses, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are approximately 9,000 practicing psychologists in Sweden, of whom about 73 percent are women and 27 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Psychologists’ work is largely cognitive and knowledge-based: assessment, diagnosis, documentation, treatment planning, counseling, and report writing are all tasks where AI can provide substantial support. AI can also assist with pattern recognition, screening, drafting notes, and generating decision-support suggestions. However, the occupation also depends heavily on human interaction, trust, therapeutic alliance, ethical judgment, and nuanced case-specific interpretation, especially in Swedish healthcare and welfare contexts where responsibilities cannot be delegated to AI. Overall, the role is highly exposed to AI as an assistive tool, but not easily replaceable because the core work is interpersonal and professionally regulated.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Psychologists in Sweden show early but not yet mainstream AI adoption. Observable current signals include use of AI-assisted note drafting, transcription, summarization of session/admin text, questionnaire scoring support, and general-purpose LLMs for documentation or patient communication drafts in some private clinics and digital mental health providers. There are also niche tools for screening, triage, and CBT-style digital therapy support, but these are unevenly deployed and often tightly constrained by clinical governance, privacy, and liability concerns. In the public sector and regulated care settings, adoption remains cautious due to GDPR, patient safety, and the need for human clinical judgment. Overall, there are real tools and some employer adoption, but they are not yet standard practice across the occupation.

descriptionEnglish: Assist with rehabilitation and care for work-related illnesses within occupational health services. Prevent illness and occupational injuries.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that competition for jobs for registered nurses will be very low over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have very good employment prospects, but the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that employment opportunities for registered nurses will be very good. Job prospects in the occupation improve if you have relevant certificates, licenses, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: The assessment for registered nurses is a combined assessment for registered nurses and occupational health nurses. There are approximately 49,900 practicing registered nurses in Sweden, of whom about 89 percent are women and 11 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Company nurses in Sweden perform a mix of cognitive, documentation, preventive, and advisory tasks alongside direct health-related contact with employees. AI could meaningfully assist with triage support, record keeping, risk analysis, rehabilitation planning, and drafting of health promotion materials. However, the role also depends on clinical judgment, personal interaction, trust-building, and assessment in a workplace context, which cannot be fully automated. Swedish legal and organizational constraints in occupational health also limit delegation of core responsibilities to AI, keeping exposure at a moderate level.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
For occupational health nurses (Företagssköterskor), there are clear but still limited current AI adoption signals as of early 2026. In Swedish occupational health services, AI is being used or piloted for administrative support, triage/appointment handling, symptom questionnaires, document summarization, and decision support around work-related rehabilitation processes. Some employers and vendors in healthcare-adjacent workflows have started introducing LLM-based tools for note drafting, patient communication, and knowledge search, which can also benefit corporate health nursing.

However, this is not yet a mainstream, occupation-specific AI workflow in Sweden. The core professional tasks—clinical judgment, rehabilitation coordination, workplace risk assessment, and individualized employee support—remain largely human-led, and public-sector-like compliance constraints, patient data sensitivity, and uneven digital maturity slow broader deployment. So the current signal is best described as early adoption with some real-world experimentation rather than significant widespread use.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be very little competition for jobs for district nurses over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have very great opportunities for work, but the labor market can vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that job opportunities for district nurses will be very good. Job opportunities within the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, licenses, or professional credentials.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: District nurses perform a mixed role with both knowledge-heavy and hands-on components. They assess patients’ health status, make care judgments, give advice, document, and plan care, which are tasks that AI could partially support through decision support, triage assistance, and documentation tools. However, the job also involves in-person patient contact, physical examinations, home visits, and relationship-based care in a Swedish healthcare setting where statutory responsibility remains with the nurse. Because the core work is partly digital/cognitive but still strongly embedded in direct care and contextual judgment, the occupation has moderate theoretical AI exposure rather than high exposure.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are observable signs of early AI use in Swedish primary and community healthcare, but not yet broad, routine deployment for district nurses specifically. Current adoption is mostly limited to adjacent tools such as AI-supported triage/chat, symptom assessment, documentation assistance, scheduling, and decision-support features embedded in electronic health record systems. In Sweden, public-sector healthcare tends to adopt more slowly than private-sector or administrative functions, and district nursing work is highly hands-on, patient-facing, and regulated, which limits near-term automation. There are pilot projects and vendor offerings, but as of early 2026 the occupation still appears to be in the early adoption phase rather than mainstream AI-enabled practice.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be very little competition for jobs for physicians without specialist training over the next year. scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation combines substantial cognitive work with significant hands-on medical duties. Physicians spend a large share of time on patient interviewing, diagnosis, treatment planning, documentation, and reviewing test results, all of which are highly AI-exposable. At the same time, they also perform physical examinations, procedures, and sometimes surgery, and they must take legal and ethical responsibility for decisions. In Sweden, statutory duties, patient safety requirements, and strong professional norms further limit direct automation. Overall, the occupation is moderately exposed: AI can strongly assist with analysis, drafting, and decision support, but cannot replace the core clinical and interpersonal responsibilities.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For Swedish physicians in this occupation, there are clear current adoption signals but not yet universal mainstream deployment. AI is being used in practice for clinical decision support, image analysis support, documentation/dictation assistance, triage, and coding/workflow tasks in both hospitals and private care settings. In Sweden, larger regions and hospital systems have started piloting or rolling out such tools, and clinicians increasingly encounter AI-enabled features in EHR-adjacent systems. However, adoption remains uneven because of regulatory scrutiny, patient safety requirements, integration burdens, procurement cycles, and the slower pace of public-sector implementation. So this looks like significant but still partial adoption rather than fully mature, occupation-wide use.

descriptionEnglish: Undertake a time-limited specialist training programme consisting of education and supervised medical work. Examine patients, make diagnoses and draw up treatment plans.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be very little competition for jobs for physicians without specialist training over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have very great opportunities for work, but the labor market can vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that job opportunities for physicians without specialist training will be very good. Job opportunities within the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, licenses, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: The assessment for physicians without specialist training is a combined assessment for other physicians, residents, and trainee doctors. There are about 18,100 working physicians without specialist training in Sweden, of whom about 56 percent are women and 44 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: ST-läkare work is highly knowledge-intensive and largely centered on digital and cognitive tasks: reviewing patient information, taking histories, assessing symptoms, formulating diagnoses, documenting findings, and drafting treatment plans. However, the job also includes direct patient interaction, supervised clinical judgment, responsibility under a licensed physician framework, and hands-on examination in real-world care settings, which limits full automation. In Sweden, statutory and professional requirements also mean AI cannot replace the clinician’s core responsibility. Overall, the occupation has high theoretical AI exposure, but not near-total exposure.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are observable but still limited current AI adoption signals for Swedish ST-läkare. In Sweden, some regions and hospitals are experimenting with AI for clinical support tasks such as image triage, documentation support, scheduling, and decision-support systems, and these can affect residents indirectly. However, ST-läkare work is highly supervised, legally regulated, and embedded in public-sector healthcare, where deployment is slower and more uneven than in private-sector knowledge work. There is no strong evidence of broad, routine AI use directly in the core ST-läkare workflow of examining patients, making diagnoses, and planning treatment. Overall this fits early adoption rather than mainstream use.

So the occupation shows real current adoption, but not yet mainstream across the full role.

nameEnglish: Occupational health and safety engineers and occupational and environmental hygienists
description: Inspekterar arbetsplatser för att kontrollera att arbetsmiljölagen och gällande föreskrifter följs. Kartlägger och analyserar arbetsmiljö för att garantera och stärka hälsosamma arbetsförhållanden. Mäter hälsorisker och utreder arbetsskador. Informerar om risker samt föreslår och genomför lösningar för att förebygga sjukdom eller skada.
descriptionEnglish: Inspect workplaces to ensure compliance with the Work Environment Act and applicable regulations. Map and analyse the working environment to ensure and improve healthy working conditions. Measure health risks and investigate occupational injuries. Inform about risks and propose and implement solutions to prevent illness or injury.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is mainly knowledge-intensive and partly digital. The core tasks—inspecting workplaces, analyzing work environment data, assessing legal compliance, measuring health risks, investigating injuries, and drafting recommendations—are all activities where AI can strongly assist with document review, risk pattern detection, report writing, regulatory checking, and decision support. However, the role also requires on-site inspections, direct interaction with employers and employees, and context-specific judgment in real workplaces, which AI cannot fully replace. In Sweden, statutory responsibilities and established labor-market practices also mean AI would more likely augment than substitute the professional role.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for occupational health and safety engineers and industrial hygienists appears to be in early-stage use rather than mainstream deployment. Observable signals suggest that AI is being used selectively for tasks like document drafting, incident-report summarization, regulatory search, sensor/data analysis, and possibly image-based workplace inspection support, but there is little evidence of broad, routine AI-assisted practice across Swedish employers in this occupation. The work is highly regulated, site-specific, and often requires professional judgment, field inspection, and liability-sensitive assessments, which slows adoption. In Sweden, some larger private employers and consultancy firms likely experiment with LLMs and analytics tools, but public-sector and compliance-oriented roles tend to adopt more cautiously. Overall, this looks like early adoption with some experimentation and niche tooling, not a widely embedded AI workflow.

descriptionEnglish: Check compliance with laws and regulations on environmental, health and animal protection, and foodstuffs. Carry out investigations, inspections and measurements. forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be little competition for jobs for environmental and health protection inspectors over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have great opportunities for work, but the labor market can vary depending on specialization within the occupation.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely knowledge-based and document-driven: interpreting regulations, reviewing compliance, writing investigation reports, making assessments, and drafting improvement proposals. scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption signals for environmental and health protection inspectors in Sweden appear to be in the early-adoption range rather than mainstream. However, there is limited evidence of occupation-specific, widely deployed AI systems in day-to-day field inspections or regulatory enforcement. The work is heavily tied to legal compliance, evidence handling, local public-sector workflows, and on-site judgement, which slows large-scale automation. In Sweden, public-sector adoption is improving, but for this occupation it still looks like selective experimentation and limited deployment rather than broad operational use.

nameEnglish: Environmental protection and environmental technology specialists
description: Undersöker miljön och utvecklar planer och lösningar för att skydda, bevara, återställa, minimera och förebygga skador på miljön. Undersöker, bedömer och rapporterar om byggnads- och konstruktionsprojekts påverkan på miljön. Inspekterar industriella och offentliga anläggningar och program för att utvärdera drifteffektivitet och garantera att miljöföreskrifterna följs.
descriptionEnglish: Examine the environment and develop plans and solutions to protect, preserve, restore, minimise and prevent damage to the environment. Examine, assess and report on the environmental impact of building and construction projects. Inspect industrial and public facilities and programmes to assess operational efficiency and ensure compliance with environmental regulations.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be little competition for jobs for specialists in environmental protection and environmental technology over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have great opportunities for work, but the labor market can vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that job opportunities for specialists in environmental protection and environmental technology will be good. Job opportunities within the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, licenses, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 3,400 working specialists in environmental protection and environmental technology in Sweden, of whom about 57 percent are women and 43 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely knowledge-based and screen-based, with heavy use of environmental data, regulations, reports, assessments, and planning. Core tasks such as evaluating environmental impacts, preparing compliance documentation, analyzing industrial/public facilities, and developing remediation or prevention plans are all areas where AI can strongly assist through document review, data analysis, drafting, and pattern detection. However, the role still includes site inspections, stakeholder interaction, and judgment in real-world regulatory contexts, which limits full automation. Overall, the job is highly exposed to AI, though not entirely replaceable.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption in Swedish environmental protection and environmental engineering appears to be in early-stage adoption rather than mainstream use. There are observable signals of AI being used for document analysis, environmental monitoring, remote sensing, GIS-based pattern detection, and parts of environmental reporting or compliance support, especially in larger consulting firms, utilities, and some public agencies. However, the occupation is still heavily grounded in site inspections, regulatory judgment, project assessment, and stakeholder communication, which limits end-to-end automation. In Sweden, public-sector environmental work tends to adopt new AI tools more slowly, and there is not yet strong evidence that AI is broadly embedded across this occupation the way it is in software, customer service, or analytics. Overall, the signal is real but uneven, suggesting early adoption with some employers experimenting and a minority using AI routinely.

nameSwedish: Designer inom spel och digitala medier
nameEnglish: Designers in games and digital media
description: Designar, formger och utvecklar digital och elektronisk media. Skapar specialeffekter, animering och andra audiovisuella rörliga bilder för dataspel, film, video m.m. Fastställer mål och restriktioner för designuppdraget genom samråd med kunder, intressenter och den egna organisationen.
descriptionEnglish: Design, create and develop digital and electronic media. Create special effects, animation and other audiovisual moving images for computer games, film, video, etc. Determine the objectives and constraints of the design assignment in consultation with clients, stakeholders and their own organisation.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for game and digital media designers and developers over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have limited to moderate opportunities for work, but the labor market can vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that job opportunities for game and digital media designers and developers will be very good. Job opportunities within the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, licenses, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: The assessment for game and digital media designers and developers is a combined assessment for game and digital media designers and game and digital media developers. There are about 6,500 working game and digital media designers and developers in Sweden, of whom about 21 percent are women and 79 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is predominantly digital and knowledge-based: it involves designing digital/electronic media, creating visual effects and animation, and defining design goals and constraints through consultation. These tasks map well to AI capabilities such as image generation, motion graphics assistance, prototyping, idea exploration, and content variation. However, the role also requires client/stakeholder communication, creative judgment, and iterative collaboration, which limit full automation. In Sweden, strong labor protections and workplace agreements may slow adoption, but the core task structure is still highly exposed to AI assistance.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
There are clear current adoption signals for AI in game and digital media design, but they are not yet universally embedded across the occupation. In practice, many Swedish employers and teams in games, animation, motion graphics, and digital content are already using generative AI and AI-assisted tools for concept ideation, image/video generation, texture and asset creation, upscaling, rotoscoping, storyboard support, and rapid prototyping. These tools are increasingly present in mainstream creative software and workflows, so adoption is observable rather than merely theoretical.

At the same time, usage is uneven and often limited by quality control, IP/copyright concerns, brand consistency, and the need for human artistic direction. Many studios still treat AI as an assistive layer rather than a core production method, and smaller employers may experiment without formal rollout. That places the occupation above early experimentation but below fully mature, standardized adoption.

descriptionEnglish:
Design and create printed publications and media. Determine the objectives and constraints of the design assignment in consultation with clients and stakeholders.

Excludes
Information officer (2432).

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for graphic designers over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have limited to moderate opportunities for work, but the labor market can vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that job opportunities for graphic designers will be limited to moderate. Job opportunities within the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, licenses, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 7,700 working graphic designers in Sweden, of whom about 47 percent are women and 53 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely digital and knowledge-based. Graphic designers primarily create visual concepts for printed publications and media, define design goals and constraints, and iterate on layouts and visual communication with clients and stakeholders. These tasks map well to AI capabilities such as generating draft layouts, image concepts, typography suggestions, editing, and rapid variation of design ideas. However, human judgment remains important for brand alignment, creative direction, client communication, and final approval, so the role is not fully automatable. In Sweden, adoption may be slowed by collective agreements and established workflows, but the core task structure is still highly exposed to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for graphic designers is significant and clearly observable by early 2026, but not yet universal. Generative design tools are now widely embedded in common workflows for layout ideation, image creation, background removal, retouching, resizing, copy-assisted mockups, and rapid variant generation. Major software used in this occupation has added AI features that many designers actively use, including Adobe's AI-powered tools, Canva's AI features, and similar functions in Figma and other design platforms. In Swedish employers, especially agencies, marketing teams, e-commerce firms, and in-house brand departments, AI is increasingly part of day-to-day production rather than just experimentation. However, the occupation still requires substantial human judgment, brand alignment, client interaction, and final visual decision-making, so AI is augmenting rather than replacing the core work. This places the occupation in the 6–7 range rather than the highest adoption band.

description: Utreder, analyserar, designar och utvecklar produkter för tillverkning. Fastställer mål och restriktioner för designuppdraget i samråd med kunder och intressenter. Samordnar tekniska, ekonomiska och estetiska förutsättningar. Förbereder design och specifikationer för tillverkning.
descriptionEnglish: Investigate, analyse, design and develop products for manufacture. Determine the objectives and constraints of the design assignment in consultation with clients and stakeholders. Coordinate technical, economic and aesthetic requirements. Prepare designs and specifications for manufacture.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Industrial designers primarily perform cognitive and digital tasks: researching user needs, analyzing constraints, creating concepts, producing specifications, and coordinating technical, economic, and aesthetic requirements. These activities are highly amenable to AI support through idea generation, rapid prototyping, image synthesis, requirements analysis, simulation, and drafting design documentation. However, the role also involves substantial human collaboration with clients and stakeholders, iterative judgment, and validation against manufacturability and brand constraints, which limits full automation. In Sweden, adoption may be somewhat slowed by collective agreements and established workflows, but the core task structure remains strongly exposed to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Industrial designers already have clear, observable AI adoption signals in current practice. Common tools such as generative design, AI-assisted concept sketching, image generation, CAD/CAE assistants, and rapid rendering/variation tools are being used by design teams to explore form, iterate faster, and support specification work. In Sweden and across Europe, employers in product development, consumer goods, automotive, and industrial equipment increasingly mention AI-enabled design workflows, though not yet as a universal standard. Adoption is strongest in ideation, visualization, and early-stage concept development, while final design decisions, engineering constraints, and manufacturability remain largely human-led. This places the occupation in significant but not full mainstream adoption, consistent with a 6 rather than 8–10.

description:
Handlägger tekniska, ekonomiska och juridiska frågor i samband med fastighetsbildning. Svarar för värdering och registrering av fastigheter. Ansvarar för mätnings- och kartläggningsarbete samt för framställning och uppdatering av kartor. Analyserar flyg- och satellitbilder. Ger råd bl.a. vid fastighetsbildning, mark- och fastighetsvärdering samt vid samverkan mellan fastigheter.

Hit hör inte
Kartingenjör (3117)
Mätningsingenjör/MBK-ingenjör (3112)
descriptionEnglish:
Handle technical, economic and legal matters relating to property formation. Are responsible for valuation and registration of properties. Are responsible for surveying and mapping work and for producing and updating maps. Analyse aerial and satellite images. Provide advice on, among other things, property formation, land and property valuation, and cooperation between properties.

Excludes
Mapping engineer (3117)
Surveying engineer / MAPPING engineer (3112).

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The occupation is largely knowledge- and information-based. Core tasks such as handling legal and technical issues in property formation, valuation and registration, map production and updating, and analysis of aerial/satellite imagery are highly digital and data-driven, which AI can meaningfully assist with through document review, geospatial analysis, drafting, and decision support. There are still important human and legal components, including exercising professional judgment, advising stakeholders, and ensuring compliance with Swedish cadastral and property law, plus some field-related surveying work. Overall, the role is strongly exposed to AI, but not fully automatable because of legal responsibility, context-specific judgment, and occasional physical/site work.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are clear but still uneven current AI adoption signals for surveyors/land registration professionals in Sweden. Observable use cases include AI-assisted interpretation of aerial and satellite imagery, automated map updating, geospatial feature extraction, document drafting for property-related case handling, and decision support in valuation and cadastral workflows. These tools are increasingly available in GIS/CAD and geospatial platforms, and some Swedish municipalities, consultancies, and state-related organizations are experimenting with them. However, for this occupation the core work is tightly regulated, legally sensitive, and often public-sector bound, which slows broad deployment. Current use appears more like selective pilot or partial workflow support than mainstream end-to-end adoption. That places the occupation in early adoption rather than significant widespread use.

description: Upprättar, utarbetar och granskar planer för nya stadsdelar, bostadsområden, industriområden samt översiktliga planer och program för utveckling av fysisk miljö. Planerar för förnyelse och förändring av befintliga områden. Väger samman funktionella, tekniska, ekonomiska, miljötekniska och estetiska krav och önskemål till en fungerande helhetslösning i dialog med uppdragsgivare och myndigheter. Utarbetar förslag i form av modeller, texter och ritningar.
descriptionEnglish: Prepare, develop and review plans for new districts, housing areas, industrial areas, as well as strategic plans and programmes for the development of the built environment. Plan the renewal and change of existing areas. Combine functional, technical, economic, environmental-technical and aesthetic requirements and wishes into a workable overall solution in dialogue with clients and authorities. Develop proposals in the form of models, text and drawings.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for urban planners and planning architects over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have limited to moderate opportunities for work, but the labor market can vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that job opportunities for urban planners and planning architects will be limited to moderate. Job opportunities within the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, licenses, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: The assessment for urban planners and planning architects is a combined assessment for urban planners and planning architects. There are about 3,300 working urban planners and planning architects in Sweden, of whom about 59 percent are women and 41 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is highly knowledge- and screen-based. The core work involves preparing, reviewing, and coordinating urban and land-use plans, weighing technical, economic, environmental, and aesthetic requirements, and producing proposals as models, texts, and drawings. These are tasks where AI can strongly assist with drafting, visualization, scenario analysis, document review, and comparison of planning alternatives. There is still an important interpersonal and institutional component in dialogue with clients and authorities, as well as judgment tied to local context, regulations, and political processes, which limits full automation. Overall, the role is highly exposed to AI, though not fully replaceable because of negotiation, responsibility, and context-sensitive decision-making.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption signals for planning architects in Sweden appear to be in the early-adoption range. There are observable uses of generative AI and design-support tools for producing concept text, report drafts, image/mood-board generation, GIS/data analysis, and faster iteration in urban planning and architectural workflows. However, these tools are not yet broadly embedded as standard practice across employers, especially in municipal and public-sector planning where procurement, accountability, and regulatory review slow deployment. The occupation still relies heavily on expert judgement, stakeholder dialogue, site-specific constraints, and legally defensible documentation, which limits full automation. Overall, the evidence suggests experimentation and selective use rather than mature, widespread adoption.

description: Utformar och projekterar yttre miljö t.ex. torg, gator, bostadsområden, parker, lekplatser och trädgårdar. Väger samman naturresursbevarande aspekter med funktionella, tekniska, ekonomiska och estetiska krav och önskemål till en fungerande helhetslösning i dialog med uppdragsgivare. Gör växt- och materialsammanställningar. Utarbetar förslag i form av modeller, texter och ritningar.
descriptionEnglish: Design and plan outdoor environments such as squares, streets, residential areas, parks, playgrounds and gardens. Combine natural resource conservation aspects with functional, technical, economic and aesthetic requirements and wishes into a workable overall solution in dialogue with the client. Prepare plant and materials schedules. Develop proposals in the form of models, text and drawings.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Landscape architects do mostly knowledge-based and screen-based work: analysis of sites, regulatory constraints, design alternatives, technical drawings, plant and material specifications, and written proposals. AI can strongly assist with concept generation, drafting, visualization, GIS/site analysis, cost and material comparisons, and documentation. However, the role still requires substantial human judgment, client dialogue, coordination with engineers and authorities, and adaptation to local physical and legal context, so exposure is high but not near-total.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Landscape architects in Sweden show early-but-real AI adoption signals, but not yet mainstream deployment. Observable current use is strongest in adjacent workflows: generative image tools and concept boards for early design exploration, AI-assisted drafting/summarization for proposals and meeting notes, image enhancement for presentation materials, and some BIM/CAD ecosystem features that automate routine drawing or layout tasks. Larger firms and multidisciplinary consultancies are more likely to experiment with these tools, especially in concept phases and client communication. However, the core profession still relies heavily on site-specific judgment, regulatory knowledge, plant/material selection, environmental coordination, and human-led design decisions, which limits full automation. There is no strong evidence that AI is a standard, widely embedded production tool across the occupation in Sweden yet, and public-sector/project-procurement constraints likely slow adoption. Overall this fits early adoption with some employers experimenting rather than broad mainstream use.

descriptionEnglish:
Design, plan and draw new-build buildings as well as alterations and extensions. Design plans and supporting materials for plans. Combine functional, technical, economic, environmental-technical and aesthetic requirements and wishes into a workable overall solution in dialogue with clients. Design internal environments such as private homes, public premises and offices. Prepare materials schedules. Develop proposals in the form of models, text and drawings.

Excludes
Interior decorators (3432)
Decorators (3432).

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for architects over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have limited to moderate opportunities for work, but the labor market can vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that job opportunities for architects will be good. Job opportunities within the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, licenses, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 7,000 working architects in Sweden, of whom about 54 percent are women and 46 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Architects’ work is predominantly cognitive and digital: designing buildings and interiors, producing plans, models, specifications, texts, and drawings, and balancing technical, economic, environmental, and aesthetic requirements. These tasks are highly amenable to AI support in drafting, generative design, code/checklist analysis, visualization, and documentation. However, the occupation also requires client dialogue, project coordination, negotiation, and context-specific judgment tied to regulations, site conditions, and stakeholder needs, which keeps it from the very highest exposure level.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
There are clear current adoption signals for architects, but not yet universal or fully transformed practice. In Sweden and internationally, architects commonly use AI-enabled features in BIM/CAD and design software for concept generation, rendering, image enhancement, clash detection, space planning, and rapid iteration of alternatives. Generative design and AI-assisted visualization are now commercially available and increasingly embedded in workflows, especially in larger firms and among tech-forward practices. Job ads and industry discussions also increasingly mention AI tools for design support, presentation, and documentation.

However, the occupation is still heavily constrained by client interaction, regulatory compliance, site-specific judgment, and the need for accountable human decision-making. Much of the work remains project-based and bespoke, so AI is more of an assistive layer than a core production engine. Adoption is therefore significant but uneven: some employers actively use these tools, while many others are still experimenting or using them indirectly through standard software features rather than dedicated AI workflows.

Overall, this fits early-to-mid mainstream adoption rather than saturation.

nameEnglish: Civil engineers in logistics and production planning
description:
Utvecklar, leder, planerar och kontrollerar material- och informationsflöden och produktionsplanering. Styr tillverkningsverksamhet och bedömer kostnadseffektivitet. Beslutar tillsammans med marknadsorganisation och kund om tidsplan och produktionsvolym. Planerar och beslutar om leverantörs- och transportkedjan, samt anläggnings- och systemplaner.

Hit hör inte
Yrken inom logistik och produktionsplanering med arbetsuppgifter motsvarande ingenjör- eller teknikernivå (3111)
descriptionEnglish:
Develop, lead, plan and control material and information flows and production planning. Manage manufacturing operations and assess cost-effectiveness. Decide, together with marketing and the customer, on schedules and production volumes. Plan and decide on supplier and transport chains, as well as plant and systems plans.

Excludes
Occupations in logistics and production planning with duties corresponding to engineer or technician level (3111).

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be little competition for jobs for civil engineers in logistics and production planning over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have great opportunities for work, but the labor market can vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that job opportunities for civil engineers in logistics and production planning will be good. Job opportunities within the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, licenses, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 5,000 working civil engineers in logistics and production planning in Sweden, of whom about 36 percent are women and 64 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is heavily focused on planning, coordination, analysis, and decision-making around material flows, production schedules, supplier chains, transport, and cost efficiency. These are primarily digital and knowledge-based tasks that AI systems can strongly support through forecasting, optimization, scenario analysis, and decision support. The role also involves communication with markets, customers, and internal stakeholders, but the core work is still mostly screen-based and analytical rather than physical. In Sweden, implementation may be slowed by organizational routines and collective agreements, but the underlying task structure is highly exposed to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are clear current adoption signals for AI in logistics and production planning, but not yet universal or deeply embedded enough to justify an 8–10. In 2026, many Swedish manufacturers, retailers, and logistics-heavy employers are using AI-enabled tools for demand forecasting, production scheduling, inventory optimization, route/transport planning, anomaly detection, and decision support in ERP/SCM systems. These are practical, observable deployments rather than speculative use cases. However, adoption is still uneven across firms: larger industrial and supply-chain organizations are ahead, while many medium and smaller employers rely on conventional planning software with limited AI features. The occupation also involves cross-functional judgment, cost tradeoffs, and coordination with customers and suppliers, which means AI is mostly augmenting planning rather than fully automating it. Overall this points to significant but not yet ubiquitous adoption, best placed at 6.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely cognitive and digital: it involves developing methods, products, materials, and technical solutions; planning and leading design and construction work; handling inspections, quality assurance, and documentation. These tasks are highly exposed to AI for analysis, drafting, optimization, simulation support, code/checklist generation, and project coordination. However, exposure is not maximal because the role also includes site-related judgment, inspections, compliance responsibility, and coordination with stakeholders, which still require human accountability and some physical presence. In Sweden, adoption may be moderated by regulations, professional standards, and collective agreements, but the core work remains strongly knowledge-based and therefore highly AI-exposed.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For Swedish civil engineering roles in building and infrastructure, there are clear current AI adoption signals, but they are still mostly at the early-adoption stage rather than mainstream. Observable use includes AI-assisted BIM and design review, document drafting and checking, tender/procurement support, schedule and risk analysis, and quality/control workflows in larger consulting firms and contractors. Swedish employers in construction and infrastructure increasingly mention digitalization, BIM, automation, and AI-related tools in job ads, but these signals are far less widespread than in software, customer service, or analytics-heavy occupations. In practice, most AI use appears to be targeted point solutions or experimentation by larger firms, while many project teams still rely on conventional engineering software and manual review. Public-sector and municipal infrastructure actors also tend to adopt more slowly. Overall, this supports a mid-level score indicating early but real adoption, not yet broad saturation.

description:
Utvecklar teorier, modeller och metoder inom tillverkningsindustri t.ex. maskin- och fordonsteknik. Leder projekt-, projekterings- och konstruktionsarbete som avser fordon, komponenter, maskiner och annan mekanisk utrustning samt värme-, vatten-, sanitets-, ventilations-, och kylsystem. Ansvarar för besiktning och kontroller. Utarbetar riktlinjer och metoder för kvalitetssäkring.

Hit hör inte
Yrken inom maskinteknik med arbetsuppgifter motsvarande ingenjör- eller teknikernivå (3114)
descriptionEnglish:
Develop theories, models and methods within manufacturing industries, such as mechanical engineering and automotive engineering. Lead project, design and construction work relating to vehicles, components, machinery and other mechanical equipment, as well as heating, water, sanitation, ventilation and refrigeration systems. Are responsible for inspections and checks. Draw up guidelines and methods for quality assurance.

Excludes
Occupations in mechanical engineering with duties corresponding to engineer or technician level (3114).

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be little competition for jobs for civil engineers in mechanical engineering over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have great opportunities for work, but the labor market can vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that job opportunities for civil engineers in mechanical engineering will be good. Job opportunities within the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, licenses, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 17,900 working civil engineers in mechanical engineering in Sweden, of whom about 24 percent are women and 76 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is heavily centered on analytical, design, documentation, and project coordination work: developing theories, models, and methods; leading engineering and construction projects; preparing guidelines for quality assurance; and handling inspection/control tasks. These are largely screen- and knowledge-based activities where AI can strongly assist with calculations, simulation, drafting, specification writing, standards checking, and documentation review. There are still important non-automatable components, such as responsibility for technical decisions, stakeholder coordination, and some on-site inspection and control work, which keeps exposure below the very highest range. Overall, the core task structure is highly digitally mediated and therefore strongly exposed to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
Current adoption for mechanical engineering civil engineers in Sweden appears to be in the significant-tooling / early-mainstream range. There are clear observable signals that AI is being used in practice, especially for CAD/CAE support, simulation, generative design exploration, predictive maintenance, requirements analysis, document drafting, and project coordination. Major engineering software ecosystems commonly used by Swedish firms now include AI-assisted features, and many employers are starting to reference AI, automation, and data-driven engineering in job ads and digital transformation initiatives.

However, this is not yet a fully saturated occupation. Much of the core work remains tied to physical systems, safety certification, regulatory compliance, and hands-on verification, which slows direct AI replacement or deep workflow integration. Adoption is uneven across employers: larger industrial firms and consultancies are more active, while smaller engineering offices and public/municipal organizations tend to move more slowly. So the strongest current signal is broad experimentation plus meaningful adoption in certain toolchains, rather than universal daily reliance.

nameEnglish: Civil engineers in chemistry and chemical engineering
description:
Utvecklar metoder och produkter inom kemi, kemiteknik, livsmedelsteknik m.m. Svarar för produktionsprocess och teknisk utveckling vid tillverkning av petroleumprodukter, kemikalier, kemiska och kemitekniska produkter, läkemedel, syntetiska material eller livsmedel. Ansvarar för kvalitets-, frisläpps- och slutkontroll av kemitekniska produkter.
 
Hit hör inte
- Yrken inom kemi och kemiteknik med arbetsuppgifter motsvarande ingenjör- eller teknikernivå (3115)
descriptionEnglish:
Develop methods and products within chemistry, chemical engineering, food technology, etc. Are responsible for production processes and technical development in the manufacture of petroleum products, chemicals, chemical engineering products, pharmaceuticals, synthetic materials or foodstuffs. Are responsible for quality control, release control and final inspection of chemical engineering products.

Excludes
Occupations in chemistry and chemical engineering with duties corresponding to engineer or technician level (3115).

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for civil engineers in chemistry and chemical engineering over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have limited to moderate opportunities for work, but the labor market can vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that job opportunities for civil engineers in chemistry and chemical engineering will be limited to moderate. Job opportunities within the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, licenses, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 3,900 working civil engineers in chemistry and chemical engineering in Sweden, of whom about 45 percent are women and 55 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely cognitive and knowledge-based. The core tasks involve developing methods and products, supervising production processes, performing technical development, and handling quality/release/final control in chemical and food-related manufacturing. These activities rely heavily on analysis, documentation, data interpretation, process design, compliance work, and decision-making—areas where AI can strongly assist with simulations, optimization, report drafting, quality analytics, and troubleshooting. However, the role is not fully screen-based: it also involves real-world production environments, safety, regulatory responsibility, laboratory or plant oversight, and coordination with other professionals. Swedish collective agreements and compliance requirements may slow adoption, but the task structure is still highly exposed to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for chemical and chemical engineering civil engineers in Sweden appears to be in the early-adoption stage. There are observable signals of AI use in adjacent functions such as process optimization, predictive maintenance, quality control, laboratory analytics, formulation work, and document drafting, but there is not yet strong evidence that AI is deeply embedded as a standard tool across the occupation. In practice, many employers are likely experimenting with AI-enabled analytics and simulation tools, while core responsibilities in production, compliance, release control, and technical process development still rely heavily on domain expertise, regulated workflows, and validated software rather than broad generative AI deployment. Compared with software, marketing, or customer service roles, adoption is clearly more limited and less standardized. Overall, the current signal is real but not mainstream, so a 5 is appropriate.

Utvecklar metoder och produkter inom gruvteknik och metallurgi. Svarar för produktion och teknisk utveckling vid utvinning av metaller, olja och gas eller vid framställning av metaller, legeringar och metallurgiska produkter. Ansvarar för kvalitets- och produktkontroller.

Hit hör inte
Yrken inom gruvteknik och metallurgi med arbetsuppgifter motsvarande ingenjör- eller teknikernivå (3116)
descriptionEnglish: Develop methods and products in mining engineering and metallurgy. Are responsible for production and technical development in the extraction of metals, oil and gas, or in the production of metals, alloys and metallurgical products. Are responsible for quality and product control.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is predominantly engineering and technical knowledge work, with substantial time spent on analysis, design, process optimization, quality control, and documentation in mining, metallurgy, and extraction-related production. These core tasks are highly amenable to AI support through data analysis, simulation, reporting, predictive maintenance, and process optimization. At the same time, the role still includes significant real-world constraints: coordination with plant operations, field and production environments, safety-critical decisions, and hands-on testing that limit full automation. In Sweden, adoption may also be moderated by collective agreements and established industrial processes, but the task structure remains strongly cognitively oriented and therefore fairly exposed to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
Current AI adoption in mining engineering and metallurgy is visible but still mostly early-stage and uneven. There are clear signals of use in adjacent operational domains: predictive maintenance, process optimization, quality control, computer vision for inspection, and digital twins in mines and smelters. Large industrial players and equipment vendors in Sweden and the Nordics increasingly market AI-enabled production analytics, and engineers in this occupation are likely to encounter such tools in plant optimization, ore sorting, process control, and anomaly detection.

However, the occupation is not yet one with broad, routine day-to-day AI tooling comparable to software development or customer support. Most deployments are embedded in production systems, vendor platforms, or specialized analytics teams rather than being universally used by all civil engineers in mining/metallurgy. Adoption is therefore real and growing, but not yet mainstream across the whole occupation. This fits an early-adoption rating around the middle of the scale.

descriptionEnglish: Conduct experiments and give advice and information on forestry, hunting and conservation. Take part in product development, marketing and sales. Survey wildlife stocks and assess forests, and draw up forest management plans, harvesting plans and the like. Carry out professional hunting of wild animals and pests.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation combines substantial knowledge-based work with some fieldwork. Core tasks such as preparing forest management plans, valuing forest assets, analyzing game populations, advising on forestry and nature conservation, and supporting product development/marketing are highly data- and document-intensive and therefore quite exposed to AI tools. However, the role also includes outdoor inventory work, practical hunting, and site-specific judgment in real environments, which AI cannot fully replace. Overall, the task mix is predominantly cognitive/digital but still meaningfully grounded in physical field conditions, so the exposure is high but not near-maximal.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption signals for forestry specialists and advisors in Sweden appear marginal to early, not mainstream. There are some observable use cases where AI can support remote sensing, forest inventory, wildlife monitoring, route/planning optimization, and image-based damage assessment, but these are usually embedded in GIS, drone, satellite, and decision-support workflows rather than clearly adopted as standalone AI tools in day-to-day advisory work. Swedish forestry organizations and related vendors do show experimentation with data-driven analytics and automated imagery analysis, yet there is little evidence of broad, occupation-specific deployment across employers for tasks like forest planning, hunting/vildlife estimation, or conservation advice. Overall, the occupation shows some pilots and niche tooling, but adoption is not yet widespread enough for a higher score.

description: Utvecklar teorier, modeller och metoder inom farmakologi. descriptionEnglish: Develop theories, models and methods within pharmacology. Overall: some real deployment and experimentation, but not yet widespread enough to score as significant mainstream adoption.

description: Utvecklar och tillämpar statistiska teorier, modeller och metoder. Planerar, utvecklar, bearbetar, framställer, analyserar och presenterar statistiska undersökningar. Gör prognoser. Bearbetar administrativa uppgifter till statistik.
descriptionEnglish: Develop and apply statistical theories, models and methods. Plan, develop, process, produce, analyse and present statistical surveys. Produce forecasts. Convert administrative data into statistics.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is highly screen- and knowledge-based. Core tasks include developing statistical theories and models, planning and conducting statistical analyses, processing administrative data into statistics, forecasting, and presenting results. These are precisely the kinds of structured cognitive and data-centric tasks that AI can strongly assist with or partially automate, especially in data cleaning, analysis, modeling, and report drafting. The role still requires human judgment, methodological choice, and quality control, but the task structure is very exposed to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for statisticians in Sweden appears significant but not yet fully mature. There are clear observable signals of active use: statisticians commonly work with data cleaning, forecasting, anomaly detection, survey analysis, reporting, and dashboarding tasks that are now frequently assisted by LLMs and other AI-enabled analytics tools in tools such as Excel, Power BI, Python/R environments, and statistical platforms. In many employers, especially larger private-sector organizations, AI is already being used to speed up exploratory analysis, automate routine coding, generate summaries, support forecasting, and draft methodological documentation. Swedish job ads and workplace tool mentions also suggest growing demand for AI-supported analytical work. However, adoption is still uneven, with public-sector and regulated environments moving more slowly, and AI is generally an assistant rather than a replacement for core statistical judgment. So the best fit is a strong mid-to-high score: meaningful current adoption, but not yet universal or deeply embedded across all employers.

Job ads and employer communication in this field occasionally mention data science, machine learning, or automated interpretation, but not at the frequency seen in more AI-mature occupations like software development or customer support. Public-sector and research-heavy employers also tend to adopt more cautiously, which limits visible penetration. Utvecklar teorier, modeller och metoder samt utformar praktiska tillämpningar inom kemi. Leder experiment, analyserar och värderar data i laboratoriemiljö. Informerar, ger råd samt utfärdar anvisningar och tillstånd. Utvecklar och provar kemiska produkter och processer. Tar fram dokumentation och handlägger registreringsärenden.

Farmakolog (2133)
Kemiingenjör (2145)
Laboratorieingenjör, kemi (3215)
Toxikolog (2131)
descriptionEnglish: Develop theories, models and methods and design practical applications within chemistry. Lead experiments, analyse and evaluate data in a laboratory setting. Provide information, advice and issue instructions and permits. Develop and test chemical products and processes. Produce documentation and handle registration cases.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be competition for jobs for chemists over the next year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have limited to moderate opportunities for work, but the labor market can vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that job opportunities for chemists will be good. Job opportunities within the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, licenses, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: There are about 3,200 working chemists in Sweden, of whom about 54 percent are women and 46 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Chemists mainly perform knowledge-intensive work: developing theories and methods, designing experiments, analyzing laboratory data, preparing documentation, and handling registration and regulatory cases. These tasks are highly amenable to AI support for literature review, hypothesis generation, data analysis, drafting reports, compliance checks, and decision support. However, the occupation also includes hands-on laboratory experimentation, product/process testing, and direct responsibility for informing, advising, and issuing instructions or permits, which require human oversight and some physical or institutional context. Overall, the role is mostly cognitive/digital with meaningful lab-based and regulatory components, so exposure is high but not near-total.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
For chemists in Sweden, current AI adoption signals are visible but still mostly early-stage rather than deeply embedded in day-to-day work. In practice, AI is being used in adjacent ways such as literature search, patent and regulatory document review, data analysis support, spectroscopy/chromatography pattern recognition, and basic lab automation/software-assisted experimentation. Some employers in pharma, materials, and industrial R&D are likely experimenting with machine learning for formulation, process optimization, and predictive modeling.

However, the occupation is not yet strongly characterized by standardized AI tooling in the way software development or customer support is. Much of a chemist’s work still depends on physical experiments, domain judgment, compliance documentation, and regulated procedures, which limits easy AI substitution and slows broad deployment. Public-sector and regulatory chemistry roles are likely even more conservative.

Overall, the signal is best described as early adoption with some real use in selected employers and tasks, but not yet mainstream across the occupation.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Meteorologists primarily perform data-intensive, screen-based work: collecting, processing, and analyzing atmospheric observations, interpreting models, and producing weather forecasts and climate analyses. Some Swedish employers and adjacent public-sector actors likely use AI-enabled forecasting tools within larger forecasting platforms, and data-heavy analysis tasks are a natural fit for model-assisted workflows. However, the occupation is still dominated by established numerical weather prediction systems, human judgment, and regulated public-service operations, especially within Swedish agencies where adoption is typically slower. The current signal is therefore stronger than mere pilot-stage talk, but not strong enough to rate as broadly deployed mainstream AI tooling across the occupation.

description: Utvecklar teorier, modeller och metoder som rör materia, rum, tid, energi, krafter och fält samt samverkan mellan dessa fenomen. Utformar praktiska tillämpningar inom fysik, astronomi, och radiofysik. Studerar fenomen, leder experiment, analyserar och värderar data. Utför beräkningar, informerar, inspekterar, samt utfärdar anvisningar och tillstånd.
descriptionEnglish: Develop theories, models and methods relating to matter, space, time, energy, forces and fields, and the interaction between these phenomena. Design practical applications within physics, astronomy and radio physics. Study phenomena, lead experiments, analyse and evaluate data. Carry out calculations, provide information, inspect, and issue instructions and permits.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The occupation is highly knowledge- and analysis-intensive. Core tasks include developing theories and models, designing methods, analyzing data, performing calculations, and producing technical advice or decisions. These are all activities where AI can strongly assist with literature review, modeling, simulation, data analysis, hypothesis generation, and drafting reports. However, the role also involves leading experiments, inspecting phenomena and systems, and issuing instructions or permits, which add some real-world, expert-judgment and institutional components that are not fully automatable. Overall, the job is mostly cognitive and digital, with only limited physical or interpersonal elements, so exposure is high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are clear but still limited current adoption signals for physicists and astronomers in Sweden. In research universities, observatories, and applied physics settings, AI tools are increasingly used for data analysis, image/signal processing, literature search, simulation support, and drafting code or reports. This is especially visible in astronomy, where machine learning is already used for classification, anomaly detection, telescope data reduction, and handling large survey datasets. However, the occupation is still centered on domain-specific scientific judgment, experiments, modeling, and interpretation, where AI is supportive rather than core. Swedish employers in this field do not yet show the kind of broad, standardized AI deployment seen in software development or customer service. Public-sector and research environments also adopt more cautiously. Overall this fits early adoption with some active experimentation and meaningful use in parts of the job, but not mainstream, occupation-wide integration.

scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For managers in elder care, there are clear but still limited current AI adoption signals. However, this occupation is in a highly regulated, human-centered public-service setting where adoption is slower than in private office work, and there is little evidence of widespread, routine AI use directly embedded in core management practice. Overall this looks like early adoption with some experimentation, but not mainstream deployment.

Svarar för budget samt fördelar resurserna inom verksamheten. Har personalansvar.
descriptionEnglish: Formulate and are responsible for objectives and strategies for operations in compulsory and upper secondary education and adult education and equivalent forms of schooling. Plan and are responsible for educational activities based on governing documents established by the Government, Parliament and education authorities. Set guidelines, monitor and evaluate operations against established objectives. Are responsible for budgets and allocate resources within the operation. Have staff responsibility.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely managerial, administrative, and knowledge-based: setting goals and strategies, interpreting regulations, planning education operations, budgeting, allocating resources, drafting guidelines, and monitoring/evaluating outcomes. These are tasks where AI can strongly assist through document drafting, data analysis, forecasting, compliance support, and decision preparation. However, the role also includes substantial human leadership, personnel responsibility, stakeholder coordination, and context-specific judgment within Swedish public-sector rules, which limits full automation. Overall, the job has high theoretical AI exposure but not near-total replaceability.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for school leaders in Sweden appears to be in the early-adoption range rather than mainstream. There are observable signals of use in adjacent administrative tasks such as drafting communications, summarizing policy documents, supporting meeting notes, and basic analytics for attendance, staffing, and budgeting. Some Swedish municipalities and school organizations have begun experimenting with generative AI and decision-support tools, and education-sector vendors are adding AI features to administrative platforms. However, for this occupation specifically, there is no strong evidence of widespread, routine AI use in core management work across employers. Public-sector procurement constraints, data protection concerns, and the sensitivity of school administration slow deployment. Overall, the signals suggest limited but real experimentation, with adoption concentrated in a subset of employers rather than being standard practice.

descriptionEnglish: Formulate and are responsible for objectives and strategies for operations in pre-school education or other childcare. Plan and are responsible for educational activities. Set guidelines, monitor and evaluate operations against established objectives. Ensure compliance with statutory regulations. Are responsible for budgets and allocate resources within the operation. Have staff responsibility.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is heavily centered on planning, administration, budgeting, compliance, documentation, and follow-up of pedagogical activities, all of which are knowledge-based tasks that AI can materially support. AI could assist with goal setting, policy drafting, scheduling, budget analysis, reporting, and regulatory checklists. However, the role also includes substantial human leadership, staff responsibility, interaction with children and parents, and context-dependent decision-making in a Swedish public-sector welfare setting, where statutory and relational duties limit delegation to AI. Overall, the job has a clear majority of digital/cognitive work, but not to the extent of being almost fully screen-based.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for preschool management roles in Sweden appears to be in the early-adoption phase rather than mainstream use. There are observable signals of AI-supported administration in the broader public sector, such as drafting assistance, meeting summaries, scheduling, document handling, and policy/quality follow-up, but these are not yet strongly embedded in preschool leadership work. For this occupation, the core tasks are leadership, compliance, budgeting, staff management, and pedagogical oversight, where AI can support reporting and information processing but does not directly replace the central responsibilities. Swedish municipalities and publicly run preschool organizations tend to adopt new AI tools cautiously because of privacy, child-related data sensitivity, procurement rules, and the need for human accountability. As a result, there are some real experiments and limited deployment signals, but not enough evidence of widespread, routine AI use in the occupation.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is primarily managerial and knowledge-based: setting goals and strategies, defining guidelines, monitoring and evaluating performance, budgeting, resource allocation, and personnel responsibility. scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current observable AI adoption for farm/forest estate managers in Sweden appears marginal rather than mainstream. The role is management-heavy and field- and asset-dependent, so work is still dominated by operational planning, personnel management, compliance, and budget control. As of early 2026, there are some pilots and selective deployments among larger agricultural and forestry enterprises, but limited evidence of widespread employer adoption specifically for this occupation.

Ansvarar för budget, kalkylering, upphandling, inköp, tidsplan samt kontakter med beställare och myndigheter. Utformar riktlinjer, följer upp och utvärderar verksamheten mot uppsatta mål. Har personalansvar.

Hit hör inte
Arbetsledare inom bygg, anläggning och gruva (3121)
descriptionEnglish: Formulate and are responsible for objectives and strategies for operations within construction, civil engineering and mining. Are responsible for budgets, costing, procurement, purchasing, schedules and contacts with clients and authorities. Set guidelines, monitor and evaluate operations against established objectives. Have staff responsibility.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is mainly managerial and knowledge-based: setting goals and strategies, handling budgets and calculations, procurement, scheduling, compliance, reporting, and coordination with clients and authorities. These are all tasks where AI can substantially assist with drafting, analysis, forecasting, document preparation, and decision support. However, the role also includes personnel responsibility, stakeholder relations, and site/business context that require human judgment, negotiation, and accountability, which keeps it from the very highest exposure range.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
There are clear current AI-adoption signals for this occupation, but they are mostly in adjacent planning/administrative workflows rather than as a standard, occupation-specific AI toolset. For construction, civil engineering, and mining operations managers, the strongest observable uses in Sweden are AI-assisted budgeting, forecasting, scheduling, document drafting, procurement support, tender analysis, and reporting. Larger contractors and infrastructure/mining firms are also adopting AI-enabled project control, risk detection, and data analytics platforms, especially where operations are asset-heavy and generate structured data.

However, adoption is still uneven and not yet mainstream across the occupation. Many employers continue to rely on traditional project management, ERP, and specialist software with only limited AI augmentation. The role is also highly site- and responsibility-driven, so core decision-making, stakeholder management, compliance, and personnel leadership remain human-led. Overall this points to early but real adoption: some employers are experimenting or using AI in parts of the workflow, but it is not yet a standard daily tool for most driftchefer in bygg, anläggning och gruva.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is highly knowledge-intensive and largely screen-based. The core tasks include setting R&D goals and strategy, defining guidelines, monitoring and evaluating performance, managing budgets, allocating resources, and making personnel decisions. These activities involve substantial information processing, report writing, planning, analysis, and decision support—areas where AI can strongly assist with drafting strategies, synthesizing research, tracking KPIs, forecasting budgets, and supporting managerial decisions. scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current adoption for R&D managers is best characterized as early-stage and uneven. In Sweden, these tools are increasingly visible in knowledge-work environments and vendor offerings, but for this occupation they are not yet a standard, deeply embedded management layer across most employers. The role is still heavily dependent on human judgment for strategy, budgeting, people leadership, and technical prioritization, so AI is more a support tool than a core workflow. The strongest observable evidence is experimentation and selective use in larger, more digitally mature firms, rather than broad, routine adoption across the occupation.

- 9–10: Almost entirely screen/knowledge-based; output is text, code, data, or decisions AI models excel at

- 5–6: Mixed — significant cognitive tasks that AI could assist with, significant physical/relational tasks it cannot
- 3–4: Mostly physical, hands-on, interpersonal, or highly context-dependent (Swedish welfare/care setting)
- 1–2: Almost entirely physical, outdoor, or dependent on real-world embodiment
- 0: No plausible AI impact on core task structure
