description: Formulerar och ansvarar för mål och strategier inom inköps-, logistik- och transportverksamhet. Utformar riktlinjer, följer upp och utvärderar verksamheten mot uppsatta mål för inköp, lagring, transporter och distribution. Deltar i förhandlingar med leverantörer, ansvarar för lagerhållning och lagerutnyttjande, svarar för lasthantering. Svarar för budget samt fördelar resurserna inom verksamheten. Har personalansvar.
descriptionEnglish: Formulate and are responsible for objectives and strategies within purchasing, logistics and transport operations. Set guidelines, monitor and evaluate operations against established objectives for purchasing, storage, transport and distribution. Take part in negotiations with suppliers, are responsible for stockholding and stock utilisation, and for cargo handling. Are responsible for budgets and allocate resources within the operation. Have staff responsibility.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely managerial and knowledge-based, with substantial work in planning, goal setting, policy development, budgeting, performance follow-up, procurement strategy, and negotiation preparation. These are tasks where AI can strongly assist through data analysis, forecasting, document drafting, scenario comparison, supplier intelligence, and decision support. The role also includes some interpersonal and organizational elements such as supplier negotiations and personnel responsibility, which AI cannot fully replace. There are few hands-on physical tasks, so the overall exposure is high, though actual adoption in Sweden may be moderated by labor agreements, established routines, and the need for human accountability.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for purchasing, logistics, and transport managers in Sweden looks like early but real adoption rather than mainstream deployment. There are clear observable signals: employers are increasingly advertising AI/automation capabilities in supply chain and logistics roles, and many firms use AI-enabled planning tools in forecasting, route optimization, warehouse management systems, procurement analytics, and document handling. However, for this occupation the core work is still heavily managerial, relational, and operational, and most employers appear to be augmenting rather than transforming the role with AI. Use is uneven across Swedish companies, with larger private-sector firms and international groups more likely to deploy AI-assisted demand forecasting, transport planning, inventory optimization, and supplier analysis, while smaller firms and public-sector-adjacent organizations lag. There is not yet strong evidence that AI tools are standard or deeply embedded across the occupation, so this fits the 4–5 range, leaning toward 5.

description: Formulerar och ansvarar för mål och strategier inom IT-verksamhet. Utformar riktlinjer, följer upp och utvärderar verksamheten mot uppsatta mål för IT-verksamheten. Ansvarar för anskaffning, utveckling, underhåll och användning av IT-system. Ansvarar för IT-säkerhet. Svarar för budget samt fördelar resurserna inom verksamheten. Har personalansvar.
descriptionEnglish: Formulate and are responsible for objectives and strategies within IT operations. Set guidelines, monitor and evaluate operations against established objectives for IT activities. Are responsible for procurement, development, maintenance and use of IT systems. Are responsible for IT security. Are responsible for budgets and allocate resources within the operation. Have staff responsibility.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: IT managers perform predominantly cognitive and digital work: setting IT strategy, drafting policies, monitoring performance, managing budgets, overseeing procurement, and making security and resource decisions. These tasks are highly exposed to AI support for analysis, reporting, planning, risk assessment, documentation, and decision support. However, the role also includes leadership, personnel responsibility, stakeholder coordination, and organizational judgment that are not fully automatable. In Sweden, implementation may be slowed by labor agreements and governance processes, but the core task structure is still strongly AI-exposed.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: IT managers in Sweden show clear current AI adoption signals, though not yet at the very top end because the role is managerial rather than a directly tool-centric production occupation. By early 2026, many Swedish employers expect IT leaders to oversee AI-enabled transformation, vendor selection, governance, cybersecurity, and productivity tooling across teams. Observable signals include frequent AI-related requirements in IT leadership job ads, active use of AI for strategy drafting, incident triage, documentation, policy work, and support for software and operations teams using copilots and other LLM-based tools. In large private-sector organizations, AI adoption in IT management is becoming normal in planning, budgeting, service management, and security oversight. However, adoption is still uneven across smaller firms and the public sector, where procurement, risk, and governance slow deployment. Overall this is stronger than early experimentation but still not as universally embedded as in hands-on developer roles, so a 7 is appropriate.

descriptionEnglish: Formulate and are responsible for objectives and strategies within other administrative and service operations in other support and specialist functions. Set guidelines, monitor and evaluate operations against established objectives. Are responsible for budgets and allocate resources within the operation. Have staff responsibility.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely managerial and administrative, with substantial time spent on planning, goal-setting, budgeting, policy formulation, follow-up, reporting, and resource allocation. These are screen- and knowledge-based tasks that AI can strongly assist with through drafting documents, analyzing performance data, forecasting budgets, preparing decision materials, and supporting HR and workflow management. However, the role also includes personnel responsibility, coordination, and leadership across service operations, which require human judgment, negotiation, accountability, and context-specific interaction that AI cannot fully replace. In Sweden, collective agreements and public-sector governance further slow direct substitution, but the task mix still indicates high theoretical AI exposure.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current adoption appears moderate and clearly observable for this occupation, but not yet near-universal. Managers in administration and service functions commonly use AI-enabled tools for drafting emails and reports, summarizing meetings, preparing budgets and follow-ups, and supporting HR/operational planning. In Sweden, many employers now mention AI literacy or use of generative AI in managerial and support roles, and these tools are increasingly embedded in office suites and workflow platforms. However, this occupation is broad, and actual deployment varies significantly by employer and sector. Large private organizations are more likely to have adopted such tools, while public-sector and smaller organizations remain more cautious due to governance, procurement, and data-security concerns. Overall, the evidence points to significant but uneven current use, fitting the 6–7 range.

Utformar riktlinjer, följer upp och utvärderar verksamheten mot uppsatta mål inom försäljning och marknad. Svarar för budget samt fördelar resurserna inom verksamheten. Har personalansvar.

descriptionEnglish: Formulate and are responsible for objectives and strategies for sales and marketing operations. Decide on frameworks for pricing, discounts and delivery terms, sales budgets, special campaigns and the like. Set guidelines, monitor and evaluate operations against established objectives within sales and marketing. Are responsible for budgets and allocate resources within the operation. Have staff responsibility.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is predominantly cognitive and management-oriented: setting sales and marketing goals, defining pricing and discount policies, budgeting, allocating resources, monitoring performance, and evaluating results. These are tasks where AI can strongly assist with forecasting, reporting, market analysis, campaign optimization, and decision support. There is also a human leadership component, including personnel responsibility, strategic judgment, and stakeholder coordination, which limits full automation. Overall, the role is highly screen- and knowledge-based, with substantial AI exposure but not complete replaceability.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
There are clear current adoption signals for sales and marketing managers in Sweden, but not yet universal or deeply standardized across employers. Common observable uses include AI-assisted campaign drafting, content generation, customer segmentation, lead scoring, CRM insights, sales forecasting, and rapid analysis of market and competitor data. Many Swedish organizations now mention AI in marketing and commercial leadership job ads, and managers are increasingly expected to use AI-enabled analytics and productivity tools.

However, the role is still primarily managerial and strategic rather than a narrowly AI-specialized occupation, so adoption varies widely by company size, sector, and digital maturity. Larger private-sector firms are more likely to use AI tools actively, while smaller firms and parts of the public sector lag. Overall this supports a significant but not yet near-ubiquitous adoption level.

nameEnglish: Information, communications and public relations managers
description: Formulerar och ansvarar för mål och strategier för informationsprogram och kampanjer samt förhandlar reklamkontrakt. Utformar riktlinjer, följer upp och utvärderar verksamhetens uppsatta mål inom information, reklam, kommunikation och PR. Svarar för budget samt fördelar resurserna inom verksamheten. Har personalansvar.
descriptionEnglish: Formulate and are responsible for objectives and strategies for information programmes and campaigns and negotiate advertising contracts. Set guidelines, monitor and evaluate the operation's objectives within information, advertising, communications and public relations. Are responsible for budgets and allocate resources within the operation. Have staff responsibility.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely cognitive, managerial, and screen-based. The core tasks involve setting communication strategies, evaluating campaigns, negotiating advertising contracts, managing budgets, allocating resources, and following up on goals in information, communication, advertising, and PR. These are activities where AI can strongly assist with drafting plans, analyzing campaign performance, generating content ideas, monitoring metrics, and supporting decision-making. However, the role also includes leadership, personnel responsibility, stakeholder negotiation, and organizational judgment, which require human oversight and interpersonal skills. In Sweden, collective agreements and organizational routines may slow adoption, but the work itself is highly exposed to AI support and partial automation.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
There are clear current adoption signals for AI in communications, PR, and marketing leadership roles, but it is not yet universally embedded. By early 2026, many Swedish employers have begun using generative AI for drafting press releases, campaign copy, social media content, stakeholder messaging, media monitoring, and rapid summarization of reports. AI is also increasingly used to analyze audience insights, sentiment, and campaign performance, which fits this occupation’s responsibilities around strategy, follow-up, and evaluation.

However, the role is still heavily managerial and judgment-based: budgeting, personnel responsibility, negotiation, and overall strategic direction remain primarily human-led. In Sweden, adoption is stronger in larger private-sector organizations and agencies than in the public sector, and usage is often assistive rather than fully workflow-integrated. So the observable signal is significant and growing, but not yet at the level of near-universal or deeply automated adoption.

descriptionEnglish: Formulate and are responsible for objectives for strategic planning and policy within administrative and planning activities within an organisation or company. Set guidelines, monitor and evaluate operations against established objectives. Have staff responsibility.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is primarily strategic, administrative, and knowledge-based: setting goals, developing policies and guidelines, monitoring operations against targets, and managing staff. These tasks are highly compatible with AI support through drafting, analysis, reporting, scenario planning, and performance monitoring. The role still involves substantial human judgment, organizational context, leadership, and accountability, which limits full automation, but the core work is largely digital and cognitive. In Sweden, collective agreements and public-sector governance may slow implementation, yet the task structure itself is strongly exposed to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
There are clear current AI adoption signals for strategic planning and management roles, but they look like early-to-mid adoption rather than mainstream deployment. In Swedish organizations, these managers increasingly encounter AI in business intelligence, forecasting, scenario analysis, policy drafting, meeting summarization, and internal decision-support workflows. Many employers now mention generative AI, automation, and analytics in leadership and planning contexts, and large firms and municipalities are beginning to pilot AI-assisted reporting and planning tools.

However, for this occupation the adoption is still uneven and not yet comparable to highly AI-embedded roles like software development or radiology. The role is broad, often tied to public administration and organizational governance, where procurement, accountability, and policy constraints slow deployment. Much of the current usage appears to be ad hoc productivity support rather than deeply integrated AI systems for planning or policy management.

Overall, this suggests moderate current adoption: real and visible, but still in an early phase for many employers.

description: Ansvarar för strategiska personalfrågor som personalplanering, rekrytering och personalutveckling. Ansvarar för lönepolicy och löneförhandlingar. Svarar för arbetsmiljö- och personalsociala frågor samt tolkning av arbetsrättsliga lagar och avtal. Utformar riktlinjer, följer upp och utvärderar verksamheten mot uppsatta mål.
descriptionEnglish: Are responsible for strategic staffing issues such as workforce planning, recruitment and staff development. Are responsible for pay policy and pay negotiations. Are responsible for working environment and employee welfare matters and for interpreting employment law and agreements. Set guidelines, monitor and evaluate operations against established objectives.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is heavily knowledge- and decision-intensive, with most core tasks involving strategic HR planning, recruitment, compensation policy, labor law interpretation, workplace policy design, follow-up, and evaluation. These are mostly screen-based, document-based, and analytical activities that AI can substantially assist with through drafting, summarizing, searching regulations and agreements, benchmarking pay, screening candidates, and preparing reports. At the same time, the role also requires significant human judgment, negotiation, leadership, and relationship management, especially in labor negotiations, organizational change, and employee matters. In Sweden, strong collective agreements and legal responsibilities also slow full automation. Overall, the job has high theoretical AI exposure but is not fully automatable.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
Current AI adoption in Swedish HR management appears to be in the early-adoption range rather than mainstream. Observable signals include widespread availability and growing use of AI for drafting job ads, screening CVs, summarizing employee survey feedback, generating policy drafts, and supporting internal HR chat/helpdesk functions. Some larger Swedish employers and HR software vendors have begun integrating generative AI features into recruitment, workforce planning, and HR case handling.

However, for senior HR managers specifically, adoption is still uneven and often limited to support tasks rather than core decision-making. Sensitive responsibilities such as labor law interpretation, wage negotiations, workplace issues, and strategic personnel decisions remain highly human-led due to legal risk, confidentiality, union relations, and trust requirements. Public-sector and unionized environments also slow deployment. Overall, there are clear current use signals, but not yet enough to classify this as broad, mature adoption.

descriptionEnglish: Are responsible for preparing budgets and for accounting and year-end financial statements. Check that the organisation's resources are used efficiently. Set guidelines, monitor and evaluate operations against established financial objectives. Report results to senior management or equivalent. Have staff responsibility.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely cognitive and screen-based: budgeting, accounting, financial control, reporting, evaluation against economic targets, and management reporting are all tasks where AI can provide strong assistance through analysis, forecasting, anomaly detection, drafting reports, and decision support. There is also a managerial and people-leadership component, including responsibility for staff and organizational judgment, which reduces full automation but does not change the fact that the core work is heavily information-driven. In Sweden, adoption may be moderated by collective agreements and governance requirements, but the underlying task structure is still highly exposed to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current adoption is clearly visible but not universal. For finance managers and controllers, AI is already being used for drafting management reports, summarizing financial data, variance analysis, budgeting support, and automating parts of accounting and close processes through ERP/accounting platforms. In Sweden, many medium and large employers are exposing finance leaders to AI-enabled BI tools, forecasting features, and workflow automation, and these uses are now common enough to count as significant tooling rather than isolated pilots. However, the role still depends heavily on accountability, judgement, and regulatory compliance, so end-to-end AI decisioning is not common. Adoption is therefore moderate-to-high: meaningful current use, but not yet fully mainstream across the occupation.

Formulate and decide on objectives and policies. Make decisions on work organisation and major investments. Represent the company in dealings with other companies, organisations and authorities. forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is heavily centered on strategic planning, policy formulation, decision-making, coordination, reporting, and other knowledge-intensive tasks that AI can substantially support. Executives spend much of their time reviewing information, comparing alternatives, drafting communications, monitoring performance, and preparing decisions for boards or owners—activities well suited to AI assistance. However, the role also requires high-level human judgment, accountability, negotiation, leadership, and representation of the company in external relations, which limits full automation. Overall, it is a highly exposed cognitive/managerial occupation, though not one that AI can replace end-to-end.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are clear current adoption signals for executive leadership roles, but they are more about decision support than automation. In Sweden, CEOs and senior executives increasingly use AI tools for board-material drafting, market and competitor analysis, scenario planning, KPI reporting, strategic communication, and meeting summarization. Many enterprise software suites now include embedded generative AI for presentations, email, document synthesis, forecasting, and business intelligence, and these tools are being actively piloted or used by management teams in larger Swedish firms. However, adoption is uneven and not universal: executive work is highly contextual, confidential, and relationship-driven, so AI is typically used as an assistant rather than a core operational tool. Compared with roles like customer service or software development, the evidence points to meaningful but not yet ubiquitous adoption, so a 6 is appropriate.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Politicians’ core tasks are highly knowledge- and decision-based: preparing policy positions, drafting and revising legislation, analyzing reports and statistics, writing speeches and proposals, and communicating decisions to stakeholders and the public. These activities are heavily screen-based and closely match what AI systems can assist with or partially automate. There are also important interpersonal, negotiation, party-political, and ceremonial elements that AI cannot replace, and formal decision-making remains human and legally accountable. In Sweden, statutory responsibility and democratic legitimacy strongly limit delegation of core authority, but the underlying work content is still very exposed to AI support.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption signals for politicians in Sweden appear to be in the early-adoption range rather than mainstream. However, these signals are fragmented and not yet clearly embedded as standard tooling across elected officials or parliamentary groups. Public-sector governance, confidentiality, accountability, and reputational concerns slow deployment, so current use is more pilot- and individual-driven than institutionalized. Overall, the occupation shows some real adoption signals, but not enough to reach significant or broad mainstream use.

Core tasks include planning, leading, coordinating, negotiations, advocacy, preparing information, responding to referrals/remiss and representing the organization in inquiries and meetings. These are mostly screen- and text-based activities where AI can strongly assist with drafting briefs, summarizing policy documents, preparing talking points, analyzing consultation responses, monitoring issues, and supporting negotiation strategy. However, the role also requires substantial human judgment, stakeholder management, trust-building, and real-world representation of members’ interests, which limits full automation. In Sweden, collective bargaining and institutional processes also slow adoption, but the core task structure remains strongly exposed to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for senior officers in interest organisations appears to be in early-stage experimentation rather than broad, standardized deployment. Observable signals in 2026 likely include use of general-purpose LLMs for drafting speeches, policy briefs, consultation responses, meeting summaries, stakeholder mapping, and internal communications, especially in organizations with limited staff capacity. However, there is no strong evidence of occupation-specific AI tools being widely embedded in core workflows such as negotiations, representation, or advocacy. In Sweden, some larger interest groups and trade associations may have introduced AI guidance or pilot use, but adoption is uneven and constrained by trust, confidentiality, political sensitivity, and the need for human judgment. Overall this fits early adoption: tools exist and some employers are experimenting, but it is not yet mainstream.

description: Leder, planerar och genomför militära operationer nationellt och internationellt. Leder militära förband.
descriptionEnglish: Lead, plan and carry out military operations nationally and internationally. Command military units.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The occupation is heavily centered on planning, coordinating, analyzing information, issuing orders, and making decisions about military operations, much of which is knowledge-based and screen-mediated. AI could plausibly support intelligence analysis, scenario planning, logistics, reporting, and decision support. However, the role also requires direct leadership of personnel, real-world command responsibility, and operating in dynamic, high-stakes field environments where embodied judgment and human authority remain essential. In Sweden, adoption may also be moderated by military procedures and institutional constraints, but the core task mix is still strongly exposed to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption signals for military officers in Sweden appear to be early and limited rather than mainstream. There are observable signs of AI use in adjacent defense and security functions, such as decision-support analytics, intelligence processing, logistics optimization, simulation, and planning tools, but these are not clearly widespread across the occupation itself. The role involves command, operational judgment, and sensitive classified information, which tends to slow deployment and restrict the use of public or generic AI tools. As of early 2026, the evidence suggests experimentation and selective use in some units or support contexts, but not broad, routine AI-assisted work across Swedish officers.

description: Deltar i planering och genomförande av militära operationer, nationellt och internationellt. Utbildar samt är specialist inom sitt tjänsteområde inom teknisk tjänst, sjöstrid, luftstrid, markstrid, ledning eller logistik. Leder lägre militära förband.
descriptionEnglish: Participate in the planning and conduct of military operations, nationally and internationally. Provide training and act as specialists within their area of service in technical support, naval warfare, air warfare, land warfare, command or logistics. Command lower-level military units.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Specialist officers in the Swedish Armed Forces combine substantial cognitive work with hands-on leadership and operational duties. They participate in planning and conducting military operations, prepare and deliver training, provide specialist expertise, and lead lower units. The planning, documentation, logistics, and training-design aspects are highly suitable for AI support, such as decision support, simulation, briefing preparation, and analysis of operational data. However, the role also requires real-time command, field presence, physical training, coordination under uncertain conditions, and interpersonal leadership that AI cannot replace. Overall, this is a mixed occupation with meaningful AI exposure but significant non-digital, context-dependent responsibilities.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for Swedish specialist officers appears to be in the early-adoption stage rather than mainstream. In defense and military operations, observable AI use is likely concentrated in planning support, logistics optimization, intelligence analysis, simulation, and decision-support tools, but there is little public evidence that AI is broadly embedded in day-to-day command roles or frontline leadership across Swedish units. The sector is also constrained by security, procurement, and certification requirements, which typically slow deployment relative to civilian occupations. As of early 2026, the most credible signal is that some employers and units are experimenting with AI-enabled tools, especially for support functions, but widespread operational adoption is not yet clearly visible.

description: Medverkar i militära operationer och övningar nationellt och internationellt med främst väpnad strid som huvuduppgift.
descriptionEnglish: Take part in military operations and exercises nationally and internationally, with armed combat as their main task.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is dominated by physical, embodied, and high-stakes real-world activity: armed combat, field exercises, patrols, and operations in dynamic environments. While AI may support planning, intelligence analysis, logistics, training simulations, or decision support, these are secondary to the core task structure. The main work cannot be automated or delegated to AI in any meaningful way, so theoretical exposure is very low.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption signals for soldiers are marginal rather than mainstream. In Sweden and comparable militaries, AI is visibly present in defense-related functions such as intelligence analysis, surveillance, logistics, training simulation, drone/image analysis, and decision-support, but these uses are typically concentrated in specialized units, procurement programs, or pilot projects rather than in the core day-to-day work of ordinary soldiers. For the occupation itself—frontline military personnel whose main task is armed combat and participation in exercises—there is little observable evidence of routine direct use of AI tools like chat assistants or automated drafting systems. Most AI deployment is indirect, embedded in command systems or supporting infrastructure, not broadly adopted by soldiers as a standard workplace tool. This places the occupation in the early/pilot range, with limited observable current adoption.

nameSwedish: Undersköterskor, hemtjänst, hemsjukvård, äldreboende och habilitering
nameEnglish: Assistant nurses in home care, home nursing, care homes and habilitation
description:
Ger individuell vård och omsorg till äldre i eget eller särskilt boende. Utför hälso- och sjukvårdsuppgifter efter instruktion och delegering t.ex. kvalificerad sårvård och kateterspolning. Ger stöd, assistans, omvårdnad och social service till barn, äldre och personer med funktionsnedsättning i deras vardagsliv. Ger stimulans och stöd till individen i aktiviteter och kontakter med omvärlden.

Hit hör inte
Habiliteringsassistent (5330)
Hemvårdsassistent (3411)
descriptionEnglish: Provide individual care and support to older people in their own homes or in residential care. Carry out health care tasks under instruction and delegation, such as advanced wound care and catheter irrigation. Provide support, assistance, care and social services to children, older people and people with disabilities in their daily lives. Provide encouragement and support for activities and contact with the outside world.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be very little competition for jobs for assistant nurses over the coming year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have very good opportunities for work, but the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for assistant nurses will be very good. Opportunities within the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or occupational credentials.
forecast.ingress: The assessment for assistant nurses is a combined assessment for assistant nurses in wards and outpatient care, home care, home nursing, elderly care and habilitation, and child care assistants. There are about 178,300 employed assistant nurses in Sweden, of whom about 91 percent are women and 9 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is mainly hands-on and relationship-based care work in home care, assisted living, and habilitation settings. Core duties include personal assistance, nursing support after instruction/delegation, hygiene, mobility support, social interaction, and practical help in daily life, all of which require physical presence and human judgment in real-world situations. AI can assist with documentation, scheduling, care planning, and possibly some monitoring or decision support, but it cannot perform the central caregiving tasks. In Sweden, statutory responsibilities and collective agreements also limit how far AI can replace or restructure the role. Overall, the occupation has low-to-moderate theoretical AI exposure.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for care assistants in home care, elder care, and habilitation appears marginal to early-stage rather than mainstream. Observable signals in Sweden point to some AI use in adjacent areas such as scheduling, route optimization, documentation support, and welfare-tech pilots, but not widespread frontline use by undersköterskor themselves. In public and municipal care settings, adoption is slowed by procurement, privacy, integration with patient records, and limited device/time capacity in daily care work. There are some employer experiments and digital care platforms, but as of early 2026 the occupation shows only a few visible pilot or support-tool deployments, not broad routine AI use.

nameSwedish: Undersköterskor, vård- och specialavdelning och mottagning
nameEnglish: Assistant nurses in wards, outpatient departments and clinics
description: Kontrollerar patienters kroppstemperatur, puls och blodtryck, tar prover och lägger om sår. Hjälper patienter med deras personliga hygien. Bäddar, delar ut mat och gör annat som ingår i den dagliga vården. Assisterar läkare och sjuksköterskor vid undersökningar och behandlingar. Tar prover, tar fram provsvar och journaler samt förbereder patienter för behandling. Plockar fram instrument inför undersökningar och ingrepp samt rengör och desinficerar dem.
descriptionEnglish: Check patients' temperature, pulse and blood pressure, take samples and dress wounds. Help patients with personal hygiene. Make beds, distribute meals and carry out other tasks involved in daily care. Assist doctors and nurses during examinations and treatment. Take samples, retrieve test results and records, and prepare patients for treatment. Prepare instruments for examinations and procedures, and clean and disinfect them.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be very little competition for jobs for assistant nurses over the coming year. This means that people with the required education or experience will have very good opportunities for work, but the labor market may vary depending on specialization within the occupation.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year horizon, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that the opportunities for work for assistant nurses will be very good. Opportunities within the occupation increase if you have relevant certificates, authorizations, or occupational credentials.
forecast.ingress: The assessment for assistant nurses is a combined assessment for assistant nurses in wards and outpatient care, home care, home nursing, elderly care and habilitation, and child care assistants. There are about 178,300 employed assistant nurses in Sweden, of whom about 91 percent are women and 9 percent are men.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is primarily hands-on and care-oriented, with substantial physical and interpersonal work: measuring vital signs, taking samples, dressing wounds, assisting with hygiene, preparing patients, and maintaining instruments and supplies. AI can support some surrounding cognitive tasks such as documentation, scheduling, checking lab results, or decision support, but it cannot replace the core embodied care, patient contact, or manual procedures. In the Swedish welfare and healthcare context, many duties are also formally tied to regulated staff responsibilities, which further limits direct AI substitution. Overall, the role has low-to-moderate theoretical AI exposure.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for this occupation appears to be in the early adoption range. There are observable signals of AI use in Swedish healthcare workflows, especially for adjacent tasks such as documentation support, scheduling, transcription, decision support, and administrative triage. However, for undersköterskor in wards, specialty units, and outpatient clinics, the core hands-on duties (vital signs, hygiene support, wound care assistance, specimen handling, preparing rooms and instruments) remain largely manual and only lightly augmented by AI. In Sweden, public healthcare organizations are adopting digital tools unevenly, and AI deployment is more visible for physicians, nurses, and administrative functions than for assistant nursing roles. Overall, there are some real deployment signals, but not enough to indicate mainstream AI integration in the day-to-day work of this occupation.
